item.title: 混音和创建新帖子 | Remix and creating new posts
item.description:
很酷，所以我们刚刚将 Postgres 添加到了我们的 Remix 应用程序中。 让我们看看如何通过 Web 界面将新帖子添加到我们的数据库中。

本文的结果将是一个出色的 Web 表单，我们可以通过它向 Postgres 数据库添加新帖子。

创建表单

首先，创建一个名为routes/posts/new.tsx的超级简单的路由。

现在在里面创建表格。

 import { Form } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; const inputClassName = `w-full rounded border border-gray-500 px-2 py-1 text-lg` ; export default function NewPost () { return ( < Form method = ' post ' > < p > < label > Post Title :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' title ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p > < label > Post Slug :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' slug ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p className = ' text-right ' > < button type = ' submit ' className = ' rounded bg-blue-500 py-2 px-4 text-white hover:bg-blue-600 focus:bg-blue-400 disabled:bg-blue-300 ' > Create Post < /button > < /p > < p > < label > Content :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' content ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < /Form > ); }

注意<Form>标签是一个混音模块吗？

让我们运行应用程序来看看它的外观。

很好，表格在那里！

处理数据

使用 Remix 表单最酷的部分是它会自动附带一个我们可以挂钩的动作。

它看起来像这样：

 export const action = async ({ request }) => { // Do a action };

在我们的例子中，这个动作是创建帖子，我们可以利用我们已经创建的post.server.ts文件。

 export const action = async ({ request }) => { const formData = await request . formData (); const title = formData . get ( ' title ' ); const slug = formData . get ( ' slug ' ); const content = formData . get ( ' content ' ); await createPost ({ title , slug , content }); return redirect ( ' /posts ' ); };

在这里，我们从表单中获取所有特定字段，并通过设置所有道具来调用createPost方法。

函数本身可能如下所示：

 export async function createPost ( post ) { return prisma . post . create ({ data : post }); }

是的，这就是你所需要的！

重新运行您的应用程序，填写表格并见证奇迹发生。

注意：它不会捕获任何错误； 我会把它留给你补充。

你可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Cool, so we just added Postgres to our Remix app. Let's see how we can add new posts to our database via the web interface.

The result of this article will be an excellent web form through which we can add a new post to our Postgres database.

Creating the form

First, create a super simple route called routes/posts/new.tsx.

Inside create the form for now.

import { Form } from '@remix-run/react'; const inputClassName = `w-full rounded border border-gray-500 px-2 py-1 text-lg`; export default function NewPost() { return ( <Form method='post'> <p> <label> Post Title:{' '} <input type='text' name='title' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p> <label> Post Slug:{' '} <input type='text' name='slug' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p className='text-right'> <button type='submit' className='rounded bg-blue-500 py-2 px-4 text-white hover:bg-blue-600 focus:bg-blue-400 disabled:bg-blue-300' > Create Post </button>
 </p>
 <p> <label> Content:{' '} <input type='text' name='content' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 </Form>
 );
}

Note how the <Form> tag is a remix module?

Let's run the app to see how it looks.

Nice, the form is there!

Handling the data

And the cool part about using the Remix form is that it automatically comes with an action we can hook.

It would look like this:

export const action = async ({ request }) => { // Do a action
};

In our case, this action is to create the post, for which we can leverage the post.server.ts file we already created.

export const action = async ({ request }) => { const formData = await request.formData(); const title = formData.get('title'); const slug = formData.get('slug'); const content = formData.get('content'); await createPost({ title, slug, content }); return redirect('/posts');
};

Here we get all the specific fields from the form and invoke the createPost method by setting all the props.

The function itself can look like this:

export async function createPost(post) { return prisma.post.create({ data: post });
}

And yes, that will be all you need!

Rerun your app, fill out the form and see the magic happen.

Note: It won't catch any error; I'll leave that to you to add.

You can find the completed code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

item.title: 2022 年面向开发人员的 13 大 Vue Devtools | Top 13 Vue Devtools for Developers in 2022
item.description:
它是一个用于设计任何用户界面的渐进式 JavaScript 框架，在 GitHub 上有 195k 星。

与市场上的其他框架相比，Vue 具有极高的可定制性。 这意味着您不必完全理解所有原则。 它在很短的时间内受到了程序员的广泛关注。

它是从头开始构建的，可以根据您的需要同时用作库和框架。 它由一个以视图层为中心的核心库和一个补充库的生态系统组成。 尽管如此，VueJS 已经成为最流行的 js 框架，因为它具有众多的特性和优点。

与 Angular、Backbone 和 React 等更复杂的前端框架相比，它相对紧凑、轻量且易于使用。 此外，Vue.js 在开发人员中迅速普及。

此外， Vue因其在线应用程序而受到大多数知名品牌的信任。 因此，将它用于您的业务可能是一个很好的策略。 一家定制的 Web 应用程序开发公司配备了必要的技能集，可以为您的业务提供最好的开发世界。

让我们开始使用最适合开发人员的 Vue 开发工具。 这些 VueJS 的开发者工具真的很有用。 因此，请密切关注这些顶级 VueJS 开发工具。

官方 Vue.Js 指南
还包括详尽的学习指南、Vue.js API 文档、官方编码风格指南、代码示例等。

故事书
它是一个 UI 开发和测试环境，支持 Vue、React、React Native、Angular 和 Ember 以及其他 JavaScript 框架。 故事是组件的单个状态的表示（如可视化测试用例）。 一个组件可以有很多故事，而一个Storybook通常有很多故事。

资料来源：故事书

该工具甚至支持隔离环境。 此外，Storybook 将使开发人员能够在主项目之外构建组件。 始终可以选择聘请专门的开发人员来满足您的 Web 开发要求，以便在不浪费宝贵时间和资金的情况下，一开始就做好。

元素
它带有大量可定制的 Vue 组件，您可以使用它们来创建具有专业外观的 Web 应用程序。 另一方面，Element UI 拥有庞大的社区并且正在积极开发，每天都有新的变化被推送。 您还可以使用由 Vue CLI 构建的 Element UI 启动工具包来快速入门。

来源：元素

您可以使用这些组件构建 Web 应用程序，而不会影响您的任何想法。 您还可以使用 Element UI 组件和功能来精确地按照您的设想构建 Web 应用程序。 首先，您可以使用 Element UI 启动工具包，然后升级到由 Vue CLI 创建的完整工具包。 对于 VueJS 开发者来说，这个工具包可能被认为是顶级的 Vue 开发工具之一。

特征：

  • 交互的
  • 可定制的主题
  • 可下载资源以提高效率

设置 VueJS 后，构建应用程序的第一步是放置一个合适的样板，以防止从头开始编写代码。 有多种样板开发替代方案，但 NuxtJS 允许您创建单页应用程序 (SPA)、渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA)、服务器端渲染 (SSR) 和静态站点。

资料来源： NUXT

此外，Nuxt 的模块化架构和 50 多个模块可帮助您完成诸如集成 Google Analytics、获得 PWA 优势和生成站点地图等操作，从而加快了您的开发过程。 验证
卡片、轮播、按钮、图标、工具栏和其他设计元素都包含在 80 多个语义和可重用的 Vue 组件中。 它还包括预构建的项目脚手架，因此您只需一个命令即可开始构建 Vue.js 应用程序。

资料来源： Vuetify

特征：

  • 具有自动摇树功能的更小的捆绑包
  • 庞大的 Discord 社区
  • 为主要版本提供 18 个月的长期支持
  • 具有 SASS/SCSS 和预设的广泛自定义选项
  • 响应式支持低至 320 像素宽度

材料
此外，考虑到最高的行业标准，为用户提供了最好的 VueJS 管理模板之一，它不仅使用快速简单，而且具有高度可扩展性。 凭借最大的易用性和多功能性，您将能够毫不费力地创建您想要的任何应用程序。 你也可以查看使用 VueJS 构建的 Laravel 管理模板。

资料来源： Materio

模糊搜索、深色、半深色和有边框的布局选择、高级卡片和图表只是其中一些有趣且有价值的功能。 它还具有一些出色的功能，例如下面列出的功能。

特征：

  • 纯 Vue js，无 Jquery 依赖
  • 使用 Vue CLI 创建
  • 使用 Vuex、Vue 路由器、Webpack
  • 代码拆分，延迟加载
  • 智威汤逊认证
  • 访问控制 (ACL)
  • 多语言支持
  • 它分为两个部分：

    来源： ViteJs

    具有高于原生 ES 模块的多项功能的开发服务器，例如高速热模块更换 (HMR)。 一个构建命令，它使用 Rollup 捆绑您的代码并将其设置为生成高度优化的静态资产以进行部署。

    特征：

    • 热模块更换
    • 打字稿
    • 静态资产
    • 它允许您快速开发项目结构和样板。 它还允许您创建快速原型来演示新功能。 除了典型的花里胡哨外，Vue CLI 还为 Babel、ESLint、TypeScript、PostCSS、PWA、单元测试和端到端测试等 Web 开发工具提供了更多支持。 该工具还与社区创建的可以扩展的第三方插件兼容。

      来源： Vue Cli

      此外，Vue CLI 不需要弹出。 与其他框架相比，Vue CLI 提供了更高级别的灵活性。 它是最好的 Vue 开发工具之一，因为它充当所有应用程序组件的集中存储，其规则保证状态只能以可预测的方式更改。 由于与 Vue 的官方 devtools 插件集成，还可以使用零配置时间旅行调试和状态快照导出/导入等高级功能。

      资料来源： Vuex

      所有状态管理任务都集中在这个 vuejs 开发工具中，状态只能以可预测的方式进行更改。 此外，允许根据状态、视图和操作进行更改。 此外，状态指定了用户通过视图看到的应用程序的真实性，并且动作导致状态发生变化，从而开始了突变周期。 视图
      另一个值得考虑的优秀 Vue UI 库是 View UI。 它包含大量有用且有吸引力的 Vue 组件。 它还将帮助您创建出色的 Web 应用程序。 它也是一个高质量的 Vue UI 组件库，包含数百个有用且美观的组件。 因此，它很容易上手，您甚至可以直观地开发新项目。

      资料来源： iView

      特征：

      • 数十个有用且美观的组件。
      • 友好的 API。 它适用于所有技能水平的个人。
      • 广泛的文档和演示。
      • 它很漂亮。

      用于性能测试的 Vue Performance DevTool
      性能测试是一种测量和识别所有上述性能瓶颈的方法。 这个插件的目的是检查 Vue 组件的性能。

      资料来源：开发工具

      此外，它使用 Vue 的“window.performance”API 对 Vue 组件的性能进行统计分析。 以下是 Vue Performance DevTools 的几个广泛应用：

      • 移除或卸载任何未使用的组件实例。
      • 检查导致操作停止或操作开始后需要更长时间的原因。
      • 检查加载时间最长的组件。

      本机脚本
      对于丰富的移动编程，该框架目前包括 iOS 和 Android 运行时。 它也可以用于各种不同的用途。

      类星体
      作为 Web 开发人员，这使您能够快速设计各种样式的响应式网站/应用程序。

      来源：类星体

      它可以通过 CSS 和 JS HTML 元素添加到当前项目中。 它还内置了对 SSR（服务器端渲染应用程序）的支持。 因此，它是最强烈推荐的 vue devtools 之一。

      特征：

      • 以性能为中心的框架
      • 通过 Cordova 或 Capacitor 的移动应用程序（Android、iOS）

      因此，如果您要进行 Vue 开发，我们建议您首先列出您的需求，然后选择最适合您项目需求的 Vue Devtools。 如果您正在处理需要专门前端开发人员的项目，我们建议您与专业的自定义 Web 应用程序开发公司合作。 专业的组织将采用最新的 VueJs 技术为您提供最佳结果。

      查看原文： 13 大 Vue 开发工具



原文:

Vue is a versatile technology that can be used to build applications without requiring a lot of resources. It is a progressive JavaScript framework used for designing any user interface, with 195k stars on GitHub.

Vue is extremely customizable when compared to other frameworks on the market. This implies you don’t have to fully comprehend all of the principles. It has received a lot of attention from programmers in a short space of time.

Vue framework is used for creating progressive user interfaces. It is built from the ground up and can function as both a library and a framework, depending on your needs. It is made up of a view layer-focused core library and an ecosystem of supplementary libraries. Evan You is the creator of Vue. Vue is a progressive JavaScript framework that is a faster alternative to Angular. VueJS, unlike Angular and React, is not funded by major behemoths such as Facebook and Google. Despite this, VueJS has become the most popular js framework because to its numerous features and benefits.

It is relatively compact, lightweight, and simple to use when compared to more complicated front-end frameworks such as Angular, Backbone, and React. Furthermore, Vue.js is gaining popularity among developers at a rapid pace.

Gitlab, Alibaba, Adobe, Xiaomi, Euronews, Behance, Nintendo, Grammarly, Codeship, and many others are among the brands that use VueJs.

Furthermore, Vue is trusted by the majority of well-known brands for their online apps. As a result, it could be a good strategy for you to use it for your business. A custom web application development company is equipped with the necessary skill sets to provide the best of the development world for your business.

Let’s get started with the Best Vue Devtools for Developers. These developer tools for VueJS are really useful. So, keep an eye on these top VueJS devtools.

Official Vue.Js Guide
The official Vue.js tutorial will assist you in getting started with the framework. A thorough learning guide, the Vue.js API documentation, the official coding style guide, code samples, and more are also included.

Storybook
Storybook is the top in our list of the greatest vue devtools. It’s a UI development and testing environment which supports Vue, React, React Native, Angular, and Ember, among other JavaScript frameworks. Storybook allows you to develop your own component library in the form of a series of stories. A story is a representation of a single state of a component (like a visual test case). A component can have a lot of stories, and a Storybook generally has a lot of them.

Source: Storybook

The Vue tool will make it simple for developers to create and test beautiful UI components. Even the isolated environment is supported by the tool. Furthermore, Storybook will enable developers to construct components apart from the main project. There is always the option to hire dedicated developers for your web development requirements, to get it right on the first go without wasting precious time and funds.

Element
Element UI is one of the most popular Vue.js 2.0 UI toolkits at the moment. It comes with a plethora of customizable Vue components that you can use to create a professional-looking web app. Element UI, on the other hand, has a large community and is actively developed, with new changes being pushed every day. You may also utilize Element UI’s starting kit, which was built by Vue CLI, to get started quickly.

Source: Element

You can build a web application using these components without compromising any of your ideas. You can also use Element UI components and functionalities to construct the web application precisely how you envisioned it. To start, you can utilize the Element UI starting kit, and then upgrade to the full toolkit created by Vue CLI later. For VueJS developers, this toolkit might be regarded one of the top Vue devtools.

Features:

  • Interactive
  • Customizable themes
  • Downloadable resources to increase efficiency

NuxtJS
NuxtJS is a Vue framework that is simple to use. After you’ve set up VueJS, the first step in constructing your apps is to put up a suitable boilerplate that allows you to prevent writing code from start. There are a variety of boilerplate development alternatives, but NuxtJS allows you to create Single Page Applications (SPAs), Progressive Web Applications (PWAs), Server-Side Rendered (SSRs), and Static Sites.

Source: NUXT

Furthermore, Nuxt speeds up your development process because of its modular architecture and over 50 modules which help you do things like integrate Google Analytics, get PWA advantages, and generate a sitemap. Nuxt’s bundle analyzer also supports VueJS and NodeJS best practices and provides out-of-the-box performance tweaking.

Vuetify
Vuetify is a well-known Vue.js component framework that adheres to the Material Design standards set out by Google. Cards, carousels, buttons, icons, toolbars, and other design elements are among the 80+ semantic and reusable Vue components included. Vuetify works with all current web browsers. It also includes pre-built project scaffolding so you can get started constructing your Vue.js app with only one command.

Source: Vuetify

Features:

  • WCAG Accessibility (a11y) / WCAG / Section 508 / RTL support
  • 30+ supported languages
  • Smaller bundle sizes with automatic tree-shaking
  • A massive Discord community
  • 18 months of Long-term support for Major releases
  • Extensive customization options with SASS/SCSS and Presets
  • Responsive support down to 320px width

Materio
Materio Vuetify Vuejs Admin Dashboard Template — is the latest Vuetify-based Admin Dashboard Template that is both developer-friendly and extremely customizable. Furthermore, the highest industry standards are taken into account to deliver users one of the best VueJS admin templates that are not only quick and simple to use, but also highly scalable. With maximum ease and versatility, you’ll be able to create any application you desire with no effort. You may also check out at Laravel Admin Templates built with VueJS.

Source: Materio

Materio also has a number of useful features aimed at assisting you in creating high-quality single-page apps precisely as you want them. Fuzzy search, Dark, Semi-Dark, and Bordered layout choices, Advanced cards, and Charts are just a few of the interesting and valuable features. It also has several fantastic features, such as the ones listed below.

Features:

  • Pure Vue js, No Jquery Dependency
  • Created with Vue CLI
  • Utilizes Vuex, Vue Router, Webpack
  • Code Splitting, Lazy loading
  • JWT Authentication
  • Access Control (ACL)
  • i18n Page
  • Multi-lingual Support
  • 2 chart libraries
  • 3 Dashboard
  • SASS Powered and many more.

ViteJS
ViteJS is a build tool for contemporary web projects that attempts to make development easier and faster. Vite is likewise uncompromising and ships with good defaults out of the box, but it’s also very extendable because because of its Plugin API and JavaScript API, both of which have complete type support. It is divided into two sections:

Source: ViteJs

A development server with several features above native ES modules, such as highly fast Hot Module Replacement (HMR). A build command which bundles your code using Rollup and sets it up to produce highly optimized static assets for deployment.

Features:

  • NPM Dependency Resolving and Pre-Bundling
  • Hot Module Replacement
  • TypeScript
  • Vue
  • JSX
  • CSS
  • Static Assets
  • JSON

Vue-CLI
Vue-CLI is a set of tools for working with VueJS. It allows you to quickly develop project structures and boilerplates. It also allows you to create rapid prototypes to demonstrate new features. In addition to the typical bells and whistles, the Vue CLI has a lot more support for web development tools like Babel, ESLint, TypeScript, PostCSS, PWA, Unit Testing, and End-to-end Testing. The tool is also compatible with community-created third-party plugins that may be extended.

Source: Vue Cli

Furthermore, ejecting is not required with Vue CLI. In comparison to other frameworks, the Vue CLI allows for a greater level of flexibility. Vue CLI’s Graphical User Interface (GUI) is one of the most useful features, allowing you to easily create new projects and manage them.

Vuex
Vuex is a Vue.js application state management paradigm and library. It’s one of the finest Vue devtools since it acts as a centralized store for all of an application’s components, with rules guaranteeing that the state may only be changed in a predictable way. Advanced capabilities like zero-config time-travel debugging and state snapshot export/import are also available thanks to integration with Vue’s official devtools plugin.

Source: Vuex

All state management tasks are centralized in this vuejs development tool, and states may only be altered in a predictable manner. Furthermore, mutations depending on State, View, and Actions are permitted. Furthermore, the state specifies the application’s truth, which the user sees through the view, and actions cause the state to change, beginning the mutation cycle. Vuex additionally includes functions such as state snapshot export/import and zero-config time-travel debugging.

IView
Another excellent Vue UI library to consider is View UI. It includes a plethora of useful and attractive Vue components. It will also assist you in creating a fantastic web application. It’s also a high-quality Vue UI component library that includes hundreds of useful and beautiful components. As a result, it’s simple to get started with, and you can even develop new projects visually.

Source: iView

Features:

  • Dozens of useful and beautiful components.
  • Friendly API. It is appropriate for individuals of all skill levels.
  • Extensive documentation and demos.
  • It is quite beautiful.

Vue Performance DevTool for Performance Testing
Performance testing is a method of measuring and identifying all of the aforementioned performance bottlenecks. Vue Performance DevTools is a browser plugin that improves the performance of your website. The purpose of this plugin is to examine the performance of Vue components.

Source: Devtools

Furthermore, it does a statistical analysis of a Vue component’s performance using Vue’s ‘window.performance’ API. The following are a couple of wide range of applications of Vue Performance DevTools:

  • Remove or unmount any component instances that aren’t in use.
  • Examine the reasons that cause an operation to stop or take longer once it has begun.
  • Examine the component that takes the longest to load.

Nativescript
NativeScript allows you to use native APIs straight from JavaScript. For rich mobile programming, the framework presently includes iOS and Android runtimes. It can also be used for a variety of different uses.

Quasar
Quasar is a high-performance Material Design component package with extensions for SPAs, SSRs, PWAs, Mobile (Cordova & Capacitor), Desktop (Electron), and Browsers. As a web developer, this enables you to swiftly design responsive websites/apps in a variety of styles.

Source: Quasar

A UMD (Unified Module Definition) version is also available from Quasar. It can be added to the current project via CSS and JS HTML elements. It also has built-in support for SSR (Server-side Rendered App). As a result, it is one of the most highly recommended vue devtools.

Features:

  • Performance-focused framework
  • PWAs (Progressive Web App)
  • BEX (Browser Extension)
  • Mobile Apps (Android, iOS) through Cordova or Capacitor

So, if you’re going to be doing Vue development, we recommend making a list of your requirements first, and then picking the best Vue Devtools that fit your project’s demands. We recommend working with a professional custom web application development company if you’re working on a project that requires dedicated frontend developers. A professional organization will employ the most up-to-date VueJs technology to provide you with the best results.

item.title: 使用 Web Workers 提高 Web 性能 | Improve Web Performance With Web Workers
item.description:
如果您希望优化或提高 Web 应用程序的性能，那么您绝对应该使用 Web Worker，但仍然有很多人没有意识到它并且不知道如何使用 Web 应用程序进行设置。 在这篇文章中，我将尽我所能帮助您使用网络工作者。

什么是网络工作者？

简单来说，web worker 只不过是一个 javascript 文件，它由浏览器在后台执行，与您的应用程序 javascript 文件分开。

由于 Web Worker 与主 javascript 文件分开运行，因此您可以将主 javascript 文件中存在的计算密集型函数放入 Web Worker 文件中，以便主线程可以顺利运行，而不会在用户界面中出现打嗝。

如何设置网络工作者

在项目目录中创建一个名为worker.js的 javascript 文件，如下所示。

 // worker.js console.log("Hello from web worker!") this.onmessage = e => { console.log('worker.js: Message received from main script', e.data) this.postMessage('Hello main') }

在main.js文件中，您可以通过将worker.js路径地址传递给 Worker 类来实例化 worker 对象，然后类似地您可以使用onmessage和postMessage函数与工作线程进行通信。

 // main.js const worker = new Worker('worker.js') worker.postMessage('Hello Worker') worker.onmessage = e => { console.log('main.js: Message received from worker:', e.data) } // if you want to "uninstall" the web worker then use: // worker.terminate()

您可以使用worker.terminate()函数来终止您的网络工作者执行。

通过这个简单的步骤，您现在可以将计算密集型功能放入 Web Worker 中，并在执行后与主应用程序线程进行通信。

感谢您阅读本文。 如果您对 Web 开发和 JavaScript 内容感兴趣，请在Twitter 上关注我。



原文:

If your are looking to optimise or improve performance of your web application then you should definitely use web workers but still many people are unaware of it and don't know how to setup with web application. In this post i'll do my best to help you with web workers.

What is Web Worker?

In simple terms web worker is nothing but a javascript file that is executed by the browser in background separate from your application javascript file.

How Web Worker can increase your application performance?

As Web worker runs separate from the main javascript file you can put your computational intensive functions present in main javascript file into web worker file so main thread can run smoothly with no hiccups in user interface.

How to setup web worker

Create a javascript file named worker.js as shown below in your project directory.


// worker.js
console.log("Hello from web worker!")
this.onmessage = e => {
  console.log('worker.js: Message received from main script', e.data)
  this.postMessage('Hello main')
}

onmessage : It is used to listen to any messages sent by the main application thread.

postMessage : It is used send message to main application thread.

In main.js file you can instantiate worker object by passing worker.js path address to Worker class and then similarly you can use onmessage and postMessage function to communicate with the worker thread.

// main.js
const worker = new Worker('worker.js')
worker.postMessage('Hello Worker')
worker.onmessage = e => {
  console.log('main.js: Message received from worker:', e.data)
}
// if you want to "uninstall" the web worker then use:
// worker.terminate()

You can use worker.terminate() function to terminate your web worker execution.

With this simple steps now you can put your compute intensive functions in web worker and communicate with main application thread once it's executed.

Thank you for reading this article. Follow me on Twitter if you are interested in web development and javascript content.

item.title: 在 Java 中插入 PDF 中的图像 | Insert Images in PDF in Java
item.description:
图片是PDF文档的重要组成部分，在PDF页面的适当位置插入图片可以使文档更具吸引力。 本文将分享如何使用 Free Spire.PDF for Java 将图像插入到现有的 PDF 文档中。

导入Jar依赖（2种方法）

方法一：下载免费库并解压。 然后将 Spire.Pdf.jar 文件作为依赖项添加到您的 Java 应用程序中。
方法二：在pom.xml文件中添加如下配置，直接将jar依赖添加到maven项目中。

 <repository> <id>com.e-iceblue</id> <name>e-iceblue</name> <url>https://repo.e-iceblue.com/nexus/content/groups/public/</url> </repository> </repositories> <dependencies> <dependency> <groupId>e-iceblue</groupId> <artifactId>spire.pdf.free</artifactId> <version>5.1.0</version> </dependency> </dependencies>

示例代码

以下代码显示了如何将图像插入到现有的 PDF 文档中，为了避免覆盖文本内容，我将透明度设置为插入的图像。

 import com.spire.pdf.* ; import com.spire.pdf.graphics.PdfImage ; public class InsertImage { public static void main ( String [] args ) { //Create PDF document PdfDocument pdf = new PdfDocument (); //Load the PDF document from disk pdf . loadFromFile ( "D:\\Files\\input.pdf" ); //Get a specified page PdfPageBase page = pdf . getPages (). get ( 0 ); //Load an image PdfImage image = PdfImage . fromFile ( "C:\\Users\\Administrator\\Desktop\\Olympics.jpg" ); //Set the width and height of image float width = image . getWidth () * 0.75f ; float height = image . getHeight () * 0.75f ; //Define a position to draw image double x = ( page . getCanvas (). getClientSize (). getWidth ()- width ) / 2 ; float y = 300 f ; //Set image transparency page . getCanvas (). setTransparency ( 0.5f ); //Draw image on page canvas page . getCanvas (). drawImage ( image , x , y , width , height ); //Save the document pdf . saveToFile ( "insertImage.pdf" , FileFormat . PDF ); pdf . close (); } }



原文:

Image is an important part of PDF documents, and inserting images at appropriate positions on a PDF page can make the document more attractive. This article will share how to insert an image into an exixting PDF document using Free Spire.PDF for Java.

Import Jar Dependency (2 Methods)

Method 1: Download the free library and unzip it. Then add the Spire.Pdf.jar file to your Java application as dependency.
Method 2: Directly add the jar dependency to maven project by adding the following configurations to the pom.xml file.

    <repository>
        <id>com.e-iceblue</id>
        <name>e-iceblue</name>
        <url>https://repo.e-iceblue.com/nexus/content/groups/public/</url>
    </repository>
</repositories>
<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>e-iceblue</groupId>
        <artifactId>spire.pdf.free</artifactId>
        <version>5.1.0</version>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>

Sample Code

Free Spire.PDF for Java provides the page.getCanvas().drawImage() method to add images to the PDFs when creating as well as insert images into existing PDFs. The following code shows how to insert an image into an exixting PDF document, and to avoid covering the text content, I set the transparency to the inserted image.

import com.spire.pdf.*;
import com.spire.pdf.graphics.PdfImage; public class InsertImage { public static void main(String[] args) { //Create PDF document PdfDocument pdf = new PdfDocument(); //Load the PDF document from disk pdf.loadFromFile("D:\\Files\\input.pdf"); //Get a specified page PdfPageBase page = pdf.getPages().get(0); //Load an image PdfImage image = PdfImage.fromFile("C:\\Users\\Administrator\\Desktop\\Olympics.jpg"); //Set the width and height of image float width = image.getWidth() * 0.75f; float height = image.getHeight() * 0.75f; //Define a position to draw image double x = (page.getCanvas().getClientSize().getWidth()-width) /2; float y = 300f; //Set image transparency page.getCanvas().setTransparency(0.5f); //Draw image on page canvas page.getCanvas().drawImage(image, x, y, width, height); //Save the document pdf.saveToFile("insertImage.pdf", FileFormat.PDF); pdf.close(); }
}

item.title: 在 React Native 中实现 Flexbox 布局 | Implement Flexbox Layout in React Native
item.description:
如果您以前从事过前端开发或移动应用程序开发，那么您肯定有使用 flexbox 设计和构建应用程序的经验。 如果您以前没有工作过并且没有使用 flexbox 的经验，请不要担心。 只需阅读我们的教程并了解如何在 React Native 应用程序中实现 Flexbox 布局。

让我们首先了解我们的教程将为您提供什么。

  • 关于 flexbox 布局的一般讨论
  • 不同的 React Native 布局
  • 在 React Native 应用中实现 flexbox 布局

什么是弹性盒

弹性盒子模块——它被称为弹性盒子。 它是一种设计与应用程序的一维布局一起工作的模型。

开始行和结束行

  • 弹性容器

    • 在我们的文章中，我们讨论了不同类型的 Flexbox 和 Flexbox 布局属性。 此外，我们还分享了一些 React Native 中的 Flexbox 布局示例。 如果您想了解更多关于React Native flexbox 布局的信息，请立即跳转到我们的完整文章。



    原文:

    If you have previously worked with fronted development or mobile app development, you surely have experience in designing and building applications using flexbox. Don’t worry, if you do not work previously and have no experience working with flexbox. Just read our tutorial and get the knowledge about how to implement Flexbox Layout in React Native Application.

    Let’s first understand what our tutorial will offer you.

    • General discussion about flexbox layout
    • Different React Native layouts
    • Flexbox layout properties
    • Implementing flexbox layout in React Native app

    What is Flexbox

    Flexible Box Module - it is knowns as flexbox. It is a kind of designing a model that works with the one-dimensional layout of an application.

    Fundamental concepts of Flexbox Layout

    • Two Axes of Flexbox
    • Start and End Lines
    • Flex Container

    Further in our article, we have discussed the different types of Flexbox and Flexbox layout properties. Also, we share some examples of Flexbox layout in React Native. If you want to know more about React Native flexbox layout, jump to our full article now.

item.title: 使用基本的 awk、sed 和 grep 操作真实数据 | Using basic awk, sed and grep for manipulate real data
item.description:
我们都知道命令行实用程序的强大功能，但很少使用此工具集。 在这篇简短的说明中，我们将操作一个 csv 文件来提取一些关于它的信息。

为了让读者完全理解本教程的内容，需要最少的命令行知识。 包含我们将使用的所有数据的文件名是all_month.csv 。 您可以将文件放在您想要的任何目录中。

我们将分析的数据是一个逗号分隔值 (CSV) 简单文本文件，取自美国地质调查局 (USGS)，它代表了过去 30 天内全国和其他地区的传感器捕获的地震活动本说明所写的内容（2022 年 3 月至 4 月）。 您可以在此处下载具有此时间段大小或更短的数据集。

第一次接触是这样的

让我们看看这个文件有多少条目。 我们可以使用wc命令来做到这一点。 我们传递了一个附加选项-l指示行数。

 wc -l all_month.csv 9271 all_month.csv

该文件包含 9271 行。
现在，让我们看看这些字段，使用head -N命令。 它打印出前N行。 默认情况下，它是 10。

 head -1 all_month.csv time,latitude,longitude,depth,mag,magType,nst,gap,dmin,rms,net,id,updated,place,type,horizontalError,depthError,magError,magNst,status,locationSource,magSource

在本教程中，我们将只使用其中的四个： time 、 depth 、 mag和place 。 输出格式为：2022-04-02 12:23:45, 10.1, 1.1, "4km SE of Honolulu - Hawaii"。
那么，如何拆分它们呢？ 为此，我们有cut 。 语法是cut -d<delim> -f<fields> <file> ，其中-d告诉分隔符，而-f以数字通知字段。 首先，让我们剪掉我们关心的前三列。

 cut -d, -f1,4,5 all_month.csv

在这里，分隔符是逗号，字段是它们出现的顺序。 例如，如果我们想要从 2 到 5 的所有字段，我们可以使用-f2-5 （在主要示例中， -f1,4-5将返回相同的结果）。

好的，一切看起来都不错，让我们添加place字段。 将其添加到命令中： cut -d, -f1,4,5,14 all_month.csv ：

 cut -d, -f1,4,5,14 all_month.csv … 2022-03-19T15:17:42.550Z,1.74,1.2,"8km NW of The Geysers 2022-03-19T15:13:52.070Z,3.59,0.6,"24 km SSW of Kanosh 2022-03-19T15:12:31.400Z,1.86,0.36,"7km WNW of Cobb 2022-03-19T15:10:07.880Z,26.4,0.48,"29 km N of Ivanof Bay

嗯……和预想的有点不一样，比如place字段的最后一部分被省略了。 但为什么？ 如您所见，我们告诉cut命令在遇到逗号时拆分数据文件的每个“单元格”。 这就是这个place被撕毁的原因。 它在文本内有一个逗号。

一个解决方案是

  1. 将place字段的文本提取出来，放到一个临时文件中；
  2. 提取其他字段并将它们放在一个临时文件中；
  3. 逐行加入这两个文件。

值得知道的是，我们可以通过以下三种方式重定向命令的输出：

  • 使用>操作符输出到文件；
  • 错误输出到文件，使用2>操作符；
  • 输出到另一个命令的输入，使用| 回到我们的例子，我们怎样才能只提取包含双引号的那部分行呢？ 答案是grep 。 它使用正则表达式 ( regex ) 的强大功能。 我们不会在本文中解释它们。

    查找所有place条目的命令是

    grep -o '"[^"]\+"' all_month.csv … "8km NW of The Geysers, CA" "24 km SSW of Kanosh, Utah" "7km WNW of Cobb, CA" "29 km N of Ivanof Bay, Alaska"

    我们可以使用sed命令删除逗号。 它也适用于正则表达式，但在这种情况下它会更简单。

    其中s/表示替换， ,是我们要替换的序列， -是要替换的序列。 我们可以将这两个命令与管道运算符连接起来，这会将grep命令的输出重定向到sed

     grep -o '"[^"]\+"' all_month.csv | sed "s/,/ -/g" … "8km NW of The Geysers - CA" "24 km SSW of Kanosh - Utah" "7km WNW of Cobb - CA" "29 km N of Ivanof Bay - Alaska"

    我们现在可以将此文本重定向到一个临时文件（我将调用places_without_comma

     grep -o '"[^"]\+"' | sed "s/,/ -/g" > places_without_comma

    现在，如果您ls进入您的目录，您将看到一个包含上述输出的新文件。

    对于第二步，这将是相似的，但有cut

     tail +2 all_month.csv | cut -d, -f1,4,5 > other_fields

    这将摆脱标题。

    要连接这两个文件，我们将使用一个名为paste的命令。

     paste -d, other_fields places_without_comma > all_month_formatted.csv

    正如您所怀疑的， -d,指定了分隔符。

    使用 awk 提取信息

    如果我们想要涉及地震深度和震级的更大值怎么办？ 我们可以使用awk来做到这一点。 一个基本的例子是awk -F, '{print $1}' all_month_formatted.csv ，它将打印我们文件的第一列，就像cut -d, -f1一样。 默认情况下，它的分隔符是空格，这里使用的许多其他工具也是如此。

    向前跳，awk 中用于过滤depth和mag的最大值和最小值的脚本可以是：

    depth_mag_script.awk

     #!/usr/bin/env -S awk -f BEGIN {FS="," ; maxDepth=0 ; maxMag=0} { if (NR==1) { # When it reads the first entry minDepth=$2; minMag=$3; } if ($2 > maxDepth) maxDepth = $2; else if ($2 < minDepth) minDepth = $2; if ($3 > maxMag) maxMag = $3; else if($3 < minMag) minMag = $3; } END { print "The deepest earthquake was " maxDepth " and the shallowest was " minDepth; print "The biggest earthquake was " maxMag " and the lowest was " minMag }

    其中FS是“文件分隔符”， NR是“记录数”，$2 和 $3 是字段的编号。 负幅值是由于测量尺度的对数特性造成的。

    最后，我们可以将日期格式化为yyyy-mm-dd hh:MM:ss并使用sed在逗号后添加空格。 这一次，通过-i选项直接在文件中进行操作。

     sed -i "s/T/ / ; s/\.[0-9]*\S// ; s/,/, /g" all_month_formatted.csv … 2022-03-19 15:17:42, 1.74, 1.2, "8km NW of The Geysers - CA" 2022-03-19 15:13:52, 3.59, 0.6, "24 km SSW of Kanosh - Utah" 2022-03-19 15:12:31, 1.86, 0.36, "7km WNW of Cobb - CA" 2022-03-19 15:10:07, 26.4, 0.48, "29 km N of Ivanof Bay - Alaska"

    下一步

    有了它，您可以轻松完成所有这些工作。 这是一个 python 脚本的示例，它给出了相同的结果，但以一种更令人愉快的方式。

     #!/usr/bin/env python3 import pandas as pd df = pd.read_csv('all_month.csv', usecols=[0, 3, 4, 13, 14]) print(df.head(10)) max_depth = df['depth'].nlargest(1) print(max_depth) print('-'*10) min_depth = df['depth'].nsmallest(1) print(min_depth) print('='*20) max_mag = df['mag'].nlargest(1) print(max_mag) print('-'*10) min_mag = df['mag'].nsmallest(1) print(min_mag)

    有了这个以及更多，如果您不知道或不知道 Pandas 库，值得一试。

    最后...

    作为我在这里的第一篇文章，它肯定包含错误并且可以改进。 随时欢迎反馈！



原文:

We all know about the power of the command line utilities but rarely this toolset is put to work. In this short note we are going to manipulate a csv file to extract some informations about it.

In order for the reader to fully understand the content of this tutorial, minimal knowledge of the command line is required. The file's name with all the data we will use is all_month.csv. You can put the file in wherever directory you want.

The data we will analyze is a comma separated values (CSV) simple text file and was taken from the United States Geological Survey (USGS), and it represents the seismic activities captured by sensors all over the country and other territories in the last 30 days which this note was written (march to april, 2022). You can download the dataset with this time period size or shorter ones here.

The first contact looks like this

Let's see how many entries this file has. We can do this using the wc command. We pass a additional option -l indicanting the number of lines.

wc -l all_month.csv

9271 all_month.csv

The file contains 9271 lines.
Now, let's take a look at the fields, using the head -N command. It prints out the first N lines. By default, it's 10.

head -1 all_month.csv
time,latitude,longitude,depth,mag,magType,nst,gap,dmin,rms,net,id,updated,place,type,horizontalError,depthError,magError,magNst,status,locationSource,magSource

For this tutorial, we are going to use just four of them: time, depth, mag and place. The output will be of the form: 2022-04-02 12:23:45, 10.1, 1.1, "4km SE of Honolulu - Hawaii".
So, how to split them? For this, we have the cut. the syntax is cut -d<delim> -f<fields> <file>, where the -d tells the delimiter, and -f inform the fields in numbers. First, lets cut the first three columns we care about.

cut -d, -f1,4,5 all_month.csv

Here, the delimiter is the comma, and the fields are the order that they appear. For example, if we wanted all fields from 2 to 5 we could use -f2-5 (In the main example, -f1,4-5 would return the same result).

Ok, everythins is looking good, let's add the place field. Adding it to the command: cut -d, -f1,4,5,14 all_month.csv:

cut -d, -f1,4,5,14 all_month.csv
…
2022-03-19T15:17:42.550Z,1.74,1.2,"8km NW of The Geysers
2022-03-19T15:13:52.070Z,3.59,0.6,"24 km SSW of Kanosh
2022-03-19T15:12:31.400Z,1.86,0.36,"7km WNW of Cobb
2022-03-19T15:10:07.880Z,26.4,0.48,"29 km N of Ivanof Bay

Well... It is a little different than expected, for instance, the final part of the place field was left out. But why? As you can see, we told the cut command to split every "cell" of our data file when it encounters a comma. This is why the place was ripped. It has a comma inside the text.

A solution for this is

  1. Extract the text of the place field and put it in a temporary file;
  2. Extract the other fields and also put them in a temporary file;
  3. Join the two files line by line.

It is worth to know that we can redirect the output of a command mostly in three ways:

  • The output to a file, using the > operator;
  • The error output to a file, using the 2> operator;
  • The output to the input of another command, using the | (pipe) operator.

Back to our case, how can we extract only the part of the line that contains the double quotes? The answer is grep. The grep command searches for a pattern of characters in the given object. It uses the power of regular expressions (regex). We won't explain them in this article.

The command to find all the place entries is

grep -o '"[^"]\+"' all_month.csv
…
"8km NW of The Geysers, CA"
"24 km SSW of Kanosh, Utah"
"7km WNW of Cobb, CA"
"29 km N of Ivanof Bay, Alaska"

We can remove the commas with the sed command. It also works with regex, but in this case it will be more simple.

Where s/ means substitute, the , is the sequence we want to be substituted and - is the sequence to replace. The /g tells to apply to all sequences in the whole line.

We can join these two commands with the pipe operator, which will redirect the output of the grep command to sed

grep -o '"[^"]\+"' all_month.csv | sed "s/,/ -/g"
…
"8km NW of The Geysers - CA"
"24 km SSW of Kanosh - Utah"
"7km WNW of Cobb - CA"
"29 km N of Ivanof Bay - Alaska"

We can now redirect this text to a temporary file (I will call places_without_comma

grep -o '"[^"]\+"' | sed "s/,/ -/g" > places_without_comma

Now, if you ls into your directory you will see a new file that contains the output above.

For the second step it will be similar, but with cut

 tail +2 all_month.csv | cut -d, -f1,4,5 > other_fields

The tail command is the opposite of head, it print the last N lines. The +2 option will print out all the lines starting from the second. This will get rid of the header.

To join these two files, we will use a command called paste.

paste -d, other_fields places_without_comma > all_month_formatted.csv

As you can suspect, the -d, specifies the delimiter.

The all_month_formatted.csv file looks like this

2022-03-19T15:17:42.550Z,1.74,1.2,"8km NW of The Geysers - CA"
2022-03-19T15:13:52.070Z,3.59,0.6,"24 km SSW of Kanosh - Utah"
2022-03-19T15:12:31.400Z,1.86,0.36,"7km WNW of Cobb - CA"
2022-03-19T15:10:07.880Z,26.4,0.48,"29 km N of Ivanof Bay - Alaska"

Extracting information with awk

What if we want the greater values involving the depth and magnitude of the earthquakes? We can do this using awk. Awk is by itself a fully capable programming language, optimized to dealing with text. A basic example is awk -F, '{print $1}' all_month_formatted.csv where it will print the first column of our file, acting like cut -d, -f1. By default it's delimiter is space, the same is valid for many of the other tools used here.

Jumping ahead, a script in awk for filter the greatest and smallest values of depth and mag can be:

depth_mag_script.awk

#!/usr/bin/env -S awk -f

BEGIN {FS="," ; maxDepth=0 ; maxMag=0}
{
    if (NR==1) {   # When it reads the first entry
        minDepth=$2;
        minMag=$3;
    }

    if ($2 > maxDepth)
        maxDepth = $2;
    else if ($2 < minDepth)
        minDepth = $2;

    if ($3 > maxMag)
        maxMag = $3;
    else if($3 < minMag)
        minMag = $3;
}
END {
    print "The deepest earthquake was " maxDepth " and the shallowest was " minDepth;
    print "The biggest earthquake was " maxMag " and the lowest was " minMag 
}

Where FS is the "file separator", NR is "number of records" and the $2 and $3 are the field's number. The BEGIN and END are self-explanatory.

./depth_mag_script.awk all_month_formatted.csv

The deepest earthquake was 623.78 and the shallowest was -3.5699999332428
The biggest earthquake was 7 and the lowest was -1.49

The negative magnitude is due to the logarithmic properties of the measurement scale.

Finally, we can format the date to yyyy-mm-dd hh:MM:ss and add spaces after the commas with sed. This time, passing the -i option to operate directly in the file.

sed -i "s/T/ / ; s/\.[0-9]*\S// ; s/,/, /g" all_month_formatted.csv
…
2022-03-19 15:17:42, 1.74, 1.2, "8km NW of The Geysers - CA"
2022-03-19 15:13:52, 3.59, 0.6, "24 km SSW of Kanosh - Utah"
2022-03-19 15:12:31, 1.86, 0.36, "7km WNW of Cobb - CA"
2022-03-19 15:10:07, 26.4, 0.48, "29 km N of Ivanof Bay - Alaska"

Next Steps

The pandas library is well known in the python and data science world, and for a reason. With it you could do all of this work easily. This is a example of a python script that gives the same result but in a more pleasant way.

#!/usr/bin/env python3

import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('all_month.csv', usecols=[0, 3, 4, 13, 14])
print(df.head(10))

max_depth = df['depth'].nlargest(1)
print(max_depth)

print('-'*10)

min_depth = df['depth'].nsmallest(1)
print(min_depth)

print('='*20)

max_mag = df['mag'].nlargest(1)
print(max_mag)

print('-'*10)

min_mag = df['mag'].nsmallest(1)
print(min_mag)

With this and much more, it worth giving the Pandas lib a try, if you haven't or didn't know it.

Finally...

As my first post here, it certainly contains errors and could be improved. Feedback is always welcome!
item.author: atdayan

item.title: 你喜欢展示哪些 JavaScript 有用的功能？ | What are some useful functions of JavaScript, which you like to show?
item.description:
好奇开发

发表于通常我很喜欢在这里找帖子，这些帖子提供了一些 JS 的标准功能。 有了这个，我可以逐步扩展我的知识，这些可以多次有用。 至少感觉比学习更多关于框架或任何其他产品更有意义，我可能不会很快使用。

您想与我们分享哪些功能？

让我从第一个贡献者开始。

我喜欢 JSON 对象的函数“parse”和“stringify”，因为 JS 对象与 JSON 的字符串化表示非常相似。 使用“解析”，您可以轻松地从 JSON 字符串创建 JS 对象，使用“字符串化”，您可以反过来执行此操作。 但是对于后者你必须小心：JS 对象更强大，你将无法将任何对象转换为 JSON。 更多信息： Mozilla 文档。 感谢您提前阅读和任何贡献！



原文:

CuriousDev

Posted on Apr 27

Usually I enjoy it a lot to look for posts here, which provide some standard functions of JS. With this I can extend my knowledge step by step and these can be useful many times. At least it feels to make more sense than learning more about a Framework or any other product, which I possibly will not use soon.

What are some funtions, which you would like to share with us?

Let me start as first contributor.

I like the JSON object's functions "parse" and "stringify", because the JS objects are very similar to the stringified representation of JSON. With "parse" you can easily create a JS object from a JSON string and with "stringify" you can do this the other way round. But for the latter you have to be careful: JS objects are more powerful, you will not be able to convert any object to JSON. More here: Mozilla documentation. Thanks for reading and any contribution in advance!
item.author: curiousdev

item.title: 使用 Amazon API Gateway 异步处理大型负载 | Processing large payloads with Amazon API Gateway asynchronously
item.description:
在我之前的文章中，我谈到了同步 AWS Lambda 和 Amazon API Gateway 限制以及如何处理它们。

正如博文中所述，“大负载”问题的最终解决方案是使架构异步。 然后让我们放大Amazon API Gateway服务上下文中的异步通信方面，并基于“待处理工作”的概念构建一个无服务器架构。

问题复习

下面说明了我在上一篇文章中提到的 Amazon API Gateway 和AWS Lambda有效负载大小问题。

无论您多么努力，您都无法将超过 6 MB 的数据同步发送到您的 AWS Lambda 函数。 该限制可能会严重影响您的重要数据处理需求。

幸运的是，有一些方法可以使用异步工作流通过 AWS Lambda 处理更多数据。

起跑线——将数据推送到存储层

假设您要向社区询问该问题的潜在解决方案。 在这种情况下，我敢打赌，最常见的答案是使用Amazon S3作为数据存储层，并利用 预签名 URL将数据推送到 Amazon S3 存储。

虽然非常普遍，但到 Amazon S3 的预签名 URL流可以有细微差别，特别是因为可以通过两种方式创建预签名 URL 。

描述这些会让我觉得这篇文章有点太长了，所以我将把你推迟到我的同事Zac Charles的这篇很棒的文章中，这篇文章比我做的更公正。

我在 Amazon S3 中有数据。 怎么办？

在处理数据之前，我们的系统必须知道客户端是否使用预签名的URL将数据推送到存储层。 据我所知，我们可以在这里进行两种选择（如果还有其他方法，请告诉我，我非常渴望学习！）

我故意省略了AWS CloudTrail到Amazon EventBridge的流程，因为我认为工程师应该更倾向于直接与 AWS EventBridge 集成。

直到最近， Amazon S3 事件通知还是一种了解数据是否进入 Amazon S3 的事实上的方式。

虽然对于大多数用例来说已经足够了，但该功能并非没有问题，我认为最大的问题是对事件过滤和 IaC 实现的误解。

在事件过滤方面要记住的最重要的事情是，您不能在过滤规则中使用通配符进行前缀或后缀匹配。 不过，还有更多的事情需要考虑。 如果您打算使用 S3 事件通知的过滤功能，我强烈建议您仔细阅读 此文档页面。

在IaC 方面，要知道，在创建AWS CloudFormation模板时，您最终可能会遇到循环依赖问题。 但是，我仍然认为您应该意识到这个潜在的问题，即使您使用部署框架。

事件桥事件

该公告在无服务器社区中受到热烈欢迎，因为 EventBridge 集成解决了大多数“本机”S3 事件通知问题。

将Amazon EventBridge用于 S3 事件可为您提供更广泛的集成表面积（您可以将事件转发到更多目标）和更好的过滤功能（Amazon EventBridge 的优势之一）。 不过，这种整合并非没有问题。

对于初学者来说，事件被发送到“默认”总线，对于高事件量，使用 EventBridge 可能成本更高。 要了解有关不同警告的更多信息，请考虑阅读这篇精彩的文章。

咀嚼数据

您在 Amazon S3 存储中拥有数据，并且您可以通过某种方式通知您的系统这一事实。 怎么办？ 您如何处理数据并将结果返回给用户？

鉴于 AWS 的性质，无论好坏，有多种情况可以向前推进。 我们将从“最简单”的架构开始，逐步部署具有共享、高速数据访问的编排器。

使用专用 AWS Lambda 进行处理

在大多数情况下，在 AWS Lambda 中处理内存中的数据就足够了。

唯一的限制是您的想象力和 AWS Lambda 服务超时——最长 15 分钟的函数运行时间。 请注意，在此设置中，您的 AWS Lambda 函数必须花费一些时间来下载对象。

我对故意保存已执行工作的结果含糊其辞，因为它非常依赖于用例。 您可能希望将工作结果保留在 Amazon S3 上或向数据库添加条目。 由你决定。

但是，如果 15 分钟的超时限制是您的眼中钉怎么办？ 如果解决方案的计算层中的过程很复杂并且会从将其拆分为多个块中受益怎么办？ 如果是这种情况，请继续阅读，接下来我们将讨论AWS Step Functions 。

使用 Amazon StepFunctions 处理

如果我们之前查看的 AWS Lambda 中的计算过程很复杂或花费的时间超过了硬超时限制，那么 AWS Step Functions 可能正是您需要的服务。

记下 Step Function 定义中的代码需要从 Amazon S3 存储中下载对象的次数（当然，插图只是一个示例）。 根据工作量的不同，这个数字可能会有所不同，但无论工作流程如何，在一定数量的阈值之后，我不得不一次又一次地下载对象确实感觉“浪费”。

请记住，AWS Step Functions 的最大有效负载大小为 256KB 。 这就是为什么您必须在需要访问该对象时重复下载该对象。

您可以在 AWS Lambda 函数中实施内存缓存，但该技术仅适用于单个 AWS Lambda 并与其容器生命周期相关联。

根据要求和限制，我喜欢在这种情况下使用Amazon EFS与 AWS Lambda 的集成。 接下来让我们研究一下。

共享 AWS Lambda 存储

在开始之前，请了解将 Amazon EFS 与 AWS Lambda 函数结合使用需要Amazon VPC 。 对于某些工作流程，这一事实不会改变任何事情。 对于其他人来说，确实如此。 我强烈主张保持开放的心态（我知道一些无服务器社区的人鄙视 VPC）并根据您的业务需求评估架构。

这种架构可以说是相当复杂的。 您必须考虑一些网络问题。 如果您的对象非常大并且下载需要很多时间，我建议您研究这种架构 - VPC 不会咬人！

假设您渴望一个实际示例，这是我为解析媒体文件而构建的示例架构。 它利用 Amazon EFS 跨 AWS Step Function 工作流程中涉及的所有 AWS Lambda 函数快速访问该视频文件。

回馈给用户

有了计算部分，现在是时候看看我们如何通知用户我们的系统处理了对象并且结果可用。

无论您选择哪种解决方案来通知用户结果，工作的状态都必须保存在某个地方。 工作待定吗？ 完成了吗？ 也许发生了错误？

跟踪工作状态

以下是跟踪已执行工作状态的示例架构图。

数据库将 CDC 事件发送到系统。 我们可以使用这些 CDC 事件来实时通知用户工作进度！ 在我看来，“工作进度”数据的key:value性质使Amazon DynamoDB成为数据库组件的完美选择（没有什么能阻止您使用 RDS，它还支持 CDC 事件，它们本身在这里是可选的）。

通知用户结果

难题的最后一块是确保用户有办法被通知（或检索）请求的状态。

通常，在这种情况下，我们有两个选择要考虑——或者我们实现一个 API，用户将在其中汇集更新，或者我们将状态直接推送给用户。

第一个选项，池化，在架构方面类似于下图。

这种架构足以满足特定规模。 架构必须根据访问 API 的轮询器进行扩展。 积极参与 API 的轮询者越多，节流或其他问题的可能性就越高（请参阅Thundering herd 问题）。

在更高吞吐量的场景中，人们可能希望使用基于WebSockets的推送模型来替换轮询行为。 对于特定于 AWS 的实施，我建议查看Amazon API Gateway WebSocket 支持或AWS IoT Core WebSockets以了解类似广播的用例。

我写了一篇文章，只关注 AWS 上的无服务器 WebSockets。 您可以在此处找到博客文章。

结束语

这就是我们旅程的终点​​。 我们已经研究了如何将大型有效负载放入我们的系统、对其进行处理并响应用户。 实现此架构并非易事，我希望本演练对您有所帮助。

如需更多 AWS 无服务器内容，请考虑在 Twitter 上关注我 - @wm_matuszewski

感谢您的宝贵时间。



原文:

In my previous article, I've talked about Synchronous AWS Lambda & Amazon API Gateway limits and what to do about them.

As stated in the blog post, the ultimate solution for a "big payload" problem is making the architecture asynchronous. Let us then zoom in on the aspect of asynchronous communication in the Amazon API Gateway service context and build a serverless architecture based on the notion of "pending work".

Problem refresher

The following illustrates the Amazon API Gateway & AWS Lambda payload size problem I've touched about in the previous article.

No matter how hard you try, you unable to synchronously send more than 6 MB of data to your AWS Lambda function. The limit can seriously mess with your significant data-processing needs.

Luckily there are ways to process much more data via AWS Lambda using asynchronous workflows.

The starting line - pushing the data to the storage layer

Suppose you were to ask the community about the potential solution to this problem. In that case, I wager that the most common answer would be to use Amazon S3 as the data storage layer and utilize presigned URLs to push the data to Amazon S3 storage.

While very universal, the flow of presigned URL to Amazon S3 can be nuanced, especially since one can create the presigned URL in two ways.

Describing these would make this article a bit too long for my liking, so I'm going to defer you to this great article by my colleague Zac Charles which did the topic much more justice than I could ever do.

I have the data in Amazon S3. Now what?

Before processing the data, our system must know whether the client used the presigned URL to push the data to the storage layer. To my best knowledge, there are two options we can pursue here (please let me know if there are other ways to go about it, I'm very keen to learn!)

I omitted the AWS CloudTrail to Amazon EventBridge flow on purpose as I think engineers should favor the direct integration with AWS EventBridge instead.

S3 Event Notifications

The Amazon S3 event notifications, till recently, was a de facto way of knowing whether data landed into Amazon S3.

While sufficient for most use-cases, the feature is not without its problems, the biggest of which, I would argue, are the misunderstandings around event filtering and IaC implementation.

The most essential thing to have in mind when it comes to event filtering is that you cannot use wildcards for prefix or suffix matching in your filtering rules. There are more things to consider, though. If you plan to use the filtering feature of S3 event notifications, I strongly encourage you to read this documentation page thoroughly.

On the IaC side of things, know that, in creating the AWS CloudFormation template, you might end up with circular dependency problems. Deployment frameworks like AWS CDK will make sure that should never happen. However, I still think you should be aware of this potential problem, even if you use deployment frameworks.

EventBridge events

In late 2021, AWS announced Amazon EventBridge support for S3 Event Notifications. The announcement had a warm welcome in the serverless community as EventBridge integration solves most of the "native" S3 event notifications problems.

Utilizing Amazon EventBridge for S3 events gives you more extensive integration surface area (you can forward the event to more targets) and better filtering capabilities (one of the strong points of Amazon EventBridge). The integration is not without its problems, though.

For starters, the events are sent to the "default" bus, and using EventBridge might be more costly for high event volumes. To learn more about different caveats, consider giving this great article a read.

Munching on the data

You have the data in the Amazon S3 storage, and you have a way to notify your system about that fact. Now what? How could you process the data and yield the result back to the user?

Given the nature of AWS, for better or worse, there are multiple scenarios one can move forward. We will start from the "simplest" architecture and move our way up to deploying an orchestrator with shared, high-speed data access.

Processing with a dedicated AWS Lambda

The AWS Lambda service is often called a "swiss knife" of serverless. In most cases, processing the data in-memory within the AWS Lambda is sufficient.

The only limitations are your imagination and the AWS Lambda service timeout – the 15-minute maximum function runtime. Please note that in this setup, your AWS Lambda function must spend some of that time downloading the object.

I'm vague about saving the results of the performed work on purpose as it is very use-case dependant. You might want to keep the outcome of your work back on Amazon S3 or add an entry to a database. Up to you.

But what if that 15-minute timeout limitation is a thorn at your side? What if the process within the compute layer of the solution is complex and would benefit from splitting it into multiple chunks? If that is the case, keep on reading, we are going to be talking about AWS Step Functions next.

Processing with Amazon StepFunctions

If the compute process within the AWS Lambda we looked at previously is complex or takes more time than the hard timeout limit, the AWS Step Functions might be just the service you need.

Take a note of the number of times the code within your Step Function definition needs to download the object from Amazon S3 storage (the illustration being an example, of course). Depending on the workload, that number may vary, but no matter the workflow, after a certain number threshold, it does feel "wasteful" for me to have to download the object again and again.

Keep in mind that AWS Step Functions maximum payload size is 256KB. That is why you have to download the object repeatedly whenever you need access to the object.

You could implement in-memory caching in your AWS Lambda function, but that technique only applies to a single AWS Lambda and is tied to its container lifecycle.

Depending on the requirements and constraints, I like to use Amazon EFS integration with AWS Lambda in such situations. Amazon EFS allows me to have a storage layer shared by all AWS Lambda functions that partake in the workflow. Let us look into that next.

Shared AWS Lambda storage

Before starting, understand that using Amazon EFS with AWS Lambda functions requires Amazon VPC. For some workflows, this fact does not change anything. For others, it does. I strongly advocate keeping an open mind (I know some people from serverless community despise VPCs) and evaluating the architecture according to your business needs.

This architecture is, arguably, quite complex. You have to consider some networking concerns. If your object is quite large and downloading it takes a lot of time, I would advise you to look into this architecture – VPCs do not bite!

Suppose you yearn for a practical example, here is a sample architecture I've built for parsing media files. It utilizes Amazon EFS for fast access to that video file across all AWS Lambda functions involved in the AWS Step Function workflow.

Yielding back to the user

With the compute part behind us, it is time to see how we might notify the user that our system processed the object and that the results are available.

No matter what kind of solution you choose to notify the user about the result, the state of the work has to be saved somewhere. Is the work pending? Is it finished? Maybe an error occurred?

Keeping track of the work status

The following is a diagram of an example architecture that keeps track of the status of the performed work.

The database sends CDC events to the system. We could use these CDC events to notify the user about the work progress in real-time! (although that might not be necessary, in most scenarios, polling for the results by the client is sufficient).

In my humble opinion, the key:value nature of the "work progress" data makes the Amazon DynamoDB a perfect choice for the database component (nothing is stopping you from using RDS, which also supports CDC events, which themselves are optional here).

Notifying the user about the results

The last piece of the puzzle is making sure the user has a way to be notified (or retrieve) the status of the request.

Usually, in such situations, we have two options to consider – either we implement an API in which the user is going to pool for the updates, or we push the status directly to the user.

The first option, pooling, would look similar to the following diagram in terms of architecture.

This architecture is sufficient up to a particular scale. The architecture must scale according to the pollers hitting the API. The more pollers actively engage with the API, the higher the chance of throttling or other issues (refer to the Thundering herd problem).

In higher throughput scenarios, one might want to replace the polling behavior with a push model based on WebSockets. For AWS-specific implementations, I would recommend looking at Amazon API Gateway WebSocket support or AWS IoT Core WebSockets for broadcast-like use-cases.

I've written an article solely focused on serverless WebSockets on AWS. You can find the blog post here.

Closing words

And that is the end of our journey. We have looked at how to get the large payload into our system, process it, and respond to the user. Implementing this architecture is not a small feat, and I hope you found the walkthrough helpful.

For more AWS serverless content, consider following me on Twitter - @wm_matuszewski

Thank you for your precious time.
item.author: wojciechmatuszewski

item.title: Microsoft Power Platform：未来会怎样 | Microsoft Power Platform: What the Future Holds
item.description:
发表于生活在数字时代，将我们的注意力转移到对Microsoft Power Platform越来越感兴趣是值得的。 此外，它还使组织能够满足其业务需求，而无需单调的劳动力和过多的时间消耗。

随着越来越多的组织了解 Power Platform 及其功能，它拥有业务流程自动化的未来。 在探讨 Power Platform 的未来发展和增强功能之前，让我们快速浏览一下它的组件。

此外，它支持您使用其他 Microsoft 应用程序进行第三方系统配置，以确保更好的工作流程。

用于收集数据、同步文件和通知的预构建模板和触发器。

此外，您可以将这些聊天机器人与您正在使用的服务集成。

从本质上讲，Power Platform 已经证明了它可以减轻开发人员和组织的沉重负担。 开发人员将不再需要进行繁琐的编码，组织也不必担心聚集熟练的程序员。 借助 Power Platform 上的低代码功能​​，组织中任何具有最少技术知识的人都可以解决重大业务问题，从而为轻松的业务工作流程铺平道路。

此外，Power Platform 加快了工作流程，同时节省了组织的大量宝贵时间以提高生产力。 从中获利的每一分钟只会导致更多的想法来增强您的业务。 因此，我们可以无可否认地说，Power Platform 及其即将推出的功能只会让商业世界受益更多。

它创建了一个平台，组织可以在该平台上快速收集客户信息并增强业务。

  • 开发人员构建应用程序的功能之一是内置评论，用户可以直接在 PowerApps、Power Virtual Agents 或 Power Automate 的画布上共享评论。 这种协作功能可确保构建应用程序时的效率。

  • 尽管其计划已经很复杂，但 PowerApps 不断更新许可计划可能会让人难以忍受。 通过为用户提供最新的即用即付计划来解决这个问题，允许用户使用 Azure 订阅进行许可。 因此，如果您拥有 Azure，就不需要任何额外的许可，并且您可以开发应用程序！

  • 更不用说，您可以在 Power Automate 上的 Process Advisor 的帮助下识别工作流中的低效率。 这是将您的业务自动化流程提升到顶峰的一种方法。

  • 任何人都想在工作中标记他们的创造力。 未来几天，越来越多的公民开发人员将在云上获得最大的人工智能技术。 借助 Power Platforms AI Builder，公民开发人员可以为其应用构建自定义 AI 模型，而无需数据科学或机器学习。 听起来很明显，企业级应用程序的未来拥有 Power Platform 的安全未来。

  • 与客户建立可信赖的关系将确保您公司的长期可靠性。 跟踪员工在远程工作中的工作方式和工作内容可能非常具有挑战性。 而只有这种深刻的观察才能保证工作的改进和效率。 幸运的是，微软的 Power BI 原生数据可视化工具可以收集和可视化员工工作信息。 这为您提供了有关他们工作的所需信息，确保当前和未来的生产和盈利业务！

  • 生活在寻求高效但无缝工作和加速业务工作流程结果同时减少所需开发所消耗的时间和成本的日子就在我们面前。 由于其目前的功能已经影响了几个组织，它迟早会产生更令人振奋的影响。

斯蒂芬妮 Repetcki - Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

vishnuchandra007

Living in the digital era, sliding our attention to how much interest is growing for Microsoft Power Platform is worthwhile. This suite of applications released in 2018 essentially makes business process automation effortless and efficient in every possible way. Additionally, it also enables organizations to fulfill their business needs without monotonous labor and excessive time consumption.

With more organizations being knowledgeable about the Power Platform and its features, it holds the future of business process automation. Before looking into the future developments and enhancements of the Power Platform, let's take a quick overview of its components.

**

**

An Overview Of Microsoft Power Platform

**

Microsoft Power Platform is a power-packed platform that consists of four essential products-

Microsoft Power BI is a business analytics service that provides real-time enterprise data, enabling users to develop profound visualizations and personalized reports on the dashboard.

Microsoft PowerApps is a low code/no-code app developing platform that helps build enterprise applications for business needs. Moreover, it supports you with third-party system configurations with other Microsoft applications to ensure better workflow.

Microsoft Power Automate is a workflow automation solution where users can create automated business workflows amongst their preferred services through Robotic Process Automation (RPA). The prebuilt templates and triggers to gather data, sync files, and notify.

Microsoft Power Virtual Agent is a chatbot development platform that can build AI-driven chatbots without needing a programmer or expertise in data science. Additionally, you can integrate these chatbots with the services you are using.

**

Power Platform: Why Does it Ensure the Future of Business Processes?

**

Essentially, Power Platform has already proven its point to release a significant burden on developers and organizations. Developers will no longer have to do the tedious coding, and organizations will not have to worry about gathering skilled programmers. With the low code facilities on the Power Platform, anybody in the organization with a minimal amount of technical knowledge can solve significant business problems and thereby pave the way to an effortless business workflow.

Moreover, Power Platform makes workflows rapid alongside saving a lot of the organization's valuable time for more productiveness. Every minute profited from this will only result in more thought to enhance your business. Hence, we can undeniably say that Power Platform and its upcoming features will only benefit more to the world of business.

Microsoft Power Platform ensures its sustainability by updating it with more innovative features that simplify business process automation. It creates a platform where organizations can rapidly gather customer information and enhance their business.

  • One of the features for developers building apps is the built-in commenting, where the users can share comments directly on the canvases of PowerApps, Power Virtual Agents, or Power Automate. This collaborating feature ensures efficiency while building an application.

  • Another major challenge that Power Platform users had was the struggle with its licensing and plans. Though being already complicated with its plans, PowerApps constantly updating licensing plans may be confusing to put up with. This problem is solved by providing users with the latest Pay-As-You-Go Plan, allowing users to use an Azure Subscription to license. So, you wouldn't need any extra licensing if you have Azure, and you can develop Apps!

  • Not to mention, you can identify the inefficiencies in workflows with the help of Process Advisor on Power Automate. This is one way to enhance your business automation process to its peak.

  • Any human would like to mark their creativity in work. The growing generation of citizen developers will be on clouds in the coming days, getting the maximum of AI technology. With Power Platforms AI Builder, citizen developers can build custom AI models for their apps that don't require data science or machine learning. As obvious as it sounds already, the future of enterprise-level apps holds a secure future with Power Platform.

  • Building a trustable relationship with customers will secure your firm's long-term reliability. Power Platform easily and rapidly enables this with its existing and upcoming solutions, accelerating your business results.

  • It may be pretty challenging to keep track of how and what the employees are working on in remote working. And only this profound observation can ensure improvements and efficiency in work. Luckily, Microsoft's Power BI native data visualization tool can gather and visualize information on your employees' work. This provides you with needed information on their work, securing a productive and profitable business for the present and the future!

  • Living through days that seek efficient but seamless work and accelerating business workflow results while reducing the time and cost consumed for the development required is right ahead of us. Power Platform will be the master of the sea in terms of business process automation. With its present features already impacting several organizations, it is yet to make more exhilarating impacts sooner or later.
