item.title: 如何在 60 秒内使用 WebAssembly 创建一个支持 Go 的 React 应用程序 | How to create a react app with Go support using WebAssembly in under 60 seconds
item.description:
使用 WebAssembly 在浏览器中使用 react 和 Typescript 运行 Go

为什么要创建反应应用程序？

为什么包括围棋？

在我的情况下，我需要运行 Go 进行JSON 模式验证，在其他情况下，您可能想要执行 CPU 密集型任务或使用用 Go 编写的 CLI 工具。

但并非所有浏览器都支持 WebAssembly！

我也是那么想的。 实际上，自 2017 年 11 月以来，所有主流浏览器都支持 WebAssembly 。 所以只要你不需要支持Internet Explorer就没有什么可担心的。

让我们开始吧😎

首先，初始化一个全新的 create-react-app 项目，假设你不是一个绝对的野蛮人，请确保使用 Typescript 模板😇

 $ npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript

接下来，在/src/LoadWasm下创建一个文件夹

$ cd my-app $ mkdir ./src/LoadWasm `

创建一个文件来扩展 Window 类型声明，我们很快就会使用它。
将用于加载 WebAssembly 代码的文件复制到浏览器中，它在全局窗口对象上添加 Go 属性，并将充当 Javascript 和 WebAssembly 之间的桥梁。

该文件类似于Go 官方存储库中的文件，稍作调整，我们将在下一节中使用它。

 $ curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/royhadad/cra-template-typescript-golang/main/template/src/LoadWasm/wasm_exec.js > ./src/LoadWasm/wasm_exec.js `

接下来，我们将创建一个包装器组件，它将包装我们的整个应用程序并等待 WebAssembly 被加载。 这可以出于性能目的进行优化，但为了简单起见，现在已经足够了。
最后，我们将使用 LoadWasm 组件包装整个应用程序，这将确保在加载 WebAssembly 之前不会加载其他组件
但是等等，Go 代码在哪里？

首先初始化一个 Go 模块

$ mkdir ./wasm $ cd ./wasm $ go mod init wasm $ go mod tidy $ touch ./main.go $ cd ..

现在我们将使用syscall/js包来访问 javascript 全局范围，并在其上设置一个函数。 其次，我们将实现一个小技巧来防止 Go 代码终止：打开一个通道并等待它完成，而无需使用它😈

这将使我们能够不断地与 Go 代码进行通信，而无需每次都重新实例化它。

注意：您可能需要配置您的 IDE 以支持 WebAssembly，请参阅VS Code 指南、 Intellij 指南

现在我们可以在应用程序的某个地方添加这个按钮，也许在App.tsx

 < button onClick = { () => { alert ( window . myGolangFunction ( 2 , 3 )); } } > Click here to invoke WebAssembly! </ button >

把它们放在一起

最后，我们将更改package.json脚本以支持 WebAssembly 的构建和热重载。

建造：

 "build" : "npm run build:wasm && npm run build:ts" ,
"build:ts" : "react-scripts build" ,
"build:wasm" : "cd wasm && GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -o ../public/main.wasm && cd .. && echo \" compiled wasm successfully! \" "

热重载

我们需要一些依赖项

$ npm install watch concurrently --save-dev

并在启动脚本中使用它们

"start" : "concurrently \" npm run watch:ts \" \" npm run watch:wasm \" " ,
"watch:ts" : "react-scripts start" ,
"watch:wasm" : "watch \" npm run build:wasm \" ./wasm" ,

最后，我们将运行npm start并访问localhost:3000上的应用程序
可以在此 GitHub 存储库中找到完整示例

你不是说需要60秒吗？ 说谎者！

好吧，也许这确实需要一段时间，但不要再害怕了！ 我有办法治愈你的懒惰！
我已经为 typescript-golang 创建了一个自定义的 create-react-app 模板，您所要做的就是在您的工作目录中运行以下命令

$ npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript-golang

还有……轰！ 一个支持 Typescript 和 Go 的工作反应应用程序，您可以直接开始编码🥳

随时通过Github和Linkedin关注和联系



原文:

Run Go in the browser with react and Typescript using WebAssembly

TL;DR

$ npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript-golang

Why create-react-app?

create-react-app allows us to quickly generate the boilerplate required to bootstrap a react application, providing a level of abstraction above the nitty-gritty infrastructure required to create a modern react app (Webpack, Babel, ESlint, etc.)

Why include Go?

Go is a statically typed, compiled programming language designed at Google, it is syntactically similar to C, but with memory safety, garbage collection, structural typing, and CSP-style concurrency.
In my case, I needed to run Go for JSON schema validations, in other cases, you might want to perform a CPU-intensive task or use a CLI tool written in Go.

But WebAssembly is not supported in all browsers!

I thought so too. In reality, since November 2017, WebAssembly is supported in all major browsers. So as long as you don’t need to support Internet Explorer, there is nothing to worry about.

Let’s get down to business 😎

First, initialize a brand new create-react-app project, assuming you’re not an absolute savage, make sure to use the Typescript template 😇

$ npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript

Next, create a folder under /src/LoadWasm

$ cd my-app
$ mkdir ./src/LoadWasm`

create a file to extend the Window type declaration, we’ll use it soon.
/src/LoadWasm/wasmTypes.d.ts

declare global { export interface Window { Go: any; myGolangFunction: (num1: number, num2: number) => number }
} export {};

Copy a file used to load the WebAssembly code into the browser, it adds the Go property on the global window object and will act as a bridge between Javascript and WebAssembly.

This file is similar to the one from the official Go repository, with a few minor tweaks, we’ll use it in the next section.

$ curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/royhadad/cra-template-typescript-golang/main/template/src/LoadWasm/wasm_exec.js > ./src/LoadWasm/wasm_exec.js`

Next, we’ll create a wrapper component that will wrap our entire application and wait for WebAssembly to be loaded. This can be optimized for performance purposes, but it’s good enough for now, for simplicity’s sake.
/src/LoadWasm/index.tsx

import './wasm_exec.js';
import './wasmTypes.d.ts'; import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; async function loadWasm(): Promise<void> { const goWasm = new window.Go(); const result = await WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(fetch('main.wasm'), goWasm.importObject); goWasm.run(result.instance);
} export const LoadWasm: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<{}>> = (props) => { const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = React.useState(true); useEffect(() => { loadWasm().then(() => { setIsLoading(false); }); }, []); if (isLoading) { return ( <div> loading WebAssembly... </div> ); } else { return <React.Fragment>{props.children}</React.Fragment>; }
};

Finally, we’ll wrap our entire application with the LoadWasm component, This will make sure no other components will load before WebAssembly is loaded
/src/index.tsx

const root = ReactDOM.createRoot( document.getElementById('root') as HTMLElement
);
root.render( <React.StrictMode> <LoadWasm> <App /> </LoadWasm> </React.StrictMode>
);

But wait, where is the Go code?

Start by initializing a Go module

$ mkdir ./wasm
$ cd ./wasm
$ go mod init wasm
$ go mod tidy
$ touch ./main.go
$ cd ..

Now we’ll use the syscall/js package to access the javascript Global scope, and set a function on it. Secondly, we’ll implement a little hack to keep the Go code from terminating: opening a channel and waiting for it to finish, without ever using it 😈

This will allow us to continuously communicate with the Go code without needing to re-instantiate it every time.

/wasm/main.go

package main import ( "syscall/js"
) func myGolangFunction() js.Func { return js.FuncOf(func(this js.Value, args []js.Value) interface{} { return args[0].Int() + args[1].Int() })
} func main() { ch := make(chan struct{}, 0) js.Global().Set("myGolangFunction", myGolangFunction()) <-ch
}

Note: you may need to configure your IDE to support WebAssembly, see VS Code guide, Intellij guide

Now we can add this button somewhere in the application, perhaps in App.tsx

<button onClick={() => { alert(window.myGolangFunction(2, 3)); }}> Click here to invoke WebAssembly!
</button>

Putting it all together

Finally, we’ll alter the package.json scripts to support build and hot-reload for WebAssembly.

build: 

"build": "npm run build:wasm && npm run build:ts",
"build:ts": "react-scripts build",
"build:wasm": "cd wasm && GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -o ../public/main.wasm && cd .. && echo \"compiled wasm successfully!\""

hot reload

We’ll need a few dependencies

$ npm install watch concurrently --save-dev

And use them in the start script

"start": "concurrently \"npm run watch:ts\" \"npm run watch:wasm\"",
"watch:ts": "react-scripts start",
"watch:wasm": "watch \"npm run build:wasm\" ./wasm",

Finally, we’ll run npm start and access the app on localhost:3000
A full example can be found in this GitHub repository

Didn’t you say it’ll take 60 seconds? Liar!

Ok, maybe this does take a while, but fear no more! I have a cure for your laziness!
I have created a custom create-react-app template for typescript-golang, all you have to do is run the following command in your working directory

$ npx create-react-app my-app --template typescript-golang

And… boom! A working react app with Typescript & Go support, you can get right into coding 🥳

Feel free to follow & connect via Github & Linkedin
item.author: royhadad

item.title: 开发人员的数字名片 | Digital Visiting Card for developer's
item.description:
我的名字是 Omkar，我很自豪能够推出我实际上已经工作了几个月的产品Digital Visiting Card 。

我确定您想知道：什么是数字名片？ 更重要的是，我该如何使用它？

数字名片就像一张普通的名片，只不过是数字形式的！ 这意味着传递您的联系信息更加容易。 它包含有关您和您的业务的所有信息，您可以与重要的人和不重要的人分享这些信息。

您只需 3 个步骤即可创建您的数字名片...

  1. 在 vcard.plugio.in 上注册

  2. 创建一个 VCard 以输入您的个人详细信息，如姓名、职务、联系电话、社交地址等并保存。

  3. 您的数字名片已准备就绪！ 您现在可以一键在 WhatsApp 上分享！ 或使用可用选项通过电子邮件或短信分享。

以下是您可以使用数字名片的一些方法：

  • 使用电子邮件或社交媒体与潜在客户分享您的联系信息

  • 当您发布到论坛和留言板时，将其用作签名

  • 申请工作时将其包含在您的简历中

最好的部分？ 您的联系方式将一键更新到收件人的电话簿中，因此您不必担心他们手动输入您的信息。

我的 VCard - smaint.plugio.in/v/omkar

毕竟，工作在完成之前永远不会完成，并且这个团队致力于添加功能和更新产品，直到它达到您的高质量标准。

如果您有兴趣了解有关 VCard 的更多信息，可以在线访问我们。 如果您决定尝试我们，我邀请您与我们分享您的反馈！ 我们喜欢听客户说什么。



原文:

My Name is Omkar, and I am proud to launch the product that I have actually been working on for months now, Digital Visiting Card.

I'm sure you're wondering: What is a digital visiting card? And more importantly, how can I use it?

A Digital Visiting Card is just like a regular visiting card, but in digital form! That means it's even easier to pass along your contact information. It has all the information about you and your business that you can share with people who matter as well as those who don't.

You can create your Digital Visiting Card in just 3 steps...

  1. Sign up on vcard.plugio.in

  2. Create a VCard to enter your personal details like name, title, contact number, social handles, etc and save it.

  3. Your Digital Visiting Card is ready! You can now share it on WhatsApp with just one click! Or share it via email or SMS by using the available options.

Here are some ways you can use Digital Visiting Cards:

  • Share your contact information with potential clients using email or social media

  • Use it as a signature when you post to forums and message boards

  • Include it with your resume when applying to jobs

The best part? Your contact details will be updated in your recipient's phone book in just one click, so you don't have to worry about them entering your info manually.

My VCard - smaint.plugio.in/v/omkar

VCard - Developed with "Love"

The VCard.plugio.in team is made up of a bunch of dedicated individuals who have worked really hard to bring this VCard —and they're not planning on stopping there! After all, the job is never done until it's finished, and this team is committed to adding features and updating the product until it meets your high standards for quality.

If you're interested in learning more about VCard, you can visit us online. And if you do decide to try us out, I invite you to share your feedback with us! We love hearing what our customers have to say.
item.author: omkarh

item.title: 如何在 Magento 2 中提高网站加载速度 | How to Increase Website Loading Speed in Magento 2
item.description:
文章最初发表在WebMeridian 博客上。

更多的流量等于更多的收入。
许多用户抱怨 Magento 2 速度慢，但事实并非如此。 您只是没有采取必要的加速步骤。

专注于社交媒体营销、销售甚至网站设计是正常的，但在网站性能方面，许多人忽视了球。

如果您想影响您网站的潜在购物者和访问者，您需要以闪电般的速度给人留下深刻的印象。 下面我们将探讨提高网站加载速度的方法。

提高网站加载速度的有效方法

谷歌已经毫不掩饰地认为页面速度是他们在对任何网站进行排名时使用的一个组件。

一个网站的加载时间不应超过三秒钟，否则用户的满意体验会直线下降。 要使网站排名靠前，您必须确保页面速度符合 Google 的标准。

硬币的另一面需要考虑：如果页面最终超过三秒，它也不会排名。 基本上，您必须快速找到最佳位置。 不太快也不太慢，不能满足谷歌相当严格的标准。

加速 Magento 2 网站

在提高网站加载速度方面，您有多种选择。 下面我们将仔细研究加速 Magento 2 网站的方法。

优化首字节时间 (TTFB)

使用 Magento 2，为了提高速度，您需要优化 TTFB。 如果您正在挠头并想知道 TTFB 到底是什么，那么它代表第一个字节的时间，是浏览器成功下载网页 HTML 所需的时间。

速度慢的结果是用户只剩下一个令人不满意的空白屏幕，这是用户在没有进行购买的情况下离开网站的主要原因。

在这个过程发生的同时，网站的尾端正在处理 HTML，并努力将其有效地发送到用户的浏览器。 基本上，像实际页面的镜像一样的骨架 HTML 文件以及 JavaScript 和 CSS 文件等其他文件正在稍后加载。

正确地，使用 Magento 2 TTFB 似乎很容易，但每次发生自定义更新时，代码都会开始备份，网站最终会变慢。 为了有效地加速 TTFB 发挥其全部潜力，您需要检查 Magento 代码库。
如果您缺乏任何编程技能，那么您应该使用在线监控工具来帮助您或成为专业人士。

考虑内容交付网络 (CDN) 计划

称为内容交付网络 (CDN) 的在线服务是一种区域服务，用于传输 HTML、图像和 CSS/JS 文件等通常称为静态内容的内容。 服务器提供快速有效的交付。

您可以使用多个服务器快速有效地到达全球许多地区。

设置清漆

它通常被称为 HTTP 加速器。 该程序将文件和文件片段存储在其内存中，因此当它需要它们时，它可以快速访问，从而降低响应时间。

如果您擅长代码，您甚至可以使用 Varnish 的 C 代码语言编写缓存策略。 它对大型和小型服务器也很有效。

花点时间检查一下 Magento 商店中的缓存选项。 选择您需要的那些并单击启用选项，然后单击提交页面缓存。 这将降低服务器的响应时间和总页面加载时间。

如何提高页面速度

页面加载速度对于在谷歌等搜索引擎中获得排名很重要。 它是排名算法的重要组成部分，因此您需要采取措施进行优化。

您的主机

您投资的托管包很重要。 您想寻求质量，因为主机就像一个储物箱。 它保存您的数据和网站内容，以确保视频、文本和图像等内容的快速上传速度。

优化图像

如果您的图像未经过优化，那么您可能会体验到速度的急剧下降，在某些情况下可能高达 80%。 显然，图像在主机上占据了大量空间，并且以“重”而闻名。
为了获得可接受的速度，您必须采取额外的步骤，确保优化图像以提高网站加载速度。

在您的电子商务网站上，您可能有很多产品照片。 是的，它们会减慢您的网站速度并影响 Magento 的速度，但它们对于任何利润丰厚的销售网站都是必需的。 您必须优化图像以获得更好的速度。 有趣的是，这是许多人在追求速度时忽略的一件事。

以下是一些获得更令人难以置信的速度的技巧：

  • 压缩图像。
  • 使用图像优化器模块
  • 确保商店的徽标是 SVG 或 PNG 格式
  • 检查您的产品图像是否都是 JPEG 格式。

优化 CSS 交付

在优化 CSS 时，您需要记住每个网页上都有成千上万的代码。 它们必须经过压缩以帮助加快网站速度。 实现这一目标的最佳方法之一是消除不必要的字符。 您还可以扣除减少文件大小的换行符。 如果你压缩文件，那么事情会加快。

更进一步，启用 Gzip 以减少发送给访问者的 HTML 和 CSS 大小。

使用 YUI Compressor 压缩 JavaScript 和 CSS。
请记住，一旦压缩，代码就很难看到，但您将节省大约 20% 到 30% 的容量。

您会发现，当您使用 Magento 网站时，您最终会经常使用 CSS 和 JavaScript。 合并 CSS 和 JavaScript 并通过配置、开发人员最后到 JavaScript 设置进入后端进行缩小。

更新最新版本的 Magento 2

如何提高页面加载速度？ 首先，你运行的是什么版本的 Magento？

推动更令人难以置信的速度的最佳方法之一就是更新到最新版本的 Magento 2 。 每次 Magento 花时间发布新版本时，您都可以放心，它的性能会更好。 它将有许多有助于改进的调整。 不断更新以获得更快的速度。



原文:

The article was initially published in WebMeridian blog.

OK Google, How to Increase Page Speed of Website

In 2021, speed is the name of the game, and every eCommerce site wants to increase its traffic.

More traffic equals more revenue.
Many users complain that Magento 2 is slow, but nothing could be further from the truth. You just haven’t taken the necessary speed up steps.

Magento 2 has the capability to increase speeds substantially, so you’ll have more traffic. It’s normal to focus on social media marketing, sales and even website design, but many lose sight of the ball when it comes to site performance.

If you want to impact potential shoppers and visitors to your site, you need to make a strong impression with lightning-fast speeds. Below we will explore ways to increase website loading speeds.

Effective Ways to Increase Website Loading Speed

Google has made it no secret that page speed is a component that they use when ranking any website.

A website should not take more than three seconds to load, or the user plummets’ satisfactory experience. For a website to rank high, it is essential that you make sure the page speed is within Google’s standards.

There is another side of the coin to consider: if the page should end up exceeding three seconds, it will not rank as well. Basically, you must find the sweet spot with speed. Not too fast and not too slow to meet Google’s rather stringent standards.

Speed Up Magento 2 Website

You have several options when it comes to ways to increase website load speed. Below we will look closely at ways to speed up a Magento 2 website.

Optimise Time to First Byte (TTFB)

With Magento 2, to increase speeds you’ll want to optimise the TTFB. If you are scratching your head and wondering what exactly is the TTFB, well it stands for time to the first byte and is the time that it takes for a browser to successfully download the HTML of a web page.

The result of a slow speed is that the user is left with nothing but a blank screen which is unsatisfactory and a major cause of a user navigating away from the website without ever making a purchase.

While the process is occurring, the tail end of the website is working on the HTML out and striving to effectively send it to the user’s browser. Basically, the skeletal HTML file like a mirror of the actual page and other files like the JavaScript and the CSS files are working to load later.

Correctly the TTFB might seem easy with Magento 2 but every time a customisation update occurs, the code starts to back up and the website ends up becoming slower. To effectively speed up the TTFB to its full potential you’ll need to examine the Magento codebase.
If you lack any programming skills, then you should use an online monitoring tool to help you or turn into a professional.

Consider a Content Delivery Network (CDN) Plan

The online service referred to as a content delivery network (CDN) is a regional service to transfer things like HTML, images, and CSS/JS files, which are commonly referred to as static content. The servers provide fast and efficient delivery.

You can use a multitude of servers to reach many areas of the globe quickly and efficiently.

Setup Varnish

The program Varnish works by caching content. It is often referred to as an HTTP accelerator. The program stores files and file fragments within its memory, so when it needs them it has rapid access, which lowers response time.

If you are good with code, you can even write caching policies using Varnish’s C code language. It is efficient with both large and small servers too.

Take the time to examine the caching options in your Magento store. Pick those you need and click on the enable option and then Submit Page Cache. This will lower the server’s response time and total page load time.

How to Improve the Page Speed

Page load speed matters when it comes to gaining rank with a search engine like Google. It is a crucial component of the ranking algorithms, so you’ll need to take steps to optimise.

Your Host

The hosting package that you invest in matters. You want to seek out quality because the host is like a storage bin. It holds your data and website content to ensure fast upload speeds for things like video, text, and images.

Optimise Images

If your images are not optimised, then you can experience a dramatic slowdown in speeds that can be as significant as 80 percent in some instances. Clearly, images occupy a great deal of space on the host and are well known as being ‘heavy’.
To gain acceptable speeds, you’ll have to go the extra step by making sure the images are optimised to increase website loading speed.

On your eCommerce website, you probably have lots of product photos. Yes, they slow your site down and impact Magento’s speed, but they are necessary for any lucrative sale site. You must optimise the images to gain better speeds. Interesting, this is one thing that many overlook in their quest for speed.

Here are a few tricks to gain more incredible speed:

  • Compress the images.
  • Use an Image Optimiser module
  • Ensure that the store’s logo is either an SVG or PNG format
  • Check to see if the images of your products are all JPEG formats.

Optimise CSS Delivery

When optimising CSS, you need to remember that there are thousands of codes on every web page. They must undergo compression to help speed up the website. One of the best ways to achieve this is to eliminate unnecessary characters. You can also deduct line breaks which reduce the file size. If you compress the files, then things will speed up.

Go one step further and enable Gzip to reduce the HTML and CSS size, which are sent out to visitors.

CSS & JavaScript

Use the YUI Compressor to compress both JavaScript and CSS.
Remember, once compressed, the code is hard to see, but you’ll save around 20 to 30 per cent capacity.

You’ll discover that when you work with a Magento website, you’ll end up using CSS & JavaScript frequently. Merge the CSS & JavaScript and minify by going to the backend through configure, developer and finally to JavaScript Settings.

Update the Latest Version of Magento 2

How to improve page load speed? Firstly, what version of Magento are you running?

One of the best ways you can push for more incredible speeds is simply to update to the latest version of Magento 2. Every time Magento takes the time to release a new version, you can be assured that its performance is better. It will have numerous tweaks that help improve things. Constantly update to gain greater speeds.

item.title: Magento 1 生命终结：您需要知道的一切 | Magento 1 End of Life: Everything You Need to Know
item.description:
文章最初发表在WebMeridian 博客上。

但是，这并不意味着它将完全停止运行。 相反，您可以根据需要继续使用它。 您必须担心的许多事情包括功能减少、错误问题以及缺少 Adob​​e 支持。

这仅仅意味着您会比您预期的更频繁地被黑客入侵。 此外，您将无法修复错误问题或从 Adob​​e 获得任何其他支持。 您将没有安全保障，您在网站上所面对的一切都将由您承担。

如果您有 Magento 1.X 版网站该怎么办？

如果您拥有 Magento 1 网站，唯一要做的就是升级。 最好计划一下如何将Magento 1 迁移到 Magento 2 ，并为您的网站用户做好升级准备。 这是因为从 Magento 1 迁移到 Magento 2 并不像看起来那么容易。

它对您和您的客户都更安全、更好。 但是，如果您仍要使用 Magento 1 一段时间，那么您应该记住一些事情。

更新 Magento 2 版本的 3 个理由

更新到 Magento 2版本的原因有很多。 这些将有利于您有效地运营您的电子商务商店。 让我们来看看其中的三个。

  • 安全性：到目前为止，这是升级 Magento 2 的一个很好的理由。该平台旨在通过其新的哈希算法、欺诈保护和身份验证因素为您提供更好的安全性。 这样，您就可以免受黑客和您在平台上可能遇到的其他小问题的影响。
  • 特性和兼容性： Magento 2 上有几个 Magento 1 上没有的新特性。这些特性使扩展更容易下载并提高平台的速度。
  • 支持：一旦您升级到新的和改进的 Magento 2，您将开始再次获得 Adob​​e 的全面支持。 这是升级软件的根本原因。 在 Adob​​e 的支持下，您还可以通过不定期修复和增强的形式获得性能改进更新。

如果您打算继续使用 Magento 1，您需要知道什么？

如果您考虑了上述原因并决定继续使用 Magento 1，那么您应该预料到会发生一些事情。

一方面，您将不再获得三个 Magento 版本中的任何一个的质量修复。 最终，这将导致平台功能受损和许多其他问题。 但这甚至不是可怕的部分。

可怕的部分是你会暴露在黑客面前，当你的网站功能被破坏时，他们很容易访问你的网站。 而且由于您失去了 adobe 的支持，您迟早也会失去对大多数支付网关的访问权限。

所以，好好想想吧。 您是否真的想经历所有这些以及更多，只是为了继续使用一个不再获得开发人员支持的平台？ 然后，您必须考虑尽早迁移。 这是你可以如何去做的。

从 Magento 1 迁移到 Magento 2 对于您的电子商务商店来说非常重要，但这并不是一件容易的事。 如果您不精通技术并且希望完美完成，您将需要开发人员或迁移服务专家来帮助您完成该过程。

完成它需要遵循几个步骤，每个步骤都是有代价的。 价格取决于您要添加到新平台的功能、扩展和自定义的数量。

整个过程需要数月才能完成，如果所有过程同时完成，则可以更快。 这就是为什么您应该联系专业的Magento 迁移服务来帮助您快速有效地完成工作。

这些是使 Magento 2 变得更好的一些功能。

  • 前端和后端：平台的前端和后端都有独特的升级。 前端实现了响应式网页设计，使网站与所有设备兼容。 它提高了性能，使网站加载速度更快。 另一方面，后端具有直观的 UI/UX，可以根据您的喜好进行配置。
  • 扩展：与 Magento 1 相比，Magento 2 只有少数扩展，因为它相对较新。 但是我们都知道，在 Magento 1 上安装扩展是相当困难的，而且这些扩展经常会偶尔发生冲突。 它们之间的区别在于 Magento 2 上扩展程序的安装和使用更快、更直观、更轻松。
  • 安全性：平台的安全系统相当完善。 该平台使用 SHA-256 系统来保护密码并确保网站不会被破坏。 还有一个欺诈保护系统，可确保网站所有者和用户都免受欺诈。
  • 支持： Adobe 将提供比其前身更多的支持。 这样，您将不断获得升级、修复和许多新功能，以使该平台成为您电子商务网站的最佳选择。
  • 更快的性能：您的整个平台开始加载比在 Magento 1 上更快，即使前端没有缓存。 添加了这些技术以优化整个站点。

概括

最好的选择是升级到 Magento 2，以避免在 Adob​​e 放弃对 Magento 1 的支持后使用 Magento 1 可能出现的任何问题。 为了安全起见，您可以联系我们的业务和技术咨询专家来帮助您。
我们可以完美地解释整个过程，并以最好的方式为您完成所有工作。 让我们谈谈吧！



原文:

The article was initially published in WebMeridian blog.

What Does Magento 1 X End of Life Mean?

Magento 1 is basically out of date, and it has given way to the new and improved version of the platform. However, this does not mean that it would stop functioning altogether. On the contrary, you can continue using it for as long as you like. Some of the many things you would have to worry about are reduced functionality, bug issues, and the absence of Adobe support.

This simply means that you would get hacked more often than you expect. Also, you would not be able to fix bug issues or get any other support from Adobe. You would have no security, and whatever you face on your website would be yours to bear.

What to Do if You Have a Magento Version 1.X Website?

The only thing to do if you have a Magento 1 website is to upgrade. It would be best to plan how you would migrate Magento 1 to Magento 2 and prepare your website users for an upgrade. This is because migrating from Magento 1 to Magento 2 will not be an as easy a task as it may seem.

It is safer and better for both you and your customers. However, if you are still going to be on Magento 1 for a while, there are some things you should have in mind.

3 Reasons to Update Magento 2 Version

There are a handful of reasons to update to the Magento 2 version. These would benefit you in operating your eCommerce store effectively. Let us take a look at three of them.

  • Security: this is, by far, a very good reason to upgrade Magento 2. The platform is designed to offer you better security with its new hashing algorithm, fraud protection, and authentication factor. This way, you are protected from hackers and other minor issues you might face on the platform.
  • Features and Compatibility: there are several new features on Magento 2 that are not present on Magento 1. These features make the extensions easier to download and enhance the speed of the platform.
  • Support: once you upgrade to the new and improved Magento 2, you would begin to receive full support from Adobe again. It is a fundamental reason to upgrade your software. With support from Adobe, you also get performance improvements updates in the form of occasional fixes and enhancements.

What Do You Need to Know if You’re Planning to Stay on Magento 1?

If you considered the reasons stated above and decided to stay on Magento 1, you should expect some things to happen.

For one, you will not get quality fixes any more for any of the three Magento versions. Eventually, this will lead to broken functionality of the platform and a host of other problems. But that is not even the scary part.

The scary part is that you would be exposed to hackers, and they would easily gain access to your website as the functionality of your site becomes broken. And since you have lost support from adobe, you would also lose access to most gateways for payments sooner or later.

So, have a deep thought about it. Do you really want to go through all that and more, just to keep using a platform that no longer receives support from its developers? Then you have to consider migrating sooner than later. Here is how you can go about it.

Magento 1 to Magento 2 Migration

Migrating from Magento 1 to Magento 2 is very important for your eCommerce store, but it is not an easy task. You would need a developer or migration service experts to help you with the process if you are not tech-savvy and if you want it done perfectly.

There are a couple of steps to follow in getting it done, and each of these steps comes at a price. The price depends on the number of features, extensions, and customizations you want to add to your new platform.

The entire process takes months to complete and can be made faster if all the processes are done simultaneously. This is why you should contact a professional Magento migration service to help you get the job done quickly and efficiently.

What Are the Top Features of Magento 2?

These are a few features that make Magento 2 better.

  • Frontend and Backend: both the frontend and backend of the platform come with unique upgrades. The frontend implements a responsive web design to make the site compatible with all devices. It boosts performance so that the site would load faster. On the other hand, the backend has an intuitive UI/UX and can be configured to fit your taste.
  • Extensions: there are only a handful of extensions for Magento 2 compared to Magento 1 since it is relatively new. But we all know that the installation of extensions on Magento 1 has been quite difficult and that these extensions often clash with each other occasionally. The difference between them is that the installation and use of extensions on Magento 2 is faster, intuitive, and stress-free.
  • Security: the security system of the platform is quite sophisticated. The platform uses the SHA-256 system for protecting passwords and ensuring that the site would not be breached. There is also a fraud protection system that ensures that both the site owner and the users are both protected from scams.
  • Support: there will be more support from Adobe than its predecessor. This way, you would constantly get upgrades, fixes, and a host of new features to make the platform the best it can be for your eCommerce website.
  • Faster Performance: your entire platform begins to load faster than it would have on Magento 1, even without caching on the frontend. These technologies have been added to optimize the entire site.

Summary

Magento 1.9 end of life date has been announced, and the platform has come to an end, and it is no news that a new and improved version is out.

The best option is to upgrade to Magento 2 to avoid any issues that might arise from using Magento 1 after Adobe has dropped support for it. To be on the safe side, you can contact our business and tech consulting experts to help you with it.
We can explain the entire process perfectly and get it all done for you in the best way possible. Let’s talk!

item.title: JavaScript 终极指南 02：DOM | JavaScript Ultimate Guide 02: The DOM
item.description:
作为 Web 开发人员，如果您想创建动态网页，则必须通过 DOM 来完成。

在本文中，我将展示 dom 是什么以及如何使用 DOM 操作网页。

表中的内容

先决条件

什么是 DOM？

先决条件

我假设您了解 JavaScript 的基础知识。
如果您还不了解基础知识，请不要担心。 我写了一篇涵盖所有JS 基础主题的文章。 你可以在这里阅读。

所以，事不宜迟，让我们开始吧。

什么是 DOM？

这是 MDN 对 DOM 的评价。

文档对象模型 (DOM) 是Web 文档的编程接口
它表示页面，以便程序可以更改文档结构、样式和内容。

通过使用 JavaScript 操作 DOM，您可以为网页添加交互性。

让我们看看如何使用 DOM 操作网页。

使用 DOM 选择单个元素

要首先操作网页，您需要访问其元素。 使用 DOM，您可以通过多种方式访问​​单个元素。 这里有几个：

  1. 使用 DOM 选择多个元素

    使用 DOM 时，有时您需要一次选择多个元素。 有很多方法可以一次获取多个元素。

    像：

    1. 使用 DOM 为元素设置样式

      现在，您知道如何选择单个元素和多个元素。

      让我们为您选择的元素添加一些样式。

      您可以使用 .style 属性和要添加或更改的 CSS 属性为元素添加样式。

      这是一个例子。

       const body = document . quearySelector ( " body " ); body . style . color = " red " ;

      使用 addEventListener 添加事件

      在我们给元素添加事件之前，首先你需要了解什么是事件？

      事件是用户或浏览器做某事时发生的某种动作。

      以下是一些事件示例：

      • 浏览器加载网站
      • 用户更改输入字段
      • 用户点击了一个按钮
      • 用户将鼠标悬停在元素上。

      最常用的事件之一是 OnClick 事件。 这是为元素的单击事件添加的。

      这是一个点击事件的例子。

       const btn = document . querySelector ( " .btn " ); btn . addEventLister ( " click " , function () { console . log ( " The button was clicked " ); })

      要添加事件，首先，您需要使用任何 DOM 选择器抓取元素。

      然后，您必须向该元素添加.addEventListener("event", eventHandlerFunction)方法。

      如您所见， addEventLister 方法有 2 个参数，一个是事件，另一个是事件处理函数。

      事件处理函数确定事件发生时会发生什么。

      在我们上面的示例中，当您单击按钮时，它将在 console.log 中显示“按钮被单击”字符串。

      结论。

      在本文中，我们讨论了 DOM 是什么以及如何使用 DOM 查询 HTML 元素，以及最后如何向它们添加 EventLIsenter。

      感谢您阅读到最后。 如果你喜欢这篇文章，你可能也会喜欢我的其他内容。

      在 Twitter @coderamrin上关注我以查看我的所有内容。



原文:

DOM is one of the most important concepts for web developers. As a web developer, if you want to create a dynamic web page, you’ll have to do that through DOM.

In this article, I’ll show what dom is and how to manipulate a web page with DOM.

Table Of Content

Prerequisite

What is DOM?

Selecting Single Element with DOM

Selecting multiple elements with DOM

Styling an Element with DOM

Adding Events with addEventListener

Prerequisite

I assume you know the fundamentals of JavaScript.
If you don’t know the fundamentals yet, don’t worry. I wrote an article that covers all the JS fundamental topics. You can read it here.

So, without any delay, let's get started.

What is DOM?

DOM stands for Document Object Model.

Here’s what MDN says about DOM.

The Document Object Model (DOM) is a programming interface for web documents
. It represents the page so that programs can change the document structure, style, and content.

By manipulating the DOM with JavaScript you can add interactivity to the web page.

Let’s look at how you can manipulate the web page with DOM.

Selecting Single Element with DOM

To manipulate a web page first you need to access its elements. With DOM you can access a single element in many ways. Here are a few:

  1. getElementById(”elements-id”)
  2. querySelector(”element name or class, or id”) 

//get element by ID
const element = document.getElementById("intro"); //Get element with querySelector
const elementQuery = document.querySelector("intro");

Selecting multiple elements with DOM

When working with DOM sometimes you’ll need to select multiple elements at once. There are quite a few ways to get multiple elements at once.

Like:

  1. getElementsByClassName(”Id”);
  2. querySelectorAll(”intro”) 
 //get all elements by class names
const element = document.getElementsByClassName("intro"); //Get all elements with querySelector
const elementQuery = document.querySelector("intro");

Styling an Element with DOM

Now that, you know how to select single element and multiple elements.

Let's add some style to the element you selected.

You can add styling to your element with the .style property and the CSS property you want to add or change.

Here’s an example.


const body = document.quearySelector("body");
body.style.color = "red";

Adding Events with addEventListener

Before we add events to elements, first you need to understand what an event is?

Events are some kind of action that happens when the user or browser does something.

Here are a few examples of events:

  • Browser load the website
  • User changes an input field
  • User clicked on a button
  • User hovers over an element.

One of the most used events is the OnClick event. Which is added for the click event of an element.

here's an example of a click event.


const btn = document.querySelector(".btn"); btn.addEventLister("click", function() { console.log("The button was clicked");
})

To add the event, first, you’ll need to grab the element with any DOM selector.

Then you’ll have to add .addEventListener("event", eventHandlerFunction) method to that element.

As you can see addEventLister method takes 2 arguments, one is the event and the other one is the event handler function.

The event handler function determines what will happen when the event occurs.

In our example above, when you clicked the button it will console.log "The button was clicked" string.

Conclusion.

In this article, we discussed what DOM is and how to query HTML elements with DOM, and finally how to add EventLIstenter to them.

Thanks for reading till the end. If you enjoyed this article you might enjoy my other content too.

Follow me on Twitter @coderamrin to see all my stuff.
item.author: coderamrin

item.title: BearCam 伴侣 | BearCam Companion
item.description:
在我作为 AWS 社区建设者的第一篇文章中，我列出了我在该计划中的第一年目标。 它是为BearID 项目定义和开发新的开源应用程序，同时涉及物联网 (IoT)、机器学习、应用程序和我对环境的热情。 经过深思熟虑，我确定了我将要开发的第一个应用程序，BearCam Companion。

对于那些不知道 BearID 项目起源故事的人来说，这一切都始于Explore的Brooks Falls Brown Bear cam 。 正如我在我的第一篇博文中解释的那样， Guilty Pleasures ，我和我的搭档都是狂热的野生动物网络摄像头粉丝。 我们最喜欢的一些网络摄像头是阿拉斯加卡特迈国家公园的探索摄像头。 我们最初专注于在 Brooks Falls 构建熊的照片数据集。 我们从国家公园管理局在卡特迈的熊监测计划中收集了个人照片和一大组照片。 从那里我们与不列颠哥伦比亚省的保护科学家 Melanie Clapham 博士合作，研究格伦代尔湾的熊。 我们构建了一台计算机来运行我们的 ML 培训，并开发了一个开源应用程序Bearid ，我们将它作为一个 Docker 容器提供给 Melanie，她可以在笔记本电脑上现场运行。

现在我想看看我们能不能把 BearID 的东西带到熊的摄像头上。 熊凸轮 Disqus 评论中最热门的问题之一是“那只熊是谁？” 最初，这将使用机器学习来定位熊并依靠用户来帮助识别它们。 稍后，我们也可以尝试训练 ML 模型来识别熊（当检测到人脸时，我们也可以尝试现有的 BearID 模型）。 以下是我将如何定义最小可爱产品：

  • 从网络摄像头抓取一帧
  • 检测框架中的熊（可能还有其他动物）
  • 在网页上显示框架和边界框
  • 允许用户（最终是 ML 模型）识别熊（并编辑检测错误）
  • 提供网页框架中每只熊的顶部标识

该项目将勾选机器学习和应用程序。 我打算将 AWS 服务用于机器学习和尽可能多的应用程序。 我还可以通过考虑AWS 架构完善的框架的可持续性支柱来评估环境影响。 我想尽可能使用基于 Graviton 的服务，既是为了环境影响，也是为了我与 Arm 的联系。 现在我将把物联网作为一个单独的话题。

下一步是决定架构。 有很多 AWS 服务和开源工具需要考虑。 我正在考虑以下问题：

  • 使用 Lambda 函数抓取帧
  • 将 Rekognition 或在 SageMaker 中训练和部署的标准对象检测器用于熊检测器
  • 将 Amplify 用于具有 2 种主要模式的 Web 应用程序（可能是 Vue 或 React 前端）：
    • 显示带有框和标签的最新帧
    • 允许用户编辑框和标签
  • 使用 S3 存储图像，使用数据库存储元数据（DynamoDB？）

这将是我第一次从头开始构建全栈 Web 应用程序。 我可以使用一些帮助。 如果您有任何想法或建议，请在评论中告诉我。 谢谢！



原文:

In my first post as an AWS Community Builder, I laid out my goal for year 1 in the program. It is to define and develop new, open source applications for the BearID Project while touching on the Internet of Things (IoT), machine learning, applications and my passion for the environment. After much consideration, I have settled on the first application I will work on, a BearCam Companion.

For those that don't know the origin story of the BearID Project, it all started with the Brooks Falls Brown Bear cam at Explore. As I explained in one of my first blog posts, Guilty Pleasures, my partner and I are avid wildlife webcam fans. Some of our favorite webcams are the Explore cams at Katmai National Park in Alaska. The bears at Brooks Falls and a desire to start a machine learning project led us to attempt to develop an application which could identify the individual bears we see on the Brooks Falls live cam.

We initially focused on building a photo dataset of the bears at Brooks Falls. We collected photos from individuals and a large set from the National Parks Service's bear monitoring program at Katmai. From there we got involved with Dr. Melanie Clapham, a conservation scientist in British Columbia, studying the bears of Glendale Cove. We built a computer to run our ML training and developed an open source application, bearid, which we provided to Melanie as a Docker container she could run in the field on a laptop.

Now I would like to see if we can bring something from BearID to the bear cams. One of the top questions on the bear cam Disqus comments is "who is that bear?" The idea of the BearCam Companion is to help users identify which bears are which. Initially, this would use machine learning to locate bears and rely on users to help identify them. Later, we can try to train ML models to identify the bears as well (when a face is detected, we can also try the existing BearID model). Here's how I will define the Minimum Lovable Product:

  • Grab a frame from the webcam
  • Detect the bears (and possibly other animals) in the frame
  • Display the frame and bounding boxes on a webpage
  • Allow users (and eventually an ML model) to identify the bears (and edit detection errors)
  • Provide the top identifications for each bear in the frame on the webpage

This project will tick the boxes for machine learning and applications. I intend to utilize AWS services for the machine learning and for as much of the application as makes sense. I can also evaluate environmental impact by considering the Sustainability Pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. I would like to make use of Graviton-based services where possible, both for the environmental impact as well as my connection with Arm. For now I will leave IoT as a separate topic.

The next step is to decide on the architecture. There are so many AWS services and open source tools to consider. I am thinking about the following:

  • Use a Lambda function for grabbing frames
  • Use Rekognition or a standard object detector trained and deployed in SageMaker for the bear detector
  • Use Amplify for the web application (maybe Vue or React frontend) with 2 main modes:
    • Display the latest frame with boxes and labels
    • Allow user to edit boxes and labels
  • Use S3 for images and a database for metadata (DynamoDB?)

This will be my first time building a full stack web application from scratch. I could use some help. If you have any thoughts or suggestions, please let me know in the comments. Thanks!
item.author: bluevalhalla

item.title: 如何使用 JavaScript 构建计数器应用程序 | How to build a counter app with JavaScript
item.description:
如果你 google 什么是学习编程的最佳方式？ 每个人都会说学习编程的最好方法是构建项目。

但是，您不会找到完整的指南来说明构建项目的内容和方法。

为了填补这个空白，我开始了 JavaScript 项目系列。 在本系列中，我将向您展示如何构建从小型玩具项目到具有投资组合价值的项目。

这是第 1 部分和项目 1。在这部分中，我将使用 Vanilla JavaScript 构建一个简单的 Counter 应用程序。

所以，事不宜迟，让我们开始吧。

先决条件

这是一个项目教程，所以我假设您具有 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 的基本知识。

你不必知道所有这些。 只是基本面。

如果您认为自己需要提高 JavaScript 技能，请不要担心。 我写了一篇关于 JavaScript Fundamental 的文章，你可以在这里阅读。

启动文件

正如我之前所说，这是一个 JavaScript 项目系列。 因此，我不会向您展示如何编写 HTML 和 CSS。

在开始下载启动文件并在您最喜欢的代码编辑器上打开它们之前。

入门文件

将项目分解成更小的部分

您可能在想她为什么不开始编码？

与我裸露。 当您开始编码时，分解项目将为您节省大量时间。

这是程序员最重要的技能之一。

尽管今天的项目并没有那么大，但我仍然会向您展示如何将其分解为小部分。

因为当您开始自己构建更大的项目时，它会为您提供帮助。

让我们分解项目。

分解项目的第一步是了解项目以及它将具有哪些功能。

计数器应用程序：计数器应用程序将有一个递增、递减和重置按钮。 和一个显示数字的显示器。

如您所见，当您了解您正在构建的项目时。 它会自动分解成小部分。

所以，这些部分是：

  1. 显示数字的显示器。
  2. 增加计数器，
  3. 递减计数器，
  4. 重置计数器。

现在你已经有了小部件，终于到了开始编码的时候了。

让我们卷起袖子开始编码吧。

要首先为数字创建显示，我们需要将元素放入 JavaScript 文件中。

这是 HTML 中的显示元素。

 <h1 class= "display" > 0 </h1>

我们将使用 DOM 来获取 JavaScript 中的元素。 如果您对 DOM 不太了解，请在继续之前查看这篇文章。

要在 JavaScript 中获取元素，您可以使用 document.querySelector 方法，如下所示：

 const display = document . querySelector ( ' .display ' );

我们将把它留在这里，当我们添加增量、减量和重置功能时，我们将更新显示。

让我们一一添加。 第一次递增计数器函数。

就像显示一样，要首先添加 Increment 功能，您必须将 incrementBtn 放入 JS 文件中。

 const incrementBtn = document . querySelector ( " #increment " )

之后，您必须将 EventListener 添加到 Button。

您可以使用 element.addEventListener 方法向元素添加事件。 像这样：

 incrementBtn . addEventListener ( ' EventName ' , eventHandlerFunction );

让我们添加您要添加​​的事件以及处理该事件的事件处理函数。

 incrementBtn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , increment );

如您所见，我只添加了增量函数的名称。 您还可以在那里声明增量函数。 这样看起来更干净。

如果你现在运行代码，你会得到一个错误。 因为增量函数还不存在。

让我们创建增量函数。

 let value = 0 ; function increment () { value += 1 ; display . textContent = value ; }

首先，声明一个变量来存储值。 然后在增量函数中，将值加一。 最后，将值设置为显示的文本，否则显示不会更新。

注意：您必须在函数外部声明 value 变量。 如果您在增量函数中声明它，它将不起作用。 因为它会在每次点击时更新，将值设置为 1。

现在增量按钮应该可以正常工作了。

让我们继续并添加递减函数。

就像首先增加按钮一样，您必须抓住减少按钮。

 const decrementBtn = document . querySelector ( ' #decrement ' );

然后您必须使用 .addEventListener 添加事件侦听器。

 decrementBtn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , decrement ); function decrement () { value -= 1 ; display . textContent = value ; }

这几乎就像增量函数，但不同的是你不是加一，而是从值中减去一。 然后将值设置为显示文本。

重置功能是最简单的功能。 抓住重置按钮后，添加 evenListener。

 const resetBtn = document . querySelector ( ' #reset ' ); resetBtn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , reset ); function reset () { value = 0 ; display . textContent = value ; }

重置按钮只是将值设置为零。

就是这个。 计数器应用程序已完成。 你可以在这里看到完成的项目。 尽管我们有很多重复的代码违背了 DRY（不要重复自己）原则。 别担心，在下一节中，我将向您展示如何重构代码以使其更加干净和干燥。

这是所有代码：

 const display = document . querySelector ( ' .display ' ); const incrementBtn = document . querySelector ( ' #increment ' ); const decrementBtn = document . querySelector ( ' #decrement ' ); const resetBtn = document . querySelector ( ' #reset ' ); incrementBtn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , increment ); decrementBtn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , decrement ); resetBtn . addEventListener ( ' click ' , reset ); let value = 0 ; function increment () { value += 1 ; display . textContent = value ; } function decrement () { value -= 1 ; display . textContent = value ; } function reset () { value = 0 ; display . textContent = value ; }

重构代码

即使我们的应用程序完成了，代码也不干净。 我们重复了很多。 例如，我们在其中添加了 EventListener ，并分别选择了三个按钮

让我们稍微清理一下代码。

您可以通过选择按钮的父级来开始重构。 如果您将 EventListener 添加到父级，则该事件也将添加到子级。

这样你可以删除很多代码。

 //refactored code const allBtns = document . getElementById ( ' #allBtns ' );

现在您可以将 EventListener 添加到 allBtns 元素。 像这样

allBtns . addEventListener ( ' click ' , counter );

现在您需要选择所有按钮并根据按钮执行操作。

为此，您可以这样做。

 function counter ( e ) { const btn = e . target ; console . log ( btn ) }

事件处理函数接受一个参数 e，它是一个具有很多属性的对象。

如果您调用 e.target ，您将获得当前单击的元素，在这种情况下是一个按钮。

现在您可以查看id名称，然后您可以将特定功能添加到按钮。

您使用 e.target.id 获得元素的 id

现在您可以将当前单击的按钮与类名进行比较并添加操作。

 function counter ( e ) { const btn = e . target . id ; if ( btn === ' increment ' ) { value += 1 ; display . textContent = value ; } else if ( btn === ' decrement ' ) { value -= 1 ; display . textContent = value ; } else { value = 0 ; display . textContent = value ; } }

以下是重构后的所有代码：

 const display = document . querySelector ( ' .display ' ); const allBtns = document . querySelector ( ' #allBtns ' ); allBtns . addEventListener ( ' click ' , counter ); let value = 0 ; function counter ( e ) { const btn = e . target . id ; if ( btn === ' increment ' ) { value += 1 ; } else if ( btn === ' decrement ' ) { value -= 1 ; } else { value = 0 ; } display . textContent = value ; }

代码更加简洁易懂。

完成项目

源代码

结论

在本文中，我们构建了一个计数器应用程序。

如果这对我有帮助，请在 twitter @coderarmin上关注我以获取更多类似的文章。

在那之前快乐编码



原文:

If you google What is the best way to learn programming? Everyone will say the best way to learn programming is by building projects.

But, you won’t find a complete guide that will show what and how to build projects.

To fill that gap, I started the JavaScript Project Series. In this series, I’ll show you how to build projects from small toy projects to portfolio-worthy projects.

This is part 1 and project 1. In this part, I’ll build a simple Counter app with Vanilla JavaScript.

So, without any delay let’s get started.

Prerequisites

This is a project tutorial so, I assume you have a basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

You don’t have to know all of it. Just the fundamentals.

If you think you need a little brush up on your JavaScript skills, don’t worry. I wrote an article on JavaScript Fundamental you can read it here.

Starter files

As I said before this is a JavaScript project series. So, I won’t show you how to write the HTML and CSS.

Before starting download the starter files and open them on your favorite code editor.

Starter Files

Breaking down the projects into smaller parts

You might be thinking why doesn't she start coding?

Bare with me. Breaking down projects will save you a ton of time when you start coding.

It’s one of the most important skills for programmers.

Even though today’s project is not that big, I’ll still show you how to break it down into little parts.

Because It’ll help you when you start building bigger projects on your own.

Let’s break down the project.

The first step of breaking down a project is understanding the project and what are the features it’ll have.

Counter App: The Counter App will have an increment, decrement, and reset button. And a display to show the number.

As you can see, when you understand the project you are building. It is automatically broken down into small parts.

So, the parts are:

  1. A display to show the number.
  2. Increment the counter,
  3. Decrement the counter,
  4. Reset the counter.

Now that you have the small parts, It’s finally the time to start coding.

Let’s roll your sleeves and start coding.

01. A display to show the number.

To create the display for the number first we need to get the element into the JavaScript file.

Here’s the display element in the HTML.

 <h1 class="display">0</h1>

We’ll use the DOM to get the element in JavaScript. If you don’t know much about DOM check out this article before moving on.

DOM article LINK

To get the element in JavaScript you can use the document.querySelector method like this:

const display = document.querySelector('.display');

The document.querySelector method takes, a class or an ID or the elements name.

We’ll just leave it here, when we add the Increment, Decrement, and Reset Functionalities we’ll update the display.

Let’s add them one by one. First Increment The Counter function.

02. Increment the counter

Just like the display, to add the Increment functionality first you’ll have to get the incrementBtn into the JS file.

const incrementBtn = document.querySelector("#increment")

After that, you’ll have to add the EventListener to the Button.

You can add an event to an element with element.addEventListener method. Like this:

 incrementBtn.addEventListener('EventName', eventHandlerFunction);

The addEventListener method takes two arguments, the first one is the Event you want to add and the second one is the function that determines what will happen when the event occurs.

Let’s add the event you want to add and the event handler function to handle the event.

 incrementBtn.addEventListener('click', increment);

As you can see I added just the name of the increment function. You can also declare the increment function there. It just looks cleaner this way.

If you run the code right now you’ll get an error. because the increment function dosen’t exist yet.

Let’s create the increment function.

let value = 0; function increment() { value += 1; display.textContent = value;
}

First, declare a variable to store the value. And then inside the increment function, increment the value by one. Lastly, set the value as the text of the display, otherwise, the display won’t update.

Note: You have to declare the value variable outside the function. If you declare it inside the increment function it won’t work. Because it’ll update on every click, setting the value to 1.

Now the increment button should work properly.

Let’s move on and add the decrement function.

03. Decrement the counter

Just like the increment button first, you’ll have to grab the decrement button.

const decrementBtn = document.querySelector('#decrement');

Then you’ll have to add the event listener with .addEventListener.

decrementBtn.addEventListener('click', decrement); function decrement() { value -= 1; display.textContent = value;
}

This is almost like the increment function, but the difference is you are not adding one you are subtracting one from the value. And then setting the value as the display text.

04. Reset the counter

The reset functionality is the simplest one. After grabbing the reset button, you add the evenListener.

const resetBtn = document.querySelector('#reset');
resetBtn.addEventListener('click', reset); function reset() { value = 0; display.textContent = value;
}

The reset button just sets the value to zero.

This is it. The counter app is done. You can see the finished project here. Even though we have a lot of repeating code that goes against the DRY(don’t repeat yourself) principle. Don’t worry in the next section I’ll show you how to refactor the code to make it more clean and DRY.

Here’s all the code:

const display = document.querySelector('.display');
const incrementBtn = document.querySelector('#increment');
const decrementBtn = document.querySelector('#decrement');
const resetBtn = document.querySelector('#reset'); incrementBtn.addEventListener('click', increment);
decrementBtn.addEventListener('click', decrement);
resetBtn.addEventListener('click', reset); let value = 0;
function increment() { value += 1; display.textContent = value;
} function decrement() { value -= 1; display.textContent = value;
} function reset() { value = 0; display.textContent = value;
}

Refactoring the code

Even though our app is done, the code is not clean. we are repeating a lot. For example, we are adding EventListener there times, and selecting the three buttons separately

Let’s clean up the code a bit.

You can start refactoring by selecting the parent of the button. If you add an EventListener to the parent the event will be added to the children too.

This way you can remove a lot of code.

//refactored code
const allBtns = document.getElementById('#allBtns');

Now you can add an EventListener to allBtns element. Like this

allBtns.addEventListener('click', counter);

Now you need to select all the buttons and perform the actions according to the button.

To do that you can do this.


function counter(e) { const btn = e.target; console.log(btn)
}

The event handler function takes an argument e, it’s an object with a lot of properties.

If you call e.target you’ll get the currently clicked element which is a button in this case.

Now you can check to see the id name, and then you can add the specific function to the button.

You get an element’s id with e.target.id

Now you can compare the currently clicked button with the class names and add the actions.

function counter(e) { const btn = e.target.id; if (btn === 'increment') { value += 1; display.textContent = value; } else if (btn === 'decrement') { value -= 1; display.textContent = value; } else { value = 0; display.textContent = value; }
}

Here’s all the code after refactoring:

const display = document.querySelector('.display');
const allBtns = document.querySelector('#allBtns'); allBtns.addEventListener('click', counter); let value = 0;
function counter(e) { const btn = e.target.id; if (btn === 'increment') { value += 1; } else if (btn === 'decrement') { value -= 1; } else { value = 0; } display.textContent = value;
}

The code is much cleaner and easy to understand.

Finished project

source code

Conclusion

In this article, we built a counter app.

If this was helpful follow me on twitter @coderarmin for more articles like this.

Until then Happy Coding
item.author: coderamrin

item.title: 了解基础知识 - 什么是 Svelte？ | Learn the basics - What is Svelte?
item.description:
艾莉亚克里希纳

发表于什么是苗条？

在 Svelte 中，您基本上以特定语法编写代码，然后 Svelte 运行代码并将其转换为 Vanilla Javascript，这就是浏览器中显示的内容。 用户将只看到已编译的代码。 基本上，就像您使用 Svelte 语法提供指令代码一样，Svelte 是一个运行代码并生成高度优化的 JavaScript 代码的工具。

与 REACT Svelte 类似，都是关于在 Web 中构建用户反应式用户界面。



原文:

Arya Krishna

Posted on Apr 27

What is Svelte?

Svelte is a popular alternative to React.js, Angular and Vue and learning it will benefit any frontend web developer.

Svelte is not a framework but a compiler. In Svelte you basically write code in a specific syntax and Svelte runs the code and converts it into Vanilla Javascript and that is what is shown in the browser. The user will be seeing only the compiled code. Basically it is like you give the instructions code using Svelte syntax and Svelte is a tool that runs over the code and generates the highly optimized JavaScript code.

Similar to REACT Svelte is all about building user reactive user-interfaces in the web.
item.author: aryakris

item.title: 如何从单一颜色生成完整的 Tailwind CSS 调色板 | How to generate a full Tailwind CSS palette from a single color
item.description:
您有一个应用程序，您可以在其中允许用户选择他们的品牌颜色来设计他们的网站。 自然地，您在设计中使用不同深浅的颜色——在某些地方您使用较浅的颜色，而在其他较深的地方。

有很多颜色生成器和应用程序可以为您生成 Tailwind CSS 调色板。 以下是我的一些最爱：

问题是，您想要一个简单的 UI，而强迫用户选择所有可能的色调的想法会让您头疼。

解决方案 1.“不透明度”

在引擎盖下 Tailwind CSS 使用 CSS 变量来修改颜色的 alpha 通道（RGBA 颜色模型中的 A），这非常聪明。 请观看上面的 YouTube 视频以查看更详细的说明，但其要点是：

 .text-brand { --tw-text-opacity : 1 ; color : rgba ( 255 , 255 , 0 , --tw-text-opacity ); }

这很好，很漂亮，但是当你也想要更深的颜色时该怎么办？ 您可以从较深的颜色开始，并具有更大的范围。 你能告诉你的用户选择“更深”的颜色吗？ 不，你不能。 你不应该。

解决方案 2.“HSL”

出于这个原因，我一直在使用 HSL 颜色模型（其中 H 代表色调，S 代表饱和度，L 代表亮度）。 通过更改基色的 L 值（亮度），我能够在整个范围内获得看起来不错的调色板 - 更亮和更暗。

我编写了一个名为Tailwind CSS Color Palette Generator的 PHP 包，正是为了使用它来生成完整的调色板。

通过指定基色很容易生成调色板：

 use LukaPeharda\TailwindCssColorPaletteGenerator\Color ; use LukaPeharda\TailwindCssColorPaletteGenerator\PaletteGenerator ; // Create base color from hex $baseColor = Color :: fromHex ( '#ffff00' ); // or from RGB $baseColor = Color :: fromRgb ( 255 , 255 , 0 ); // or from HSL $baseColor = Color :: fromHsl ( 60 , 100 , 50 ); // Generate a palette $paletteGenerator = new PaletteGenerator ; $paletteGenerator -> setBaseColor ( $baseColor ); $palette = $paletteGenerator -> getPalette ();

生成的$palette将是一个数组，其中键是 Tailwind CSS 颜色步骤和值Color对象：

 $palette = [ 50 => Color , 100 => Color , 200 => Color , 300 => Color , ... ];

然后，您可以循环它以生成 CSS 变量或以任何您认为合适的方式使用它：

 foreach ( $palette as $key => $color ) { echo '--color-brand-' . $key . ': #' . $color -> getHex () . ';' ; }

在您的tailwind.config.js文件中扩展颜色设置并添加brand调色板：

 module . exports = { theme : { extend : { colors : { brand : { 50 : ' var(--color-brand-50, #F5F3FF) ' , 100 : ' var(--color-brand-100, #EDE9FE) ' , 200 : ' var(--color-brand-200, #DDD6FE) ' , 300 : ' var(--color-brand-300, #C4B5FD) ' , 400 : ' var(--color-brand-400, #A78BFA) ' , 500 : ' var(--color-brand-500, #8B5CF6) ' , 600 : ' var(--color-brand-600, #7C3AED) ' , 700 : ' var(--color-brand-700, #6D28D9) ' , 800 : ' var(--color-brand-800, #5B21B6) ' , 900 : ' var(--color-brand-900, #4C1D95) ' , } } } } }

之后，您可以将您的颜色用作常规 CSS Tailwind 类，例如text-brand-100或bg-brand-300 。

可以在此处找到微调的文档和设置。

生成的调色板不如官方 Tailwind CSS 调色板中的调色板那么好，但嘿，这些都是精心挑选的。



原文:

You have an app where you allow users to select their brand color in order to style their website. Naturally, you use different shades of the color in your design - in some places you use ligher ones, and in other darker ones.

There are a lot of color generators and app that generate Tailwind CSS color palette for you. Here are some of my favorites:

Problem is, you want to have a simple UI and the thought of forcing users to select all possible shades gives you a headache.

Solution 1. "Opacity"

Official theming video from Tailwind CSS suggests adding a single (brand) color and then modify opacity (either text opacity or background opacity) to have lighter shades of your color.

<h1 class="text-brand text-opacity-75">Howdy</h1>

Under the hood Tailwind CSS uses CSS variables to modify alpha channel of the color (A in RGBA color model) which is pretty smart. Please watch the above YouTube video to see more detailed explanation but the gist of it is this:

.text-brand { --tw-text-opacity: 1; color: rgba(255, 255, 0, --tw-text-opacity);
}

This is fine and dandy but what to do when you want to have darker colors as well? You could start with a darker color and have a greater range. Can you tell your users to select "darker" color? No. You can't. You shouldn't.

Solution 2. "HSL"

For this reason I've been playing with HSL color model (where H stands for hue, S for saturation and L for lightness). By changing the L values (lightness) of a base color I was able to get OK looking palette for the whole range - both lighter and darker.

I've written a PHP package called Tailwind CSS Color Palette Generator to use exactly this to generate a full palette.

It is easy to generate a palette by specifying your base color:

use LukaPeharda\TailwindCssColorPaletteGenerator\Color;
use LukaPeharda\TailwindCssColorPaletteGenerator\PaletteGenerator; // Create base color from hex
$baseColor = Color::fromHex('#ffff00'); // or from RGB
$baseColor = Color::fromRgb(255, 255, 0); // or from HSL
$baseColor = Color::fromHsl(60, 100, 50); // Generate a palette
$paletteGenerator = new PaletteGenerator;
$paletteGenerator->setBaseColor($baseColor);
$palette = $paletteGenerator->getPalette();

Generated $palette will be an array where keys are Tailwind CSS color steps and values Color objects:

$palette = [ 50 => Color, 100 => Color, 200 => Color, 300 => Color, ...
];

You can then loop over it to generate CSS variables or use it anyway you see fit:

foreach ($palette as $key => $color) { echo '--color-brand-' . $key . ': #' . $color->getHex() . ';';
}

Extend color settings in your tailwind.config.js file and add brand color palette:

module.exports = { theme: { extend: { colors: { brand: { 50: 'var(--color-brand-50, #F5F3FF)', 100: 'var(--color-brand-100, #EDE9FE)', 200: 'var(--color-brand-200, #DDD6FE)', 300: 'var(--color-brand-300, #C4B5FD)', 400: 'var(--color-brand-400, #A78BFA)', 500: 'var(--color-brand-500, #8B5CF6)', 600: 'var(--color-brand-600, #7C3AED)', 700: 'var(--color-brand-700, #6D28D9)', 800: 'var(--color-brand-800, #5B21B6)', 900: 'var(--color-brand-900, #4C1D95)', } } } }
}

Afterwards you can use your color as regular CSS Tailwind class, for example as text-brand-100 or bg-brand-300.

Documentation and settings for fine tunning can be found here.

Generated palettes are not as great as the ones from the official Tailwind CSS color palettes but hey, those were carefully hand picked.
