item.title: C中的链表 | Linked Lists in C
item.description:
发表于链表背后的想法是将创建节点指针移动到存储地址，将地址移动到单独声明的节点指针或节点中包含的节点指针。
理解这个过程并能够在现实生活中与之相关，可以更好地理解。

要理解链表的概念，请考虑将所有连接在一起的回形针链。

对链表执行的基本操作可以通过以下方式看到：

  1. 创建一个回形针链（或链表）是通过将每个回形针首尾相连直到您选择停止来完成的。

  2. 要在回形针链的中间添加回形针，您必须断开连接，将链的后半部分保持在某处，将新的回形针连接到链的另一半，然后连接另一半远离新连接的回形针末端的链条。

  3. 移除回形针（或节点）遵循类似的简单过程。 找到需要移除的回形针点。 从该点拆分回形针链，但不要忘记始终将链的后半部分存储在某处[如临时变量:)]。 遵循与上述相同的程序，您就可以开始了。

现在可以轻松实现上述代码。 祝你好运 ：）

尤里·卢查尼诺夫—— Apr 26 4月26日

Leom Ayọdele Aransiọla 🇳🇬♎ - Leom Ayọdele Aransiọla 🇳🇬♎ - Apr 26 4月26日



原文:

TobyMike-max

Posted on Apr 27

The idea behind linked list is about moving creating node-pointers to store addresses, moving addresses to node-pointers declared individually or node-pointers contained in a node.
Understanding the process and being able to relate with it in real life situations makes understanding better.

To understand the concept of linked lists think of a chain of paper clips all connected together.

The basic operation performed on a linked list can be seen in the following ways:

  1. Creating a chain of paper clips (or a linked list) is done by connecting each paper clip end to end until you choose to stop.

  2. To add a paper clip in the middle of a chain of paper clips you'll have to break the connection, keep the second half of the chain somewhere, connect the new paper clip to the other half of the chain, then connect the second half of the chain that was kept away to the end of the newly connected paper clip.

  3. Removing a paper clip (or a node) follows a similar simple process. Locate the point of paper clip tat needs to be removed. Split the chain of paper clips from that point but don't forget to always store the second half of the chain somewhere [like a temp variable :)]. Follow the same procedure as above and you're good to go.

Implementing the code for the above can now be easily done. Good luck :)

Yuriy Luchaninov - Apr 26

Leom Ayọdele Aransiọla 🇳🇬♎ - Apr 26
item.author: tobymikemax

item.title: #100DaysofCode - 天[2](3) | #100DaysofCode - Day[2](3)
item.description:
今天是一个有点艰难的一天。 经过漫长的一天实际进入办公室进行全天会议后，我感到非常筋疲力尽。 尤其是在昨晚比我预期的晚了之后发布了我昨天的经历。

尽管如此，我还是挺过来了。 今天我学习了逻辑运算符和 if/else 语句。 在此过程中还需要学习一些非常简洁的练习。 我做了一个闰年计算器，它是一堆 if/else 语句，用于确定年份是否能被 4 整除，除了能被 100 整除的年份，除非年份也能被 400 整除。

闰年计算器

还有这个愚蠢的爱情计算器，它根据您和您伴侣的名字中的字母数量以及“真爱”一词中出现的字母数量来计算兼容性。

爱情计算器

最后，最有趣的事情是做一个自己的文字冒险有点交易。 回想一下超级超级基础电脑游戏的时代，当时文字冒险风靡一时。 它又短又傻，但我很想有朝一日重新审视一次严肃的文字冒险的想法。

宝藏岛



原文:

Today was a bit of a rough one. After a long day of actually going into the office for an all day meeting, I was feeling pretty drained. Especially after staying up later than I expected last night to post my experience from yesterday.

Nevertheless, I got through it. 😌

Today I learned about logical operators and if/else statements. There were some pretty neat exercises to be learned along the way as well. I made a leap year calculator, which is a bunch of if/else statements determining whether or not the year is evenly divisibly by 4, except years that are evenly divisible by 100, unless the year is also evenly divisibly by 400.

leap-year-calculator

There is also this silly love calculator which calculates compatibility depending on the amount of letters in you and your partner's name and how many letters appear in the words "TRUE LOVE".

love-calculator

Finally, the most fun thing was doing a make your own text adventure kinda deal. Think back to the era of super super basic computer gaming, when text adventures were all the rage. It's short and silly, but I'd love to revisit the idea of a serious text adventure someday.

treasure-island
item.author: keatonpalmer

item.title: 在 JavaScript 中克隆数组的 6 种不同方法📑 | 6 Different Ways To Clone An Array In JavaScript 📑
item.description:
在 JavaScript 中，数组是用于存储不同元素的单个变量。 当我们想要存储值列表并通过单个变量访问它们时，通常会使用它。

在某些情况下，您可能需要在不影响原始数组的情况下通过复制它来更新数组。

在 JavaScript 中克隆一个数组

  1. 使用 Array.slice() —最快的方法
var arrayToClone = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] let clone = arrayToClone . slice ( 0 )
 var arrayToClone = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] let clone = []. concat ( arrayToClone )
 var arrayToClone = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] let clone = arrayToClone . map ( value => value )
 var arrayToClone = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] let clone = [... arrayToClone ]
  1. 使用 JSON.stringify() 和 JSON.parse()
 var arrayToClone = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] let clone = JSON . parse ( JSON . stringify ( arrayToClone ))
  1. 定义自定义 clone() 方法

您可以在 Array 的原型中创建自己的 clone() 方法，以便在需要时使用它。

 var arrayToClone = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] Array . prototype . clone = function () { return this . map ( e => Array . isArray ( e ) ? e . clone () : e ); }; // this is how to use the method let clone = arrayToClone . clone () console . log ( clone )

如果您喜欢这篇文章，请与您的朋友和同事分享！

保持联系，

  • 在推特上关注我
  • 看看我的开源项目

詹姆斯'但丁'米兹 - Apr 21 4月21日



原文:

In JavaScript, an array is a single variable that is used to store different elements. It is often used when we want to store a list of values and access them by a single variable.

In some cases, you might need to update an array without affecting the original one by making a copy of it.

Cloning an Array in JavaScript

  1. Using Array.slice() — the fastest way 
var arrayToClone = [1, 2, 3]
let clone = arrayToClone.slice(0)

var arrayToClone = [1, 2, 3]
let clone = [].concat(arrayToClone)

var arrayToClone = [1, 2, 3]
let clone = arrayToClone.map(value => value)

var arrayToClone = [1, 2, 3]
let clone = [...arrayToClone]
  1. Using JSON.stringify() and JSON.parse() 
var arrayToClone = [1, 2, 3]
let clone = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(arrayToClone))
  1. Defining a custom clone() method

You can create your own clone() method in the prototype of your Array to use it whenever you need it.

 var arrayToClone = [1, 2, 3] Array.prototype.clone = function() { return this.map(e => Array.isArray(e) ? e.clone() : e); }; // this is how to use the method let clone = arrayToClone.clone() console.log(clone)

If you enjoyed this article, share it with your friends and colleagues!

Keep in touch,

  • Follow me on Twitter
  • Take a look at my open-source projects

James 'Dante' Midzi - Apr 21
item.author: 6km

item.title: 使用 KotlinJS 和 NPM 创建 Discord Bot - Discord Bot 系列（第 2 部分） | Using KotlinJS and NPM to create a Discord Bot - Discord Bot Series (Part 2)
item.description:
机器人可以响应消息、根据命令执行操作，甚至可以在语音聊天中添加音乐。

在本系列中，我们将介绍如何在 KotlinJS 项目中使用 DiscordJS 的 NPM 模块创建一个小型示例机器人。

注意：这是三篇系列文章中的第二篇，关于如何使用 KotlinJS 和 NPM 模块来创建一个完整的项目，在本例中是一个 Discord Bot。 你可以在这里找到第一篇文章。

之前

在我的上一篇文章中，我们讨论了如何将节点模块导入到我们的 KotlinJS 项目中，并创建了一个简单的项目来测试我们的导入。 在这篇文章中，我们将讨论如何将 DiscordJS 添加到我们的项目中，并开始创建我们的机器人！ 让我们开始吧。

作为复习，在本系列中，我们将创建一个小型 Discord 机器人，它使用其计算机名称响应特定消息。

注册 Discord 机器人

在深入研究代码之前，请确保您注册了一个 bot 。 选择New Application ，添加名称并单击create 。

您可以在此处找到有关此过程的更多文档。

创建机器人后，您应该会看到此屏幕。

单击左侧的 Bot 选项卡，然后在Build-a-bot部分中单击add bot 。 完成此操作后，您将创建一个机器人！ 请务必保留此屏幕中的令牌以在项目中使用。

还请务必使用本指南将您的机器人注册到您的频道。

初始化 DiscordJS

对于我们的 KotlinJS 项目，我们将使用DiscordJs ，它是一个有据可查且广泛使用的不和谐节点模块。

因此，首先我们将像以前一样将依赖项添加到build.gradle文件中：

 dependencies { implementation(npm("discord.js", "13.6.0")) }

然后我们可以参考创建主文件文档来了解如何启动机器人。 我们可以看到客户端被初始化，然后它监听消息，最后调用登录。

这是在 Javascript 中，但 kotlin 实现看起来基本相同。 首先，我们需要定义Client类。 因此，我们将转到client 的文档，看看我们需要定义什么。 我们可以看到：

  • 客户端接受ClientOptions来初始化。
  • 客户端login函数接受一个字符串令牌并返回一个字符串承诺。

在里面

在我们定义ClientOptions并可能进入兔子洞之前，我们可以看到它被定义为一个对象。 在这种情况下，我们可以只在构造函数中传递一个 Json 对象。

 @JsModule ( "discord.js" ) external class Client ( config : Json ?) const val GUILDS = 1 // 1 << 0 val client = Client ( json ( Pair ( "intents" , arrayOf ( GUILDS ))))

客户端接受“intents”，它是一个仅Intents.FLAGS.GUILDS的数组。 如果我们按照 DiscordJS 的文档了解意图，我们可以在此处找到它们的定义。 我们看到Intents.FLAGS.GUILDS是 1 << 0（或 1），所以我们将通过它。

一次

这个函数我们可以通过简单的调用来弄清楚。 这只是为了让我们知道客户已准备好，因此我们将打印出确认信息。

 @JsModule ( "discord.js" ) external class Client ( config : Json ?) { fun once ( event : String , cb : ( Unit ) -> Unit ) } client . once ( "ready" ) { println ( "Hello From ${computerName()}!" ) }

我们可以看出它接受一个字符串，并返回一个块。 由于没有输入或输出，我们可以只使用Unit ，它充当void 。

登录

根据我们学到的知识，我们可以很容易地定义它。 我们将传入的令牌来自您在 discord 网站上创建的机器人。 这是从本文开头抢到的令牌。

 @JsModule ( "discord.js" ) external class Client ( config : Json ?) { //... fun login ( token : String ): Promise < String > } //Main.kt client . login ( "YOUR_TOKEN" )

在这种情况下，我们可以告诉该函数从 DiscordJS 文档中返回一个 Promise。

运行你的机器人

此时所有的调用都应该在，你的Main.kt应该是这样的：

 const val GUILDS = 1 // 1 << 0 val client = Client ( json ( Pair ( "intents" , arrayOf ( GUILDS )))) client . once ( "ready" ) { println ( "Hello From ${computerName()}!" ) } client . login ( token )

如果您运行它，您应该能够在终端中看到您的计算机名称，并且您的机器人应该在线！

回复消息

机器人正在运行，这很好，但是现在它什么也没做。 因此，让我们让机器人响应消息。

注：意图

为了让机器人获取消息，客户端需要注册另一个意图GUILDS_MESSAGES 。

 const val GUILDS = 1 // 1 << 0 const val GUILDS_MESSAGES = 512 //1 << 9 val client = Client(json(Pair("intents", arrayOf(GUILDS, GUILDS_MESSAGES))))

定义消息

因此，首先我们将快速定义一条消息，因为这是传入的内容。从文档中我们可以看到一条消息包含一个content字符串、 author和发送它的channel 。我们将使用channel发送回复并使用author创建个性化消息。 为了让事情变得更快，我从以下文档中定义了我们需要的内容：



原文:

Discord is a great platform for keeping in touch with friends, and adding a bot can enrich that experience. A bot can respond to messages, perform an action based on a command, or even add music to a voice chat.

In this series we'll be going over how to create a small example bot by using the NPM module for DiscordJS in a KotlinJS project.

Note: This is the second post in a series of three posts, about how to use KotlinJS and NPM modules to create a full fledged project, in this case a Discord Bot. You can find the first post here.

Previously

In my last post we went over how to import node modules into our KotlinJS project, and created a simple project to test our imports. In this post we'll go over how to add DiscordJS to our project, and start creating our bot! Let's get started.

As a refresher, in this series we're going to create a small discord bot that responds to a specific message with its computer name.

Registering a Discord Bot

Before digging into code make sure you register a bot. Choose New Application, add a name and click create.

You can find more documentation on this process here.

After you create your bot you should see this screen.

Click on the Bot tab on the left and in the Build-a-bot section click add bot. Once you've done this you'll have created a bot! Be sure to keep the token from this screen for use in the project.

Also be sure to register your bot to your Channel using this guide.

Initializing DiscordJS

For our KotlinJS project we'll be using DiscordJs, which is a well documented and widely used node module for discord.

So first we'll add the dependency to the build.gradle file like before:

dependencies {
    implementation(npm("discord.js", "13.6.0"))
}

Then we can reference the Creating the main file documents to find out how to launch the bot. We can see that the client is initialized, then it listens for messages, then finally calls to login.

This is in Javascript but the kotlin implementation will look mostly the same. First we need to define the Client class. So we'll go to the documentation for the client, and see what we need to define. We can see that:

  • Client takes in ClientOptions to init.
  • Client inherits from EventEmitter, which is how once is called
  • Client login function takes in a string token and returns a string promise.

Init

Before we go defining ClientOptions and potentially going down the rabbit hole, we can see that it's defined as an object. In this case we can just pass a Json object in the constructor.

@JsModule("discord.js")
external class Client(config: Json?) const val GUILDS = 1 // 1 << 0
val client = Client(json(Pair("intents", arrayOf(GUILDS))))

The client takes in "intents", which is an array of just Intents.FLAGS.GUILDS. If we follow the documentation from DiscordJS to the intents we can find their definition here. We see that Intents.FLAGS.GUILDS is 1 << 0 (or 1) so we'll pass that.

Once

This function we can figure out by the simple call. This is just to let us know the client is ready, so we'll just print out a confirmation.

@JsModule("discord.js")
external class Client(config: Json?) { fun once(event: String, cb: (Unit) -> Unit)
} client.once("ready") { println("Hello From ${computerName()}!")
}

We can tell that it takes in a string, and returns a block. Since there's no input or output we can just use Unit, which acts as void.

Login

Based on what we learned we can define this easily. The token we will pass in comes from your created bot on the discord site. This was the token that was grabbed from the beginning of this post.

@JsModule("discord.js")
external class Client(config: Json?) { //... fun login(token: String): Promise<String>
} //Main.kt
client.login("YOUR_TOKEN")

In this case we can tell the function returns a Promise from the DiscordJS documentation.

Running your bot

At this point all the calls should be in, your Main.kt should look something like this:

const val GUILDS = 1 // 1 << 0
val client = Client(json(Pair("intents", arrayOf(GUILDS))))
client.once("ready") { println("Hello From ${computerName()}!")
}
client.login(token)

If you run it you should be able to see your computer name in the terminal and your bot should be online!

Responding to Messages

This is great that the bot is running, however right now it's not doing anything. So let's get the bot to respond to a message.

Note: intent

In order for the bot to get messages, the client needs to register another intent, GUILDS_MESSAGES.

const val GUILDS = 1 // 1 << 0
const val GUILDS_MESSAGES = 512  //1 << 9
val client = Client(json(Pair("intents", arrayOf(GUILDS, GUILDS_MESSAGES))))

Defining Messages

So first we'll quickly define a message, as that's what's passed in. From the docs we can see a message contains a content string, the author, and the channel it was sent in. we're going to use the channel to send the reply to, and use the author to create a personalized message. To make things quicker I've defined what we need from the documentation below:
item.author: kevinschildhorn

item.title: 灭霸 | Git Thanos
item.description:
特别是对于像我这样的编码新手。 但是变得更好的方法，不是双关语，就是熟悉它。 所以让我们从 git 是什么开始。 这是一个跟踪repos变化的软件。 是的，repo 是存储库的缩写。 一个 repo 由快照组成，每个快照称为一个提交。 我在我的 github 帐户中创建了一个名为“getThanos”的存储库。 然后我通过键入git clone nameOfTheRepoGoesHere将它克隆到我的本地计算机。 该 repo 带有名为“main”的第一个提交。

我开始处理我的文件 getThanos.js。 我的第一个编辑是收集所有的复仇者。 我喜欢这个编辑，所以我通过输入git add getThanos.js将它添加到我的暂存区。 暂存区域是您在再次提交之前放置编辑的地方。 暂存区通知 git 文件发生了一些更改。

所以现在复仇者有一个得到灭霸的计划。 但这是有风险和危险的，所以我现在想安全并承诺。 我通过输入git commit -m 'gathered avengers and now have a plan'来做到这一点。 在这种情况下，该提交让复仇者计划获得灭霸。

现在在继续计划之前，我想制作一些版本来尝试我的计划，这样如果出现可怕的错误，我可以回去。 好吧，在 git 中，它们有分支，您可以在其中进行编辑，并且不会影响主分支。 所以我通过输入git branch sendOutSpiderman来做到这一点。

我创建了一个分支，但我仍在“主”分支上。 要对“sendOutSpiderman”分支进行编辑，我必须使用命令git checkout sendOutSpiderman来检查它。 所以现在我继续这个计划。 但是蜘蛛侠遇到了问题，因为他没有被麻省理工学院录取，所以他没有集中注意力。 好的，所以这个计划行不通，让我们输入git checkout main回到主分支。 我们还有另一个派出绿巨人的计划。 所以我们创建了另一个分支， git checkout -b sendOutHulk 。 将 'checkout' 与 '-b' 结合使用是创建分支并一步将其签出的快捷方式。

现在我们继续讨论绿巨人。 他无法得到灭霸，但这次编辑有一些好处，蜘蛛侠在这里被麻省理工学院录取。 所以我想保存这个编辑并将它带入我的主分支。 为了合并分支，git 有一个合并命令。 我保存这些编辑并提交它们， git add getThanos.js然后git commit -m 'spiderman got into MIT' 。 现在我已经准备好合并它们了，所以我用git checkout main签出我的主分支，然后与git merge sendOutHulk 。

现在让我们快进到最后，你知道铁人在哪里打响指。 假设我对此感到非常伤心，并想在那之前回去。 一个被重置，另一个被还原。 重置会将我当前的主分支移回提交。 他们说这就像“改写历史”。 但是当与其他人共享一个分支时，重置可能会混淆其他用户。 这个新的提交不会有我不想要的提交中所做的编辑。 所以在钢铁侠的情况下，假设我在那之前的承诺是星爵因为灭霸杀死卡魔拉而对灭霸发火。 现在我可以重写结局了。 这样一来，我的铁人打响指仍然在我的历史中，我可以将它用于我的花絮。



原文:

Git can be pretty scary. Especially for a person that’s new to coding like me. But the way to get better , no pun intended, is to get familiar with it. So let’s start off with what git is. It's a software that tracks changes in repos. And yes, a repo is short for repository. A repo consists of snapshots, each snapshot is called a commit. I create a repo called ‘getThanos’ in my github account. Then I clone it to my local computer by typing git clone nameOfTheRepoGoesHere. The repo comes with its first commit named ‘main’.

Git add
I start working on my file getThanos.js. My first edit is to gather all the avengers. I like this edit so I add it to my staging area by typing git add getThanos.js. The staging area is where you place your edits before making another commit of them. The staging area notifies git that there’s been some changes to the file.

Git commit
So now the avengers have a plan to get thanos. But this is risky and dangerous, so I wanna be safe and commit now. I do this by typing git commit -m 'gathered avengers and now have a plan'. The -m is for adding a message to my commit so that I know what edits that commit has. In this case, that commit has the avengers plan to get Thanos.

Git branch
Now before going forward with the plan, I wanna make some sort of version to try out my plan, so that if something goes horribly wrong, I can go back. Well in git, they have branches, where you can do edits and it won’t affect the main branch. So I do this by typing git branch sendOutSpiderman.

Git checkout
I created a branch, but I’m still on the ‘main’ branch. To do edits on the ‘sendOutSpiderman’ branch, I have to check it out with the command git checkout sendOutSpiderman. So now I proceed with the plan. But spiderman runs into problems, he’s not focused since he wasn’t accepted into MIT. Okay so this plan isn’t gonna work, let’s go back to the main branch by typing git checkout main. We have another plan to send out the Hulk. So we create another branch, git checkout -b sendOutHulk. Combining ‘checkout’ with ‘-b’ is a shortcut to creating a branch and checking it out in one step.

Git merge
Now we proceed with the Hulk. He couldn’t get Thanos, but there is some good in this edit, spiderman got accepted to MIT here. So I wanna save this edit and bring it into my main branch. To combine branches, git has a merge command. I save these edits and commit them, git add getThanos.js then git commit -m 'spiderman got into MIT'. Now I’m ready to combine them, so I checkout my main branch with git checkout main then combine with git merge sendOutHulk.

Git revert / git reset
Now let’s fast forward to the end, you know where Ironman snaps his fingers. Let’s say I’m too heartbroken by this and want to go back right before that happened. There’s two ways git allows this. One is reset and the other is revert. Reset will move my current branch main back a commit. Which they say is like “rewriting history”. But when sharing a branch with others, reset can confuse other users. Revert will move the current branch to a new commit. This new commit won’t have the edits made in the commit I didn’t want. So in the situation with Ironman, let’s say my commit before that was when Star-Lord got mad at Thanos for killing Gamora. Revert will make a new commit after my current commit with Ironman snapping his fingers, but this new commit will be a copy of the Star-Lord commit. And now I can rewrite the ending. In this way, my Ironman snapping his fingers is still in my history, and I can use it for my bloopers.
item.author: jimenezfede

item.title: 大家好！ | Hello everyone!
item.description:
莫希古尔

发表于嗨，我是Mohigul ，我是新来的）我拥有生物科学学士学位。 目前我是生物技术领域的硕士生。

那么，问题来了：理科学生在这里做什么？ 嗯，我从小就对电脑很感兴趣。 现在我正在学习 IT。 我希望它对我未来的职业有所帮助，因为生物信息学正在与生物学的所有领域融为一体。

我将学习编程基础知识，还将分享我在这里学到的所有知识。

所以，我们走吧！



原文:

Mokhigul

Posted on Apr 27

Hi, I'm Mohigul and I'm new here) I have got my B.S. in Biological Sciences. Currently I'm a M.S. student in the field of Biotechnology.

So, then there is question: What is a science student doing here?. Well, I'm quite interested in computers from my childhood. And now I'm learning IT. I hope it's gonna help my future career because bioinformatics is mixing with all the field of biology.

I will be learning basics of programming and also I will be sharing all what I learn here.

So, let's go!
item.author: mokhigul99

item.title: [Part 1] NextJS + Golang Service in Kubernetes - 设置 | [Part 1] NextJS + Golang Service in Kubernetes - Setup
item.description:
对于刚开始使用它的人来说，有时将 Web 应用程序部署到 Kubernetes 可能并不那么简单。 在本系列中，我们将在实时环境中部署 Kubernetes 集群（是的！），让您在 minikube 和 localhost 之外获得 Kubernetes 的真实体验 - 希望在本系列结束时您能够将生成的项目用作您未来项目的基础。

我们将使用可能会产生一些成本的云提供商，而选择哪个提供商的决定主要是由于其设置成本 - 所有会产生成本的组件都将使用 Terraform 进行配置，以便您可以轻松地实例化和销毁它们时间你想测试一些东西——尽可能降低“实验”的成本。

我正在为具有一定经验的软件开发人员设计这个系列，但可能尚未将其引入 DevOps 的世界。

技术和概念

在我们开始之前，我想稍微思考一下我们将在这个项目中使用的技术、工具和提供商。

  • 除此之外，它是一种快速、低内存占用、类型安全的语言，可让您拥有易于预测的良好构建软件，并在软件开发中融入现代最佳实践。

来自 PHP 开发世界，我总是发现部署带有大量网络警告和组件（如 Web 服务器、代理等）的应用程序很麻烦——直到几年前我开始迁移到 Golang。 与 PHP 内置 Web 服务器不同——强烈建议生产 PHP 应用程序使用 Apache 或 NGINX 等外部 Web 服务器的原因——Golang 内置 Web 服务器快速、可靠且可用于生产，无需外部配置文件或类似的东西让你启动和运行。

  • 在 JavaScript 框架的游戏中，有一些很突出——但在大多数列表的顶部，你总是会读到 Next.js 这个名字。 增量静态再生 (ISR)：前端部署中最重要的事情之一是页面的速度以及它的视口初始渲染的优化程度，以及其他因素——大多数人出于这个原因选择构建静态网站。 但是静态网站通常存在这样的问题，即每当您想显示新页面（例如新的博客文章）时，您都必须重建整个网站并再次部署。 使用 ISR，您可以轻松定义重新验证周期，以便您的前端知道何时检查是否有“静态生成”的新内容并自动提供，而无需重新部署/重建您的应用程序。

  • 内置图像优化组件：您经常会发现自己试图在图像大小和质量之间取得适当的平衡，并为您的图像找到一个延迟加载方案/库。 这对于开发工作流程来说可能非常烦人和破坏性。 对于这种情况，Next.js 提供了一个<Image>组件，该组件可以自动优化以.webp格式显示的图像 - 与.jpeg相比，这通常已经有助于图像尺寸减小 30%，并且图像会自动延迟-加载。

  • 在容器编排方面，Kubernetes 是市场标准/领导者 - 其声明式/命令式方法来管理对象。 但它也提供了一些通常未被提及的其他好处：

    • 扩展和自我监控：您可以轻松地水平和垂直扩展您的资源——在大多数情况下，您可以定义您可以为pod或节点等资源分配的最小和最大资源量。 除此之外，如果你的一个资源，比如说一个 pod - 崩溃，它会立即（或在几秒钟内）产生一个新的来替换它。
    • 对云资源的透明控制： AWS EKS等服务检测何时在您的 Kubernetes 清单中指定了特定的外部资源，它会“自动”在您的 Kubernetes 集群之外生成该资源 - 一个示例是 LoadBalancer 类型。 你可以在这里阅读更多关于它的信息。

    • 当然，在您的项目中使用 Kubernetes 还有许多其他好处。 我个人喜欢将它们带入我的个人项目中，因为一旦你有一个符合你的架构偏好/愿望的清单/Helm 图表。 它只是应用它，您拥有运行应用程序的所有基础设施。 如果您是 Kubernetes 的初学者，我强烈建议您了解它的概念。

    基础架构即代码 (IaC) 是您使基础架构可重现且易于管理的关键。 如今，任何具有某种配置或指令的项目都需要我通过面板并为我通常查找的项目手动执行某些操作，如果该服务提供了 Terraform Provider。 您可以找到范围广泛的提供商——从NewRelic 、 Auth0到更标准的提供商，如AWS 、 GCP和Scaleway 。 它将允许我们为这个项目创建基础设施并使用 2 个简单的命令将其销毁 - 确保我们的成本不会失控，并且一旦完成，我们就会移除所有资源。
    对于这个特定的项目，我们将使用 Scaleway 提供程序，也许还有 NewRelic（我们必须边看边看😄）。

    • 云提供商如上所述——我们将使用Scaleway Elements来配置我们的 Kubernetes 集群，但到目前为止我没有告诉你的是，我们还将利用AWS来配置我们的无服务器数据库。 所以，是的，我们的解决方案将是多云的，因为我很便宜，我们将使用他们提供的最小实例/配置 - 但非常欢迎您使用您想要的 - 自费😂
      • 但尽管如此，Scaleway 是一项非常优秀且可靠的服务，与大多数云提供商不同，它为您提供了一个真正透明且可预测的定价结构和专为测试/实验而构建的实例。 它的一个缺点是它们没有 AWS 那样广泛的服务——所以，如果你的解决方案需要特定的东西，比如 Kafka 集群——我宁愿建议你查看另一个云提供商（即 AWS EKS 来访问AWS MSK）。 您可以手动尝试不同的实例大小，以查看每年/每月的成本等等。
      • 概述

        在这张图片中，您可以更好地了解我们将要构建的内容 - 我省略了一些组件，例如网络配置、证书 (cert-manager) 和我们的 NewRelic 侧推车。

        我们将为这个系列创建的所有代码都将在这个存储库中可用 - 您可以通过查看与帖子部分相关的 git 标签来浏览系列进度（即，帖子部分 1 = 标签 1.0. 0，帖子第 2 部分 = 标签 2.0.0)。 所以事不宜迟 - 让我们动手吧😎。

        设置

        项目

        您可以创建以下文件夹结构：

        地形

        我们需要的第一件事实际上是安装 Terraform - 它通常非常简单。 您可以访问Terraform 网站并安装特定版本，也可以安装tfswitch ，它允许您从终端轻松安装和切换 Terraform 版本。

        除此之外，您还可以安装VS Code Extension或JetBrains Plugin来为您提供语法高亮。

        出于本系列的目的 - 我们将使用 Terraform 1.1.9。 因此，完成安装后，您应该可以执行以下命令：

         terraform version ## Terraform v1.1.9 ## on linux_amd64

        现在让我们创建我们的主要 Terraform 文件 - 该文件将用于配置我们的云提供商，以便我们可以实际创建项目所需的资源。 从项目结构的根目录运行命令。

         touch .infrastructure/main.tf

        现在让我们使用AWS和Scaleway提供程序配置填充此文件 - 您可以在各自的页面中找到有关提供程序及其所有文档的更多详细信息。

        让我们首先从 AWS 提供商开始：

         terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } } } provider "aws" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps }

        然后添加 Scaleway Provider：

         terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } scaleway = { source = "scaleway/scaleway" version = "2.2.1-rc.3" } } } provider "scaleway" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps } provider "aws" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps }

        现在您可以进入.infrastructure文件夹并执行以下命令 - 这将下载提供程序。

         cd .infrastructure terraform init

        之后，您会注意到创建了一个新文件夹.terraform和一个.terraform.lock.hcl 。 我们不需要对这 2 个文件进行版本控制 - 所以让我们创建一个.gitignore

         echo ".terraform" >> .gitignore echo ".terraform.lock.hcl" >> .gitignore

        在工具方面，我们设置在这里。 我们仍然需要使用我们的凭据配置提供程序，并且要检索这些凭据，我们需要在控制台上为每个提供程序进行挖掘。

        如果您已经拥有 AWS 账户的凭证（访问令牌和机密），则可以完全跳过此部分 - 确保您的用户具有下述策略非常重要。

        让我们先创建您的帐户 - 访问AWS 网站。 然后点击顶部栏上的“完成注册”

        完成整个流程（您可能需要输入付款详情）。

        在该过程结束时，您应该进入以下屏幕 - 不要太习惯它：

        作为 AWS 账户的第一步 - 我强烈建议您设置预算警报和多因素身份验证 - 如果您已经这样做了，您可以跳到设置用户凭证。 您刚刚创建的帐户称为“root”帐户，默认情况下具有管理员权限。

        转到右上角菜单上的“计费仪表板”：

        然后在右侧菜单上单击“预算”，然后单击“创建预算”。 您将看到以下向导：

        我推荐的选项是：

        • 预算类型：成本预算
        • 预算名称：个人预算
        • 期间：每月
        • 预算更新类型：经常性预算
        • 开始月份：（当前月份）
        • 预算方法：固定
        • 输入您的预算金额（美元）：（个人喜好 - 我通常每月投入 20 美元）
        • 预算范围：所有 AWS 服务（推荐）

        然后根据您定义的预算定义对您有意义的警报。 明确地说，定义预算并不意味着 AWS 会向您收取预算金额，也不会阻止您超出预算 - 它只是让您可以更好地监控账户中的成本。

        多重身份验证

        如果您在注册时没有设置多重身份验证，您可以通过访问 AWS 控制台左上角的“安全凭证”进行设置：

        然后，您将被发送到可以分配 MFA 设备的页面：

        从那里它几乎遵循向导 - 使用您喜欢的 MFA 设备（即 Google Authenticator）扫描 QR 码，之后您将退出它。

        设置 AWS 用户凭证

        我们将为这个项目创建一个角色和一个特定的用户 - 这是一个很好的做法，因为它允许您在权限和安全性方面对您正在运行的所有项目进行细粒度控制。

        从 AWS 控制台 - 转到 IAM 页面：

        单击“用户”，然后单击“创建用户”，然后为您的用户输入任何名称（即乙烯基目录用户）并选择“访问密钥 - 编程访问”并点击下一步。

        在下一页中选择“直接附加现有策略”并搜索并选择以下策略：

        • 这是非常广泛的政策，现在没关系 - 我们不确切知道我们需要配置什么，我们可以稍后调整到更精细的级别控制，并可能添加一些其他的。 现在，这就是我们可能需要的全部内容 - 所以您可以点击“下一步”，如果需要，请填写一些“标签”，以便稍后更好地参考新创建的用户 - 再次点击“下一步”，您将结束在以下屏幕中：

          单击“创建用户”，然后您应该看到以下屏幕：

          复制访问密钥和秘密密钥并粘贴到您选择的编辑器中，并将其保存在文本文件中（临时）。 我们会回到它。

          让我们确保我们的访问密钥和秘密密钥不是在我们的笔记本电脑中以纯文本形式出现 - 为了帮助我们，我们将使用AWS Vault - 这是一个帮助我们以安全方式管理 AWS 配置文件和凭证的工具 - 有一些漂亮解决方案通过 CLI 等进行密钥轮换。但在我们这样做之前，我们需要安装 AWS CLI（如果您还没有的话）：

          1. 安装 AWS CLI - 访问CLI 网站，您将在右侧看到为您的操作系统安装的链接。

          1. 安装 AWS Vault - 访问Github 存储库并按照自述文件中的安装说明进行操作。

          完成后 - 您应该能够执行以下命令：

          现在让我们实际添加我们的配置文件 - 并复制/粘贴您在上一步中保存在临时文件中的访问密钥和秘密密钥：

           aws-vault add vinyl-catalog Enter Access Key ID: [ XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX] Enter Secret Access Key: [ XXXXXXXXXXXXXX] Added credentials to profile "vinyl-catalog" in vault

          在这一步之后——确保你删除了你的临时文件——以确认一切都与我们的用户访问运行有关：

           aws-vault exec vinyl-catalog -- aws rds describe-db-instances

          这个命令应该列出我们所有的数据库（此时 - 我们没有）。

          现在，让我们回到我们的 Terraform 文件 - [main.tf](http://main.tf) - 我们将指定最近创建的配置文件和更适合您的区域 - 在我的例子中是法兰克福（eu-central -1)。 所以你的main.tf现在应该是这样的：

           terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } scaleway = { source = "scaleway/scaleway" version = "2.2.1-rc.3" } } } provider "scaleway" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps } provider "aws" { profile = "vinyl-catalog" region = "eu-central-1" }

          如果您已经拥有 Scaleway Elements 帐户的凭据（访问令牌和机密），则可以完全跳过此部分 - 确保您的用户具有下述策略非常重要。

          让我们先创建您的帐户 - 访问Scaleway 网站。 并完成注册过程 - 当出现帐户类型选项时 - 我建议您选择“构建”选项 - 它会要求您输入帐单信息并验证您的身份 - 这是一个有点冗长的过程，身份检查可能花几分钟/几小时 - 但到最后你应该能够到达主页：

          我们要做的第一件事是创建一个新项目——点击顶部的下拉菜单，然后点击“创建项目”：

          填写项目名称：

          现在就是这样 - 让我们确保我们的帐户设置正确，这样我们就不会对它的计费和安全性产生任何重大意外。

          点击右上角的菜单按钮，选择“结算”：

          就像 AWS 预算警报一样 - 为自己设置一个您认为对您来说足够好的预算，并且不要忘记添加警报触发器：

          这样 - 如果你超出预算，至少你知道你可以采取行动。

          设置 Scaleway 用户凭据

          现在我们将创建凭据，以使我们能够通过 Terraform 通过 Scaleway API 进行通信。 为此——确保你在我们的项目中——在我的例子中，我将它命名为“vinyl-catalog”：

          单击右上角的菜单按钮，然后单击“凭据”：

          您应该看到以下屏幕：

          点击“生成新的 API 密钥”——你会被问到目的——你可以在这里放任何东西——我会指定“Terraform”：

          将 Access Key 和 Secret Key 复制到一个临时文本文件中 - 我们将回到它。

          与 AWS 不同 - Scaleway 目前没有围绕其生态系统的大量工具 - 也没有可与 AWS Vault 相媲美的工具。 出于这个原因，我们将采用 Scaleway 推荐的最安全的方法——利用 Scaleway CLI 创建的共享配置。

          首先，您可以按照此Github 存储库中的安装步骤安装 Scaleway CLI。 最后，您应该能够在终端中执行以下命令：

           scw version #### BuildDate unknown #### GoVersion go1.17.2 #### GitBranch unknown #### GitCommit unknown #### GoArch amd64 #### GoOS linux

          现在让我们使用我们的凭据初始化 CLI - 利用上一步中显示的密钥（记得删除临时文件）。 如果您已经有可用的 SSH 密钥，您可能会被要求将其链接到您的帐户 - 您应该接受！ 以后会很方便的。 如果您不知道如何创建 SSH 密钥 - 请遵循本教程。 对于这个特定的系列，我们将使用“vinyl-catalog”配置文件 - 但将来，如果您希望拥有不同的用户或凭据，您可以在$HOME/.config/scw/config.yaml (Unix) / %USERPROFILE%/.config/scw/config.yaml ****(Windows)。

          作为最后一步 - 让我们将新创建的配置文件添加到我们的 Terraform 文件 ( main.tf ) - 您还可以选择对您更有意义的区域/区域。 在设置结束时，文件应如下所示：

           terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } scaleway = { source = "scaleway/scaleway" version = "2.2.1-rc.3" } } } provider "scaleway" { profile = "vinyl-catalog" zone = "fr-par-1" region = "fr-par" } provider "aws" { profile = "vinyl-catalog" region = "eu-central-1" }

          后端服务

          完成所有云和凭据设置并完成（现在）我们现在可以开始查看代码🚀！ 所以 - 如前所述，我们将构建一个 Golang 服务 - 在这个阶段，它的意图非常基本：在localhost:8080/up中接收请求并返回一个简单的状态 200 任何内容。

          您可以利用Golang 扩展来开发服务 VS Code，或者像我一样利用JetBrains GoLand ，它是 Golang 特定的 IDE。 在我们开始创建文件并开始疯狂地进行一些 Go 开发之前 - 让我们安装 GoLang - 我发现的最简单和最简单的方法是使用gvm - 你可以找到它作为 JavaScript nvm但用于 GoLang。

          所以你可以安装它，首先 - 根据你的操作系统在这里检查要求/依赖项，在你安装了它的要求/依赖项之后运行以下命令：

           ### Download the GVM installer (Bash) bash < < ( curl -s -S -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/moovweb/gvm/master/binscripts/gvm-installer ) ### Or Download the GVM installer (zsh) zsh < < ( curl -s -S -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/moovweb/gvm/master/binscripts/gvm-installer )

          最后，您应该能够运行以下命令来验证您的安装：

           gvm version ### Go Version Manager v1.0.22 installed at /home/thiago/.gvm

          之后，您可以安装 Golang 1.18 版：

           gvm install go1.18 ### In some OS the command above might fail (ie. Ubuntu 20.04), ### in that case you can can try downloading the binary with the -B flag gvm install go1.18 -B

          并开始使用新安装的版本并验证您的安装运行：

           ### start using and set the Go 1.18 as the default version gvm use go1.18 --default ### Now using version go1.18 go version ### go version go1.18 linux/amd64

          在services/api文件夹中打开一个终端并运行以下命令。 将字符串替换为您的 Github 用户名。 重要的是要注意这个存储库不一定存在，如果您愿意，您可以将您的代码托管在不同的存储库中 - 只有当您希望最终将您的应用程序作为一个包分发给其他人使用时，这才会发挥作用。 该命令将创建一个 go.mod 文件（Go Modules 文件） - 稍后将用于我们管理我们的 Golang 包依赖项。

           go mod init github.com/ { YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME } /vinyl-catalog ### ie. go mod init github.com/thiduzz/vinyl-catalog

          在services/api/cmd/vinyl_catalog/文件夹中创建一个main.go文件。 该文件将是我们后端应用程序的入口点。 让我们的应用程序（ main.go ）打印出一些东西，看看我们的本地 Golang 设置是否正常工作。

           // Main package package main //import a package fmt that enable us to print things to the stdout (console) import "fmt" // Entry function of the application func main () { fmt . Println ( "Hey! My name is Thiago" ) }

          然后从services/api目录运行以下命令：

           go run cmd/vinyl_catalog/main.go ### Hey! My name is Thiago

          很酷，如果您在控制台中看到 Print，则表示设置正常，我们可以继续开始定义基本的 Golang Web 服务器。 对我们来说幸运的是，Golang 有一个我们将在本系列中使用的，你甚至可以将它用于生产目的。

          因此，让我们从调整我们的 main 函数和导入开始：

           import ( "log" "net/http" ) func main () { log . Println ( "Starting server at port 8080" ) if err := http . ListenAndServe ( ":8080" , nil ); err != nil { log . Fatal ( err ) } }

          如果我们再次运行应用程序（ go run cmd/vinyl_catalog/main.go ），我们将得到类似下面的日志并且终端将保持（退出你可以在终端中执行 CTRL+C） - 发生的事情基本上是我们的Web 服务器正在侦听连接，但没有为我们的路由创建处理程序 - 您甚至可以在浏览器中访问[localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080) （但您只会得到 404）：

           2022 / 04 / 23 12 : 03 : 35 Starting server at port 8080

          所以让我们添加一个映射到/up路由的 handleFunc - 现在我们将只使用 DefaultServeMux 这个简单的设置，但我们将逐步优化它，以便在我们有更多路由时更容易管理并更好地处理身份验证，依赖项、验证和 SSL：

           func main () { log . Println ( "Starting server at port 8080" ) // Adds a HandleFunc to the DefaultMux http . HandleFunc ( "/up" , func ( w http . ResponseWriter , r * http . Request ) { // Utilize fmt.Fprintf that accepts a io.Writer // and write the content of the page fmt . Fprintf ( w , "God save the Queen!" ) }) if err := http . ListenAndServe ( ":8080" , nil ); err != nil { log . Fatal ( err ) } }

          如果您想了解更多有关 handleFunc、Handlers 和 DefaultServeMux 的信息，我建议您阅读这篇文章。

          只是为了验证是否一切正常运行应用程序 ( go run cmd/vinyl_catalog/main.go ) 并从浏览器访问[localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080) - 你应该看到以下内容：

          前端服务脚手架

          我们几乎完成了项目的基本设置 - 最后剩下的部分是最简单的部分，因为有大量关于如何做到这一点的文档。 我们将快速创建一个 Next.JS 应用程序，最终将允许我们与 Golang 后端进行交互。

          我假设您现在已经安装了 NodeJS 和 Yarn - 如果没有，请按照以下链接中的说明进行操作： NodeJS 安装和Yarn 安装。 我将使用 Node 版本 v16.13.1 和 Yarn 版本 1.22.4。 如果你已经安装了 Node/NPM/Yarn，你也可以使用nvm 。

          我强烈建议您使用 VS Code 或 JetBrains Webstorm 作为这部分解决方案的 IDE。

          让我们首先进入clients文件夹并在终端中运行以下命令：

           ### creates the application with typescript support ### and Tailwindcss pre-installed in the "web" directory yarn create next-app --example with-tailwindcss web cd web ### Runs the NodeJS webserver yarn dev ### $ next dev ### ready - started server on 0.0.0.0:3000, url: http://localhost:3000 ### wait - compiling... ### event - compiled client and server successfully in 1728 ms (124 modules)

          如果您在浏览器中访问http://localhost:3000 ，您将看到默认的 Next.js 主页：

          您在运行 yarn dev 时运行 Next.js 应用程序时执行的任何更改都将（几乎）立即应用到您的浏览器中 - 保持开发周期精简和快速。

          但是现在，让我们在结束本系列的这一部分之前清理所有这些样板。 您可以安全地删除以下文件：

          • 并且您可以将整个/pages/index.tsx文件完全替换为以下内容：

             import type { NextPage } from ' next ' import Head from ' next/head ' const Home : NextPage = () => { return ( < div className = " w-full h-screen p-0 flex flex-col " > < Head > < title > Vinyl Catalog < /title > < link rel = " icon " href = " /favicon.ico " /> < /Head > < header className = " flex justify-center bg-white py-4 fixed w-full z-10 border-b " > Header < /header > < main className = " flex flex-col grow flex-nowrap " > < div className = " flex flex-col min-h-full w-full justify-center items-center container mx-auto py-10 " > < div className = " flex flex-col min-h-full items-center justify-center flex-nowrap bg-cool-gray-700 " > < h1 className = " text-9xl font-black text-white text-center " > < span className = " bg-gradient-to-r text-transparent bg-clip-text from-green-400 to-purple-500 " > Vinyl Catalog < /span > < /h1 > < /div > < /div > < /main > < footer className = " flex w-full items-center justify-center border-t " > < span className = " py-5 " > Footer < /span > < /footer > < /div > ) } export default Home

            这应该已经为我们提供了一个带有页眉和页脚的响应式页面，供我们使用，它应该看起来像这样：

            结论

            如果您按照现在应该完成的每个步骤进行操作：

            • 一个正在运行的 Golang 应用程序
            • 一个正在运行的 NextJS 应用程序
            • 使用 AWS Provider 和 Scaleway Provider 正确配置 Terraform

            我知道这是一个累人的过程，现在，没有那么多编码 - 但项目中更烦人的部分之一已经完成 - 初始设置。 在本系列的下一篇文章中，我们将从基础架构开始。 如果你想获得我们迄今为止所做的一切——除了 Cloud Providers 设置，这不可避免地你必须在你自己的机器上做——你可以克隆存储库的标签版本 1.0.0：

             git clone --depth 1 --branch v1.0.0 git@github.com:thiduzz/vinyl-catalog.git



    原文:

    Deploying a web application to Kubernetes some times may not be so straight-forward for people that are just starting with it. In this series we are gonna be deploying a Kubernetes cluster to a live environment (yes!) for you to have a real-life experience with Kubernetes - outside of your minikube and localhost - and hopefully by the end of this series you will be able to use the resulting project as a base for your future projects.

    We will be utilizing cloud providers that might incur some costs and the decision on which provider to pick is mainly due to its setup cost - all of the components which will incur costs will be provisioned with Terraform so that you can easily instantiate and destroy them every time you want to test something — keeping the costs of “experimentation” as low as possible.

    I am designing this series towards software developers with some level of experience but that may not have been introduced to the world of DevOps.

    Technologies and Concepts

    Before we start, I would like just to ponder a bit about the technologies, tools and providers we are gonna be utilizing in this project.

    • Golang Golang is one of the fastest-growing languages for the past years and has a really good learning curve for people coming from other languages and people that are just beginning with software development. Other than that, its a fast, with a low memory footprint, type-safe language that allow you to have well built software that is easy to predict and incorporate modern best practices in software development.

    Coming from a PHP development world I always find it troublesome to deploy applications with loads of networking caveats and components like web servers, proxies and etc - until I started migrating to Golang some years ago. Different than the PHP built-in web server — reason why for a production PHP application is highly recommended to use an external web server like Apache or NGINX — the Golang built-in web server is fast, reliable and production ready with no need of external configuration files or anything like that to get you up and running.

    • Next.js (React)
      In the game of JavaScript frameworks there are some that standout - but on top of most lists you will always read the name Next.js. Next is a React Framework that has been growing into peoples tastes and more and more people are dropping their React, Create React App and going to Next.js for a more robust solution - it’s community has been growing exponentially with the years by it’s intuitive usage, performance at scale and the ecosystem Vercel - the company that developed and maintain the project - provides and the amazing documentation that cover most (if not all) scenarios.Here are a couple of features that standout from the rest of the frameworks:

      • Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR): One of the most important things in a front-end deployment is the speed of the page and how optimal it’s view port initial rendering is, among other factors —most people opt for building static websites for this reason. But static websites usually have the problem that whenever you want to show a new page (like a new blog post) - you will have to rebuild the entire website and deploy once again. With ISR, you can easily define re-validation periods so that your front-end knows when to check if there is a new content to be “statically generated” and automatically make available without having to re-deploy/rebuild your application.
      • Built-in Image Optimization Component: Quite often you will find yourself trying to get the right balance between an image size and it’s quality and also find a lazy-load scheme/library for your images. This can be quite annoying and disruptive for the development workflow. For this scenario, Next.js provides a <Image> component that automatically optimize the image to be shown in .webp format - which usually already contribute to 30% of size decrease of the image in comparison with .jpeg and the images are automatically lazy-loaded.

    • Kubernetes (K8s)
      Kubernetes is the market standard/leader when it comes to container orchestration - with its declarative/imperative approach to managing objects. But it also provides some other goodies that usually are left unmentioned:

      • Ingress: Usually, in projects that do not use Kubernetes, if you had several services running and would like to put them behind a single API, the most standard approach would be to introduce an API Gateway or something like this - with Kubernetes, there is no need to do that to achieve the same effect, with Ingress you can specify internal routing rules just like you would with API Gateway.
      • Scaling and self monitoring: You can scale your resources horizontally and vertically with ease - in most cases allowing you to define the min and max amount of resources you can potentially dedicate for a resource like a pod or node. In addition to that if one of your resources, let’s say, a pod - crashes, it will instantly (or in a matter of seconds) spawn a new one to replace it.
      • Transparent control over cloud resources: Services like AWS EKS detect when a specific external resource is specified in your Kubernetes manifest and it will “auto-magically” spawn that resource outside of your Kubernetes cluster - one example is the LoadBalancer kind. You can read more about it here.

    There are, of course, many other benefits to use Kubernetes in your projects. I personally like to bring them into my personal projects because once you have a manifest/Helm charts that follow your architectural preference/wishes. It’s just applying it and you have all the infrastructure to run your applications. If you are a total beginner with Kubernetes I would highly suggest you to go through it’s concepts.

    Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) is the key for you to make your infrastructure reproducible and easy to manage. Nowadays any project that has some sort of configuration or instruction that require me to go through a panel and perform something manually for a project I usually look up if the service provides a Terraform Provider. You can find a wide range of providers - from NewRelic, Auth0 to more standard ones like AWS, GCP and Scaleway. It will allow us to create the infrastructure for this project and destroy it with 2 simple commands - ensuring that our costs don’t get out of control and we remove all resources once we are done.
    For this specific project we are gonna be using Scaleway provider and maybe the NewRelic (we will have to see as we go 😄 ).

    • Cloud ProvidersAs mentioned above - we are gonna be making use of Scaleway Elements to provision our Kubernetes cluster, but what I did not tell you so far is that we are also going to utilize AWS to provision our Serverless database. So, yes, our solution will be multi-cloud and because I am cheap we are going to be using the smallest instances/configurations they provide - but you are more than welcome to use the one you want - at your own expense 😂
      • Scaleway: Actually a provider I haven’t heard about so much in the past because all tutorials and buzz is usually with AWS and GCP. But nonetheless, Scaleway is a really good and reliable service that different than most cloud providers, it gives you a really transparent and predictable pricing structures and instances that are specifically built for testing/experimentation. One of it’s drawbacks is that they do not have such a wide range of services as AWS - so, if your solution need something specific like a Kafka Cluster - I would rather recommend you to look in another Cloud Provider (ie. AWS EKS to access AWS MSK). You can manually try out the different instance sizes to see how much it will cost you per year/month and so on.
      • AWS: No need for introductions at this point - the reason we are going to use AWS it is mainly for its cheap Aurora Serverless database - MySQL flavored - paying only for what we utilize and probably being covered by the free-tier quotas 😊 It’s important to note that in theory with the Kubernetes cluster we could instantiate pods with MySQL containers (or any DB) - but this comes with it’s own burden - we would need to manage the storage of our cluster - this is not recommended for Kubernetes beginners and probably I will be covering this in a future post.

    Overview

    In this image you can have a better idea of what we are gonna be building - I omitted some components such as Network configuration, Certificates (cert-manager) and our NewRelic side-cart.

    All of the code we are going to be creating for this series is gonna be available in this repository - and you can navigate through the series progress by checking out the git tag related to the post part (ie. Post part 1 = tag 1.0.0, Post part 2 = tag 2.0.0). So without further ado - let’s get our hands dirty 😎.

    Setup

    Project

    You can create the following folder structure:

    |—— clients

    |—— services

    |——————- api

    Terraform

    The first thing we will need is actually to install Terraform - it’s usually quite straight-forward. You can either go to Terraform website and install a specific version or you can install tfswitch which allow you to easily install and switch versions of Terraform from your terminal.

    In addition to that, you can install the VS Code Extension or the JetBrains Plugin to provide you syntax highlight.

    For the purpose of this series - we are gonna be using Terraform 1.1.9. So after you are done with the installation you should be able do to the following command:

    terraform version
## Terraform v1.1.9
## on linux_amd64

    Now let’s create our main Terraform file - this file will be used to configure our Cloud Providers so that we can actually create the resources necessary for our project. Run the command from the root of your project structure.

    touch .infrastructure/main.tf

    Now let’s fill this file with the AWS and Scaleway providers configuration - you can find more detail about the providers and all of its documentation in their respective pages.

    Let’s start first with the AWS provider:

    terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } }
} provider "aws" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps
}

    And then the add the Scaleway Provider:

    terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } scaleway = { source = "scaleway/scaleway" version = "2.2.1-rc.3" } }
} provider "scaleway" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps
} provider "aws" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps
}

    Now you can go into the .infrastructure folder and perform the following command - which will download the providers.

    cd .infrastructure
terraform init

    After doing that, you will notice that a new folder .terraform and a .terraform.lock.hcl is created. The .terraform folder container mainly the binaries that allow us to communicate to the provider APIs. We don’t need to version this 2 files - so let’s create a .gitignore

    echo ".terraform" >> .gitignore
echo ".terraform.lock.hcl" >> .gitignore

    In terms of tooling, we are set here. We still need to configure the providers with our credentials, and to retrieve those credentials we will need to dig on the console for each provider.

    AWS Account Setup

    If you already have credentials (Access Token and Secret) for your AWS Account you can completely skip this section - it’s just important to ensure that your user has the policies described below.

    Let’s create your account first - go to AWS website. And click on “Complete Sign Up” on the top bar

    Go through the entire flow (you may be required to input your payment details).

    By the end of the process you should land in the following screen - don’t get too used to it:

    As a first step in your AWS Account - I highly suggest you to setup a Budget Alert and Multifactor Authentication - if you already done that, you can skip to the Setup User Credentials. The account you just created is called “root” account and has Administrator powers by default.

    AWS Budget Alert

    Go to “Billing Dashboard” on the Top Right menu:

    Then on the right-side menu click on “Budgets” and then click on “Create Budget”. You are gonna be presented the following Wizard:

    My recommended options are:

    • Budget type: Cost Budget
    • Budget Name: Personal Budget
    • Period: Monthly
    • Budget renewal type: Recurring budget
    • Start Month: (the current month)
    • Budgeting method: Fixed
    • Enter your budgeted amount ($): (personal preference - I usually put 20 USD, month)
    • Budget Scope: All AWS services (Recommended)

    And then define the alerts that make sense to you according to the budget you defined. To be quite clear, defining a budget does not mean AWS will charge you the budgeted amount and it won’t prevent you from going over-budget - it just allows you to have a better monitoring of the costs in your account.

    Multi-Factor Authentication

    If you haven’t setup the Multi-Factor Authentication while registering, you can do so by accessing “Security Credentials” on the top left corner of the AWS Console:

    You are then gonna be send to a page where you can Assign an MFA device:

    From there it’s pretty much following the Wizard - scan the QR code with the MFA Device of your preference (ie. Google Authenticator) and you will be logged out of it afterwards.

    Setup AWS User Credentials

    We are going to be creating a role and a user specific for this project - this is a good practice because it allows you to have fine-grained control over all the projects you are running in terms of permissions and security.

    From AWS Console - go to the IAM page:

    Click on “User” and then “Create User” and then input any name for your user (ie. vinyl-catalog-user) and select “Access key - Programmatic access” and hit next.

    In the next page select “Attach existing policies directly” and search and select the following policies:

    • AmazonVPCFullAccess - We will probably need to configure how our VPC is configured - allowing that only our Scaleway Kubernetes cluster can have access to our database
    • AmazonRDSFullAccess - This policy will allow us to create the database instance and may as well allow us to configure database users.

    This are really wide policies and for now that’s alright - we don’t know exactly what we are gonna need to configure and we can later on adjust to a more granular level control and maybe add some others. For now, that’s all we will probably need - so you can hit “Next” and if desired fill in some “Tags” so you can later on have a better reference to your newly created user - hit “Next” again and you will end up in the following screen:

    Click “Create User” and then you should see the following screen:

    Copy both the Access Key and Secret Key and paste into an editor of your choice and save it in a text file (temporarily). We are gonna come back to it.

    AWS Credentials Vault

    Let’s ensure that our access key and secret key are not in plain text in our laptops - to help us with that we are gonna use AWS Vault - which is a tool that help us manage AWS profiles and credentials in a secure way - with some nifty solutions as key rotation via CLI and etc. But before we do that, we need to install the AWS CLI (if you don’t have it yet):

    1. Install AWS CLI - visit the CLI website and you will see on the right side links to install for your OS.

    1. Install AWS Vault - visit the Github repository and follow the installing instructions in the README.

    Once you are done with it - you should be able to perform the following command:

    Now let’s actually add our profile - and copy/paste the Access Key and Secret Key you saved in a temporary file in the last step:

    aws-vault add vinyl-catalog
Enter Access Key ID: [XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX]
Enter Secret Access Key: [XXXXXXXXXXXXXX]
Added credentials to profile "vinyl-catalog" in vault

    After this step - ensure you delete your temporary file - to confirm everything is working with our user access run:

    aws-vault exec vinyl-catalog -- aws rds describe-db-instances

    This command should list all our databases (at this point - we have none).

    Now, let’s go back to our Terraform file - [main.tf](http://main.tf) - we are gonna specify the recently created profile and the region that better suits you - in my case it’s Frankfurt (eu-central-1). So your main.tf should look like this now:

    terraform { required_providers { aws = { source = "hashicorp/aws" version = "4.11.0" } scaleway = { source = "scaleway/scaleway" version = "2.2.1-rc.3" } }
} provider "scaleway" { # We are gonna change this in the next steps
} provider "aws" { profile = "vinyl-catalog" region = "eu-central-1"
}

    Scaleway Account Setup

    If you already have credentials (Access Token and Secret) for your Scaleway Elements Account you can completely skip this section - it’s just important to ensure that your user has the policies described below.

    Let’s create your account first - go to Scaleway website. And go through the registration process - when presented the account type option - I would suggest you to pick the “Build” option - it will require you to input your Billing Information and verify your identity - it’s a bit lengthy process and the Identity check might take some minutes/hours - but by the end you should be able to reach the main page:

    The first thing we are going to do is create a new Project - click on the drop-down menu on the top and click “Create Project”:

    Fill in the project name:

    That’s it for now - let’s just ensure our account is properly setup so we don’t have any major surprises with the billing and security of it.

    Scaleway Budget Alert

    Click on the menu button the top right corner and select “Billing”:

    Just like the AWS Budget Alert - set yourself a budget that you believe it’s good enough for you and don’t forget to add the Alert triggers:

    This way - if you go over budget at least you know you can act upon.

    Setup Scaleway User Credentials

    Now we will be creating the credentials to enable us to communicate through the Scaleway API via Terraform. To do so - ensure that you are within our project - in my case I named it “vinyl-catalog”:

    Click on the right-hand top corner menu button and then click on “Credentials”:

    You should be presented the following screen:

    Click on “Generate new API key” - you will be asked the purpose - you can put anything here - I will specify “Terraform”:

    Copy both Access Key and Secret Key into a temporary text file - we are gonna back to it.

    Scaleway Client Configuration

    Different than AWS - Scaleway does not have right now a lot of tooling surrounding it’s ecosystem - and there is no tool comparable to AWS Vault as well. For that reason, we are gonna take the safest approach recommended by Scaleway - utilizing the a Shared Configuration created by the Scaleway CLI.

    First of all - you can install the Scaleway CLI by following the installation steps within this Github Repository. By the end of it you should be able to perform the following command in your terminal:

    scw version
#### BuildDate unknown
#### GoVersion go1.17.2
#### GitBranch unknown
#### GitCommit unknown
#### GoArch amd64
#### GoOS linux

    Now let’s initialize CLI with our credentials - utilize the Secret Key shown in the previous step (remember to delete the temporary file). :

    scw init --profile vinyl-catalog
Enter a valid secret-key or an email: [XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX]
### To improve this tool we rely on diagnostic and usage data.
### Sending such data is optional and can be disabled at any time by running "scw config set send-telemetry=false".
Do you want to send usage statistics and diagnostics? (Y/n): [n] ### To fully enjoy Scaleway CLI we recommend you install autocomplete support in your shell.
Do you want to install autocomplete? (Y/n): [Y]
### Creating new config at /home/thiago/.config/scw/config.yaml
### Config saved at /home/thiago/.config/scw/config.yaml:
### send_telemetry: false
### profiles:
### vinyl-catalog:
### access_key: [XXXXXXXXXXXXX]
### secret_key: [XXXXXXXXXXXXX]
### default_organization_id: [XXXXXXXXXXXXX]
### default_project_id: [XXXXXXXXXXXXX]
### default_region: fr-par
### default_zone: fr-par-1
### To enable autocomplete, scw needs to update your shell configuration.
What type of shell are you using (default: zsh): [zsh]
We found an SSH key in ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub. Do you want to add it to your Scaleway account? (Y/n): [Y]
### Initialization completed with success.

    If you already have an SSH Key available you might get asked to link it to your account - you should accept that! Later on, it will be quite convenient. If you don’t know how to create an SSH Key - follow this tutorial. For this specific series, we are gonna utilize the “vinyl-catalog” profile - but in the future, if you wish to have different users or credentials you can set different profiles and active profile in $HOME/.config/scw/config.yaml (Unix) / %USERPROFILE%/.config/scw/config.yaml ****(Windows).

    And as the last step - let’s add our newly created profile to our Terrraform file (main.tf) - you can also pick the region/zone that makes more sense to you. By the end of the setup the file should look like so:

    terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "4.11.0"
    }
    scaleway = {
      source = "scaleway/scaleway"
      version = "2.2.1-rc.3"
    }
  }
}

provider "scaleway" {
  profile = "vinyl-catalog"
  zone   = "fr-par-1"
  region = "fr-par"
}

provider "aws" {
  profile = "vinyl-catalog"
  region = "eu-central-1"
}

    Back-end Service

    With all the Cloud and credentials setup done and out of the way (for now) we can now start looking into code 🚀! So - as mentioned before we are going to be building a Golang Service - at this stage it’s intentions are really basic: receive a request in localhost:8080/up and return a simple status 200 with any content.

    Golang Setup

    You can utilize for the development of the service VS Code with the Golang extension or, like me, utilize JetBrains GoLand which is an IDE specific for Golang. Before we start creating files and going crazy with some Go development - let’s install GoLang - the easiest and simplest way I found is by utilizing gvm - you can find of it as the JavaScript nvm but for GoLang.

    So you can install it, first of all - check the requirements/dependencies according to your OS here and after you have its requirements/dependencies installed run the following commands:

    ### Download the GVM installer (Bash)
bash < <(curl -s -S -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/moovweb/gvm/master/binscripts/gvm-installer)
### Or Download the GVM installer (zsh)
zsh < <(curl -s -S -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/moovweb/gvm/master/binscripts/gvm-installer)

    By the end you should be able to run the following command to validate your installation:

    gvm version
### Go Version Manager v1.0.22 installed at /home/thiago/.gvm

    After that you can install Golang version 1.18:

    gvm install go1.18
### In some OS the command above might fail (ie. Ubuntu 20.04), 
### in that case you can can try downloading the binary with the -B flag
gvm install go1.18 -B

    And to start using the newly installed version and validate your installation run:

    ### start using and set the Go 1.18 as the default version
gvm use go1.18 --default
### Now using version go1.18
go version
### go version go1.18 linux/amd64

    Golang Application Scaffolding

    Open a terminal in the services/api folder and run the following command. Replace the string with your Github username. It’s important to note that this repository does not have to exist and you can host your code in a different repository if you wish - this will only play a role if you would like to eventually distribute your application as a package for other people to consume. This command will create a go.mod file (Go Modules file) - which will later on be used for us to manage our Golang package dependencies.

    go mod init github.com/{YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME}/vinyl-catalog
### ie. go mod init github.com/thiduzz/vinyl-catalog

    Create a main.go file within services/api/cmd/vinyl_catalog/ folder. This file will be the entrypoint of our Backend application. Let’s make our application (main.go) print out something to see if our local Golang setup is working properly.

    // Main package
package main //import a package fmt that enable us to print things to the stdout (console)
import "fmt" // Entry function of the application
func main() { fmt.Println("Hey! My name is Thiago")
}

    And then from the services/api directory run the following command:

    go run cmd/vinyl_catalog/main.go
### Hey! My name is Thiago

    Cool, if you saw the Print in your console it means that the setup is fine and we can move on to start defining a basic Golang web server. Lucky for us, Golang have one that we are gonna be using across this series and you can utilize it even for production purposes.

    So let’s start by adapting our main function and imports:

    import ( "log" "net/http"
) func main() { log.Println("Starting server at port 8080") if err := http.ListenAndServe(":8080", nil); err != nil { log.Fatal(err) }
}

    If we run the application again (go run cmd/vinyl_catalog/main.go) we will get something like the log below and the terminal will hold (to quit you can do CTRL+C within the terminal) - what is happening is basically our web server is listening for connections but no handler for our routes have been created - you can even access in your browser [localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080) (but you will get only 404s):

    2022/04/23 12:03:35 Starting server at port 8080

    So let’s add a handleFunc mapped to the /up route - for now we are gonna be using this simple setup with the DefaultServeMux only but we are gonna incrementally optimize it to make it easier to manage when we have more routes and to better treat authentication, dependencies, validation and SSL:

    func main() { log.Println("Starting server at port 8080") // Adds a HandleFunc to the DefaultMux http.HandleFunc("/up", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { // Utilize fmt.Fprintf that accepts a io.Writer  // and write the content of the page  fmt.Fprintf(w, "God save the Queen!") }) if err := http.ListenAndServe(":8080", nil); err != nil { log.Fatal(err) }
}

    If you are curious to know a bit more about handleFunc, Handlers and DefaultServeMux I recommend you to give a read on this post.

    Just to validate if everything works run the application (go run cmd/vinyl_catalog/main.go) and access the [localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080) from your browser - you should see the following:

    Front-end Service Scaffolding

    We are almost done with the basic setup of our project - the last remaining piece is the easiest part given the amount of documentation on how to do that. We are gonna be quickly creating a Next.JS application which eventually will allow us to interact with our Golang Backend.

    I assume that you already have NodeJS and Yarn installed by now - if not, just follow the instructions within this links: NodeJS installation and Yarn installation. I am going to be using Node version v16.13.1 and Yarn version 1.22.4. You may as well use nvm in case you already have an installation of Node/NPM/Yarn.

    I highly recommend you to use VS Code or JetBrains Webstorm as your IDE for this part of the solution.

    Next.js Application Scaffolding

    Let’s start by going into the clients folder and running in the terminal the following command:

    ### creates the application with typescript support 
### and Tailwindcss pre-installed in the "web" directory
yarn create next-app --example with-tailwindcss web
cd web
### Runs the NodeJS webserver
yarn dev
### $ next dev
### ready - started server on 0.0.0.0:3000, url: http://localhost:3000
### wait - compiling...
### event - compiled client and server successfully in 1728 ms (124 modules)

    And if you visit the http://localhost:3000 in your browser you will be presented the default Next.js homepage:

    Any change that you perform when running the Next.js application while running yarn dev will be (almost) instantly applied in your browser - keeping the development cycle lean and fast.

    But now, let’s clean up all this boilerplate before we wrap this Part of the series. You can safely delete the following files:

    • /public/vercel.svg
    • /pages/api (entire folder with its content)

    And you can completely replace the entire /pages/index.tsx file with the following content:

    import type { NextPage } from 'next'
import Head from 'next/head' const Home: NextPage = () => { return ( <div className="w-full h-screen p-0 flex flex-col"> <Head> <title>Vinyl Catalog</title>
 <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" /> </Head>
 <header className="flex justify-center bg-white py-4 fixed w-full z-10 border-b"> Header </header>
 <main className="flex flex-col grow flex-nowrap"> <div className="flex flex-col min-h-full w-full justify-center items-center container mx-auto py-10"> <div className="flex flex-col min-h-full items-center justify-center flex-nowrap bg-cool-gray-700"> <h1 className="text-9xl font-black text-white text-center"> <span className="bg-gradient-to-r text-transparent bg-clip-text from-green-400 to-purple-500"> Vinyl Catalog </span>
 </h1>
 </div>
 </div>
 </main>
 <footer className="flex w-full items-center justify-center border-t"> <span className="py-5">Footer</span>
 </footer>
 </div>
 )
} export default Home

    This should provide us already a somewhat responsive page with a header and footer for us to play around and it should look like so:

    Conclusion

    If you followed along each of the steps you should have by now:

    • A running Golang application
    • A running NextJS application
    • Terraform properly configured with AWS Provider and Scaleway Provider

    I know it’s a tiring process and for now, not so much coding - but one of the more annoying parts of the project is done - the initial setup. In the next post of this series we will be starting with our Infrastructure. If you want to get everything we’ve done so far — apart from the Cloud Providers setup, that inevitably you will have to do in your own machine — you can clone tag version 1.0.0 of the repository:

    git clone --depth 1 --branch v1.0.0 git@github.com:thiduzz/vinyl-catalog.git
item.author: thizaom

item.title: 全国大学高校基础信息API接口 | 全国大学高校基础信息 API 接口
item.description:
全国大学高校基础信息API接口

提供全国大学高校基础信息。

一、产品功能

  • 提供最新的全国学院基本信息以及录取数据；
  • 近总计300条全国高校数据；
  • 每月更新数据；
  • 全接口支持HTTPS（TLS v1.0 / v1.1 / v1.2 / v1.3）；
  • 中央苹果ATS；
  • 全国多节点CDN部署；
  • 接口极速响应，多台服务器制造API接口均衡；
  • 接口调用与状态监控

接口请求地址： https ://api.gugudata.com/location/college

返回格式： application/json； 字符集=utf-8

请求方式： GET

请求协议： HTTPS

答：一般情况下，我们建议您在我们的请求中安装您的应用程序的服务，并应用您的所有请求的 API 服务，所有请求都应该如此，因此服务的架构也因此易于维护。

答：使用于所有可以请求的开发语言，用于您的项目可以快速地进行网络构建。

咕咕咕咕，专业的数据提供数据，让数据提供专业数据的接口、商业分析成为您的生产。

谷歌数据基于我们七年为企业客户的千亿级数据存储与优化、相关海量基础数据部分提供支持，将合规的通用数据、通用功能抽象成产品级数据API，满足用户在产品开发过程中对基础数据的需求，同时降低了海量数据的存储和运维成本，以及复杂功能的技术窗帘与人力开发。

除了开放的分类数据与功能接口外，还有海量数据正在整理、清洗、集成、制造中，会开放更多的数据与云端功能API供用户使用。

原文:

全国大学高校基础信息 API 接口

提供全国大学高校基础信息。

1. 产品功能

  • 提供最新的全国学院基本信息以及招生数据；
  • 总计近 3000 条全国高校精准数据；
  • 每月一次更新校正数据；
  • 全接口支持 HTTPS（TLS v1.0 / v1.1 / v1.2 / v1.3）；
  • 全面兼容 Apple ATS；
  • 全国多节点 CDN 部署；
  • 接口极速响应，多台服务器构建 API 接口负载均衡；
  • 接口调用状态与状态监控

API 详情地址: https://www.gugudata.com/api/details/college

接口请求地址: https://api.gugudata.com/location/college

返回格式: application/json; charset=utf-8

请求方式: GET

请求协议: HTTPS

请求示例: https://api.gugudata.com/location/college?appkey=YOUR_APPKEY&keywords=YOUR_VALUE&pagesize=10&pageindex=1&keywordstrict=false&collegecategory=

接口测试: https://api.gugudata.com/location/college/demo

3. 请求参数

参数名 参数类型 是否必须 默认值 备注
appkey string 是 YOUR_APPKEY 付费后获取的 APPKEY
keywords string 是 YOUR_VALUE 搜索关键字，模糊匹配高校名称、省市区、高校旧称、地址字段，参数值为空则返回所有数据
pagesize int 否 10 每页数据量，参数最大值为 100
pageindex int 否 1 页码，第几页数据
keywordstrict string 否 false 控制 keyword 参数在查询时是否进行模糊查询，true 为精确匹配高校名称，默认值为 false 进行模糊查询
collegecategory string 否 学院类别，参数默认值为空，不进行筛选。可选参数为：理工类\

Data.Coordinate string 学院地理坐标经纬度，格式为 [经度,纬度]，地理坐标系为 GCJ-02。需要转换坐标系请调用地理坐标系转换接口

Data.Is985 bool 是否为 985 院校

Data.IsDualClass bool 是否为双一流院校
Data.CollegeCategory string 学院类别，值的枚举为: 综合类\
Data.CollegeTags string[] 学院标签
Data.EduLevel string 学院学制，值的枚举为: 普通本科\
Data.CollegeProperty string 学院资质，值的枚举为: 公办\
Data.CollegeCode string 学院编号
Data.Ranking int 全国排名，接口返回的数据结果默认按照此数值进行升序排列

Data.WebSite string 学院官网

Data.ShortName string 学院简称
Data.MajorList.MajorTitle string 开设专业大类
Data.MajorList.Majors string[] 此大类下开设的专业
状态码 状态码解释 备注
100 正常返回 可通过判断此状态码断言接口正常返回
-1 请求失败 请求处理请求失败
501 参数错误 请检查您传递的参数个数以及参数类型是否匹配
502 请求频率受限 一般建议每秒请求不超过 100 次
503 APPKEY 权限超限/订单到期 请至开发者中心检查您的 APPKEY 是否到期或是否权限超限
504 APPKEY 错误 请检查传递的 APPKEY 是否为开发者中心获取到的值
505 请求的次数超出接口限制 请检查对应接口是否有请求次数限制以及您目前的接口请求剩余次数
900 接口内部响应错误 接口可用性为 99.999%，如获取到此状态码请邮件联系我们

示例代码包含的开发语言：C#, Go, Java, jQuery, Node.js, Objective-C, PHP, Python, Ruby, Swift 等，其他语言进行对应的 RESTful API 请求实现即可。

7. 常见问题 Q&A

A: 所有的数据都是直接返回的，部分周期性数据在更新周期内数据是缓存的。

A: 一般建议将对我们 API 的请求放置在您应用的后端服务中发起，您应用的所有前端请求都应该指向您自己的后端服务，这样的架构也更加地纯粹且易维护。

A: 可以使用于所有可以进行网络请求的开发语言，用于您项目快速地进行数据构建。

A: 接口后台架构和我们给企业提供的商业项目架构一致，您可以通过访问测试接口查看接口相关返回性能与信息。

item.title: 共享配置 | Shared Configs
item.description:
互操作性的一个关键部分是从一台机器移动到另一台机器时编码环境之间的一致性。 实现这种一致性的一种方法是确保您的常用配置（例如 .bashrc/.zshrc 文件）已保存到托管位置。

这是我自己的共享配置 repo 的示例： https ://github.com/nezhivar/shared-configs

这使我能够灵活地确保我可以使用提供的脚本以最短的停机时间从一个环境移动到另一个环境。 旨在创建从一台机器到另一台机器的接缝过渡。

为什么

过渡到新环境或新机器可能是一个挑战，也是一个耗时的过程，因为您必须记住或研究旧机器中的某些配置如何才能使项目正常工作。 通过在所有机器之间创建共享配置，您至少可以缩短从一台机器到另一台机器的过渡时间

未来状态

到目前为止，我已将其配置为我偶尔运行的手动过程。 将来，这可以通过每月一次的 cronjob 来设置。 也可以添加其他配置，例如 VSCode 设置和 mac 特定设置。



原文:

A key piece of interoperability is consistency between coding environment when moving from one machine to another. One way to achieve this consistency is to ensure your common configs such as your .bashrc/.zshrc files have been saved to a hosted location.

Here is an example of my own shared config repo: https://github.com/nezhivar/shared-configs

This gives me the flexibility to ensure that I can move from one environment to the next with minimal downtime with provided scripts. Aiming to create a seaming transition from one machine to the next.

Why

Transition to a new environment or a new machine can be challenge and a time consuming process as you will have to either remember or research exactly how some was configured in your old machine in order to get a project to work. By creating a shared configs amongst all your machines, you will at least improve your transition time from one machine to the next

Future State

I have configured it so far to be a manual process that I run occasionally. In the future, this could be setup through a monthly cronjob. Other configurations could be added as well such as VSCode settings and mac specific settings.

item.title: 如何准备 SC-400 考试？ | How to Prepare for SC-400 Exam ?
item.description:
为本次考试测量的技能

始终检查为考试测量的技能非常重要，因为它们现在大约每 6 个月左右刷新一次。

以下是本次考试测量的技能。

  1. 实施信息保护 (35-40%)
  2. 实施数据丢失防护 (30-35%)
  3. 实施信息治理 (25-30%)

先决条件

在参加这次考试之前，我们应该：

  • 信息保护概念的基本知识。
  • 了解云计算概念。
  • 了解 Microsoft 365 产品和服务。

微软学习平台

以下是可用于准备考试的模块。

SC-400：在 Microsoft 365 中实施信息保护

SC-400：在 Microsoft 365 中实施信息治理

在从微软学习上面的模块学习之后，实验室也可以得到一些实践经验。

讲师指导的培训

在 Microsoft 的网站上，可以访问考试 SC-400：Microsoft 信息保护管理员的培训包。

下面提供了 Microsoft 培训计划的列表。
课程 SC-400T00：Microsoft 信息保护管理员

考试转储可在下面找到

参考链接

微软信息保护管理员 (SC-400)

感谢您阅读我的文章直到最后。 我希望你今天学到了一些特别的东西。 如果您喜欢这篇文章，请分享给您的朋友，如果您有建议或想法与我分享，请在评论框中写下。



原文:

Skills measured for this exam

It is really important to always check the skills measured for the exam because they are refreshing quite frequently now about every 6 months or so.

Below are the skills measured for this exam.

  1. Implement information protection (35-40%)
  2. Implement data loss prevention (30-35%)
  3. Implement information governance (25-30%)

Prerequisites

Before attending this exam, we should have:

  • Foundational knowledge of Microsoft security and compliance technologies.
  • Basic knowledge of information protection concepts.
  • Understanding of cloud computing concepts.
  • Understanding of Microsoft 365 products and services.

Microsoft Learning Platform

Below are the modules available to get prepared for the exam.

SC-400: Implement Information Protection in Microsoft 365

SC-400: Implement Data Loss Prevention

SC-400: Implement Information Governance in Microsoft 365

After learning the above modules from Microsoft learn, Labs are also available to get some practical experience.

SC-400T00A-Microsoft-Information-Protection-Administrator

Instructor-led training

On Microsoft’s website, training packages for the Exam SC-400: Microsoft Information Protection Administrator are accessible.

A list of Microsoft’s training programs is provided below.
Course SC-400T00: Microsoft Information Protection Administrator

Exam dumps are available in the below

Microsoft SC-400 Exam dumps

Reference Links

Microsoft Information Protection Administrator (SC-400)

item.title: 在 C++ 中使用 using 的 4 种用途 | The 4 use of using in C++
item.description:
当我编写代码时，我不仅想编写正确的代码。 我还想编写可理解且可维护的代码。 我想提供不仅对编译器而且对其他人都易于阅读的代码。 毕竟，人类会比编译器更频繁地阅读我的代码。

我一直在思考帮助我们编写可读代码的最重要的关键字是什么。 可能这个问题没有多大意义，但const和using绝对是其中之一。 我们已经讨论了很多const ，这次是时候看看 using using如何改进我们的代码了。

我们将回顾一下我们可以使用它的 4 种方式：

  • 别名

    在旧的 C++ 中，我们可以使用typedef来提供另一个名称，为我们的类型提供别名。 有时您可能想使用它而不是强类型，只是为了从更有意义的名称（如int ）中受益。

    其他时候你想缩短长类型以便于使用：

     typedef std :: vector < std :: string >:: iterator Iterator ;

    从 C++11 开始，我们可以使用using而不是typedef来实现相同的结果。

     using Horsepower = int ; using Iterator = std :: vector < std :: string >:: iterator ;

    为什么要使用using而不是旧的typedef ？ 只需阅读以上声明！ 就像T.43 核心指南所说的那样，它更具可读性！ 关键字的含义很明确，然后 name 在前，old 在 a =之后。

    此外， using可以更普遍地使用。 它可用于typedef会导致编译错误的模板别名。

     template < typename T > typedef std :: map < int , T > MapT ; // error template < typename T > using MapT = std :: map < int , T > ; // OK

    在命名空间和块范围内使用指令

    您可能已经看到很多代码示例在#include语句之后包含using namespace std行。

    您可能已经看过很多这样的应用程序代码。

    你可能已经被告知这很糟糕。

    如果您在头文件中的全局范围内进行操作，那就特别糟糕，就像[核心指南中的 SF.7 说的]：

     // bad.h #include <iostream>
using namespace std ; // bad // user.cpp #include "bad.h"
// some function that happens to be named copy bool copy ( /*... some parameters ...*/ ); int main () { // now overloads local ::copy and std::copy, could be ambiguous copy ( /*...*/ ); }

    在我看来，即使作为读者你不能确定一个函数来自哪里，这也是不好的。 这是一个简单的示例，但是当您在长.cpp文件中使用using namespace时，很难跟踪某些对象的来源。 我更喜欢using -declarations，而且我也经常引入别名命名空间。

     //some.h #include <other.h>
using mcs = mynamespace :: component :: subcomponent ; msc :: Class foo (); //some.cpp msc :: Class foo () { using msc :: AnotherClass ; AnotherClass bar ; // ... }

    因此，我不会污染全局命名空间。 您必须在 find 中保留的是，当您将using -directive 引入到全局名称空间标头的头文件中时，您不会只是在当前范围内搞砸了。

    如果您在其他文件中包含头文件，您还将包含所有这些引入的符号。 如果使用`-directives 引入不同全局级别的不同头文件，情况会变得更糟，名称查找的结果可能取决于包含的顺序。

    为避免所有此类问题，只需遵循SF.7并且不要在头文件中写入全局范围内的 using namespace 。

    在命名空间和块范围内使用声明

    虽然using -directive 将命名空间的所有符号都带入当前范围，但using -declaration 只会带入一项！

    cpp using std::string; string foo{"bar"};

    在上面的例子中，我们只是演示了它是如何工作的。 它仍然是不要过度使用的东西！ 在文件范围内使用它比在同一范围内using指令更危险，但风险仍然存在。

    从 C++20 开始，还可以将作用域枚举器引入块作用域的命名空间中！

    枚举类颜色{红、绿、蓝}；

    类 MyClass { 使用 Color::red; 颜色 c = 红色； 事实上，它也适用于旧式的 unscoped enum ，但我们为什么要这样做呢？

    这是一种在派生类中将protected的基类成员公开为public的简单方法。 它可以用于函数和变量。

    包括

    类 Base { protected: void foo() { std::cout << "Base::foo()\n";

    派生类：公共基础 { 公共：使用 Base::foo; 使用 Base::m_i;

    如果您尝试修改上述示例并using -declarations 删除这两个示例中的任何一个，您将看到编译失败。

    如果派生类已有同名成员，则编译不会。 从基类导入的符号将被隐藏。

    包括

    类 Base { protected: void foo() { std::cout << "Base::foo()\n"; 派生类：公共基础{公共：

    使用 Base::foo;

    我发现这种技术对单元测试非常有用。 当您手动编写模拟时，您通常必须从基类和您将要模拟的类中公开受保护的成员函数。

    一种方法是转发呼叫。

    希望模拟中的函数名称没有改变，但我已经看过几次了。 当他们意识到有更好的选择时，这确实给维护者带来了额外的负担。

    虚空 testMeToo() { }

    类 MockClassUnderTest : public ClassUnderTest { public: void testMe() override { ClassUnderTest::testMe();

    除了捆绑了很多不必要的代码之外，上面的问题是如果testMe或testMeToo的参数列表发生变化，您还必须更新MockClassUnderTest 。 您可以使用using摆脱这种需求。

    cpp class MockClassUnderTest : public ClassUnderTest { public: using ClassUnderTest::testMe; using ClassUnderTest::testMeToo; };

    现在我们的代码更少了，发生的事情也更容易理解了。 作为奖励，甚至简化了维护。

    结论

    在本文中，我们讨论了使用using关键字的 4 种不同方式。 这是在派生类中创建别名和导入基类成员的正确方法。 同时，它们还可以用于将整个命名空间引入当前范围，这可能特别危险。 最后但同样重要的是， using还可以将单一类型引入当前范围，这是一个比引入整个命名空间更危险的选择，但仍应谨慎使用。

    连接更深

    如果你喜欢这篇文章，请

    米哈乌·皮奥特罗夫斯基 - Nov 5 '21 21 年 11 月 5 日



原文:

When I write code I don't only want to write code that is correct. I also want to write code that is understandable, and maintainable. I want to deliver code that is easy to read not only for the compiler but also for other human beings. After all, humans will read my code more frequently than compilers.

I have been thinking what are the single most important keywords that help us write readable code. Probably this question doesn't make much sense, but const and using are definitely among these. We already discussed const a lot, this time it's time to see how using using can improve our code.

We are going to review the 4 ways we can use it:

  • type aliasing with using
  • introducing complete namespaces with using-directive
  • introducing members of another namespace with using-declaration
  • importing class members with using-declaration

Aliasing

In old C++ we could use typedef to give another name, to give an alias for our types. Sometimes you might want to use it instead of strong typing, just to benefit from more meaningful names like int.

Other times you want to shorten long types for easier usage:

typedef std::vector<std::string>::iterator Iterator;

Since C++11 we can use using instead of typedef to achieve the same results.

using Horsepower = int;
using Iterator = std::vector<std::string>::iterator;

Why would you use using over the good old typedef? Just read the above statements! Exactly like the T.43 core guideline says, it's more readable! The keyword has a very clear meaining, then the name comes first and the old comes after a =.

Besides, using can be used more generally. It can be used for template aliases where typedef would lead to a compilation error.

template<typename T>
typedef std::map<int, T> MapT; // error template<typename T>
using MapT = std::map<int, T>; // OK

Using-directive in namespace and block scope

You've probably seen many code examples that right after the #include statements contain the line using namespace std.

You've probably seen lots of such application code.

You've probably been told that it's bad.

It's particularly bad if you do in at the global scope in a header file, just like [SF.7 from the Core Guidelines says]:

// bad.h
#include <iostream>
using namespace std; // bad  // user.cpp
#include "bad.h"

// some function that happens to be named copy
bool copy(/*... some parameters ...*/); int main()
{ // now overloads local ::copy and std::copy, could be ambiguous copy(/*...*/);
}

In my opinion, even the fact that as a reader you cannot be sure where a function comes from is bad. This is a simplistic example, but when you use using namespace in a long .cpp file it's hard to keep track of where certain objects come from. I prefer having using-declarations instead and I also often introduce alias namespaces.

//some.h
#include <other.h>

using mcs = mynamespace::component::subcomponent; msc::Class foo();
//some.cpp
msc::Class foo() { using msc::AnotherClass; AnotherClass bar; // ...
}

As such, I don't pollute the global namespace. What you have to keep in find is that when you introduce a using-directive into a header file at the global namespace header, you don't just mess things up in the current scope.

If you include the header file in other files, you'll also bring the inclusion of all those introduced symbols. If you introduce different header files with different global levels using`-directives, the situation becomes even worse and the results of name lookup might depend on the order of inclusion.

To avoid all such problems, just follow SF.7 and don’t write using namespace at global scope in a header file.

Using-declaration in namespace and block scope

While the using-directive brings all the symbols of a namespace into the current scope, a using-declaration will bring only one item!

cpp using std::string; string foo{"bar"};

In the above example, we just demonstrated how it works. After using std::string, we can refer to std::string without mentioning the std namespace.

It's still something not to overuse! A using-declaration may also expand an overload set. It's less dangerous to use it at a file scope than having a using-directive at the same scope, but risks still remain.

Starting from C++20, you can also introduce scoped enumerators into a namespace of block scope!

`cpp
enum class Color { red, green, blue };

class MyClass { using Color::red; Color c = red; // This is OK from C++20 };

`

In fact, it would also work with the old-style unscoped enum, but why would we do that?

Importing base class members with using-declaration
