This shift in understanding is important because the first version can easily lead to bugs in your app. For example, instead of rendering out the button immediately, let’s say that we need to defer the rendering for some reason.

Simple enough, we’ll add a setTimeout and a boolean to render the button.

const RefEffectComp = ()=>{ const buttonRef = React.useRef(); const [count, setCount] = React.useState(0); React.useEffect(() => { function buttonAdder() { setCount(v => v + 1); } console.log('UseEffect has run'); // This will throw an error during the first render otherwise if (!buttonRef.current) return; buttonRef.current.addEventListener('click', buttonAdder); return () => { buttonRef.current.removeEventListener('click', buttonAdder); } }, [buttonRef.current]) const [shouldRender, setShouldRender] = React.useState(false); React.useEffect(() => { const timer = setTimeout(() => { setShouldRender(true); }, 1000); return () => { clearTimeout(timer); setShouldRender(false); } }, []); return <div> <p>{count}</p> {shouldRender && <button ref={buttonRef}>Click me</button>} </div>
}

Run the related code sample in a sandbox environment

Now, if we wait a second for the button to render and click it, our counter doesn’t go up!

This is because once our ref is set after the initial render, it doesn’t trigger a re-render and our useEffect never runs.

A better way to write this would be to utilize a “callback ref”, and then use a useState to force a re-render when it’s set.

const RefEffectComp = ()=>{ const [buttonEl, setButtonEl] = React.useState(); const [count, setCount] = React.useState(0); React.useEffect(() => { function buttonAdder() { setCount(v => v + 1); } if (!buttonEl) return; buttonEl.addEventListener('click', buttonAdder); return () => { buttonEl.removeEventListener('click', buttonAdder); } }, [buttonEl]) const [shouldRender, setShouldRender] = React.useState(false); React.useEffect(() => { const timer = setTimeout(() => { setShouldRender(true); }, 1000); return () => { clearTimeout(timer); setShouldRender(false); } }, []); return <div> <p>{count}</p> {shouldRender && <button ref={buttonElRef => setButtonEl(buttonElRef)}>Click me</button>} </div>
}

This will force the re-render when ref is set after the initial render and, in turn, cause the useEffect to trigger as expected.

To be fair, this “rule” is more of a soft rule than anything. There are absolutely instances - such as setTimeout timers - where utilizing a ref inside of a useEffect make sense. Just make sure you have a proper mental model about refs and useEffect and you’ll be fine.

Want to refine your understanding of refs even further? See my article outlining the important details of refs for more.

Don’t expect an empty dependency array to only run once

While previous versions of React allowed you to utilize an empty array to guarantee that a useEffect would only run once, React 18 changed this behavior. As a result, now useEffect may run any number of times when an empty dependency array passes, in particular when a concurrent feature is utilized.

Concurrent features are new to React 18 and allow React to pause, halt, and remount a component whenever React sees it appropriate.

As a result, this may break various aspects of your code.

You can read more about how an empty dependency array can break in your app from our article about React 18’s changes to mounting.

Conclusion

React’s useEffect is an instrumental part of modern React applications. Now that you know more about its internals and the rules around it, you can build stronger and more dynamic programs!

If you want to continue learning skills that will help make your React apps better, I suggest taking a look at our guide to React Unidirectionality, which outlines a good way to keep your application flow more organized.

item.title: 无线温度监测系统的优势 | The Advantages of a Wireless Temperature Monitoring System
item.description:
无线温度监控系统与行业中已使用多年的传统有线系统相比具有显着优势。 虽然无线系统的优点对大多数人来说都是显而易见的，但在评估您的企业或组织的最佳选择时，您可能没有考虑过以下一些事情。

简易安装

使用无线设备的最大优势之一是它们易于安装。 与需要您在墙壁或设施周围铺设电缆的有线传感器不同，无线温度传感器通过无线电波传输数据。 这使得安装比运行物理电线更容易且更具成本效益，同时还提高了安全性和可靠性。 在某些情况下，您甚至可以使用胶带或磁性支架来加快安装速度。 大多数情况下，只需插入无线温度监测系统，它们就可以开始监测您的环境。 这是因为它们具有内置所需的所有功能。 此外，许多无线设备配备的电池可在两次更换之间持续使用数年，因此您不必担心在年度服务电话之间会耗尽电量。

更好的可访问性

无线温度监测设备越来越多地适用于各行各业的企业和组织。 现在有用于谷仓、仓库、工业存储单元、实验室等的无线温度监控器。 如果您希望保护重要的医疗和食品产品或减少设备磨损，可能是时候为您的企业或组织研究无线监控系统了。

根据您的环境，其中许多系统允许您选择所需的设备数量。 这可以分布在多个设施中，范围从一到数千不等。 较新的温度监控系统最好的部分是它们使用无线和物联网技术，因此您可以访问任何设备上的所有传感器。 知道您可以随时随地检查您的环境可以帮助您确信您的敏感产品或设备是安全的。

所有这些辅助功能都可以帮助您确保一切顺利进行。 能够从任何地方访问您的数据是一个非常重要的功能，只有无线温度监控设备才允许，这就是为什么这种类型的系统对数据重要的组织更有价值的主要原因。

更多自定义选项

许多无线温度监测系统允许您随时随地更改和更新您的温度传感器。 这意味着随着环境不同部分的温度升高或降低，您只需在计算机或移动设备上单击几下即可快速进行调整。 如今，许多系统还可以轻松地在某些区域甚至不同位置组织和分组特定的温度监控器，因此您可以确保根据区域的重要性对其进行监控。

除了无线温度监测，还有无线传感器可以检查和记录湿度和压力数据。 在寻找无线温度监测系统时，请寻找那些具有定制选项的系统。 有了这些，您可以根据您的需求和要求进行调整，即使这些需求在未来的某个时候发生变化。 例如，虽然基本系统可以只为您提供有关环境温度的数据，但有些高级系统可以为您提供更多详细信息，例如每天的高/低/平均温度以及时间和日期他们发生了。 然后，这些高级系统将存储它们的所有读数，以便您随时访问它们。

安心的安全功能

近年来，世界变得越来越危险，企业加强了安全措施以保护其资产。 许多企业现在使用具有高级安全措施的无线温度监控系统，为他们的数据增加一层额外的保护，并确保它们不被侵犯。

其中一项功能是在您选择的设备上即时通知警报，因此您不再需要担心错过环境中的任何重要内容。 您将能够设置温度警报，当出现故障或任何读数超出正常范围时会向您发出警告。 在您的环境中检测到温度变化的那一刻，您将立即收到警报，以便您采取行动。 这可以帮助您在问题变成更大的紧急情况之前解决问题。 能够实时检测高温和低温使您可以快速轻松地登录系统以在需要时进行调整。 设置这些保护措施意味着减少成本高昂的库存故障，并在整个公司内提高审计流程的效率——并且由于支出减少，每年年底的收入可能会更多。

其他重要的安全功能包括云存储、故障转移灾难恢复、自动日志记录等。 在无线温度监控系统中寻找所有这些都很重要，这样您就可以放心地知道您的数据是安全的，不会被盗或其他问题。

结论

下次您需要出于商业或专业目的进行温度监控时，请考虑投资无线温度监控系统。 只要您进行研究并确保您选择的系统具有我们在本文中提到的一些功能，它对企业有如此多的优势肯定是一项不错的投资。

许多行业，如医院、餐馆和其他企业，都依靠良好的温度监测来保证敏感产品的安全。 您选择的系统必须能够处理不同类型的监控并具有安全功能以确保您的数据受到保护，这一点至关重要。 这就是无线监控系统的用武之地，因为它们可以让每个人更快、更轻松地进行审计和其他业务流程。 投资无线技术可以让您同时监测大型建筑物和多个房间的温度。 这些系统的优点之一是它们比以往任何时候都更实惠，即使是小型组织和企业也可以使用最先进的设备。

原文:

A wireless temperature monitoring system offers significant advantages over traditional wired systems that have been used in the industry for years. While the merits of wireless systems are obvious to most people, here are some things you may not have thought about when evaluating the best choice for your business or organization.

Easy Installation

One of the biggest advantages of using wireless devices is that they are easy to install. Unlike wired sensors, which require you to run cables through walls or around your facility, wireless temperature sensors transmit data via radio waves. This makes installation easier and more cost-effective than running physical wires, while also improving security and reliability. In some cases, you may even be able to use adhesive strips or magnetic mounts for even faster installation. Most of the time, wireless temperature monitoring systems just have to be plugged in and they’re ready to begin monitoring your environment. This is because they have all the features needed built-in. Additionally, many wireless devices come with batteries that last several years between changes—so you won’t have to worry about running out of juice in-between annual service calls.

Better Accessibility

Wireless temperature monitoring devices are becoming increasingly accessible for both businesses and organizations in all sectors. There are now wireless temperature monitors for barns, warehouses, industrial storage units, labs, and more. If you're looking to protect important medical and food products or reduce wear and tear on equipment, it may be time to look into a wireless monitoring system for your business or organization.

Depending on your environment, many of these systems allow you to choose how many devices you want. This can range anywhere from one to thousands spread across several facilities. The best part of newer temperature monitoring systems is that they use over-the-air and IoT technology so you can have access to all your sensors on any device. Knowing that you can check in on your environment whenever and wherever you are can help you feel confident that your sensitive products or equipment is safe.

All these accessibility features can help you to make sure that everything is running smoothly. Being able to access your data from anywhere is a very important feature that only wireless temperature monitoring devices allow for, which is the main reason why this type of system can more valuable to an organization where data matters.

More Customization Options

Many wireless temperature monitoring systems allow you to change and update your temperature sensors from anywhere and at any time. This means that as temperatures increase or decrease in different parts of your environment, you can quickly make adjustments with just a few clicks on your computer or mobile device. Many systems nowadays also make it simple to organize and group-specific temperature monitors in certain areas or even different locations, so you can make sure areas are monitored based on their importance.

In addition to wireless temperature monitoring, there are also wireless sensors that can check and record data for humidity and pressure. When looking for wireless temperature monitoring systems, look for those that have customization options. With these, you can adjust them based on your needs and requirements, even if those needs change at some point in the future. For instance, while a basic system could just provide you with data about the temperature in your environment, there are advanced systems that will provide you with more details such as high/low/average temperatures of each day as well as times and dates of when they occurred. These advanced systems will then store all of their readings so you can access them at any time.

Security Features for Peace of Mind

The world has become increasingly dangerous in recent years, and businesses have stepped up their security measures to protect their assets. Many businesses now use wireless temperature monitoring systems with advanced security measures to add an extra layer of protection to their data and ensure they’re not being violated.

One such feature is instant notification alerting on your chosen devices, so you no longer have to worry about missing anything important in your environment. You'll be able to set up temperature alarms that will warn you if something is malfunctioning or if any readings fall outside of normal range. The minute there is a change in temperature detected in your environment, you will be alerted immediately so you can take action. This can help you fix the problem before it turns into a bigger emergency. Being able to detect high and low temperatures in real-time allows you to quickly and easily log into your system to make adjustments when they’re needed. Having these protections set up means fewer costly breakdowns of inventory and more efficient audit processes throughout your company—and possibly even more revenue at the end of each year because of reduced expenditures.

Other important security features can include cloud storage, fail-over disaster recovery, automatic logging, and more. All of these are important to look for in a wireless temperature monitoring system so that you can have peace of mind knowing that your data is safe and secure from theft or other issues.

Conclusion

The next time you need temperature monitoring for business or professional purposes, consider investing in a wireless temperature monitoring system. With so many advantages for businesses it is sure to be a good investment as long as you do your research and make sure that the system you choose has some of the features we mentioned in this article.

Many industries such as hospitals, restaurants, and other businesses, rely on good temperature monitoring to keep sensitive products safe. It is vital that the system you choose can handle different types of monitoring and has safety features to make sure your data is protected. That’s where wireless monitoring systems come in since they can make audits and other business processes faster and easier for everyone. Investing in wireless technology allows you to monitor temperatures across large buildings and multiple rooms at once. One of the best things about these systems is that they are more affordable than ever, giving even small organizations and businesses access to state-of-the-art equipment.
